observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management designate a municipal official or personnel who will be in-charge in overseeing the proper utilization of the motor vehicles for stronger internal control.  We also recommended that the designated official ensure that the required information are duly indicated in the driver’s trip ticket before its issuance and to oblige the driver of each government vehicle to summarize all the trip tickets issued during the month in the Monthly Report of Official Travels in chronological order to support the reasonableness and propriety of gasoline expenses.

We recommended that Management instruct the Market Supervisor, in coordination with Municipal Treasurer, to:

Enforce the collection of delinquent accounts totaling ₱60,579.38 by sending demand letters to the concerned stallholders;

Initiate the termination of lease contract of delinquent stall holders for failure to pay the rental fee for three consecutive months as stipulated in the Contract of Lease; and

Regularly monitor and update the records on payments of the stallholders including the Contract of Lease.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Instruct the Inventory Committee and the Accounting Unit to perform the preliminary activities of one-time cleansing and reconcile their records thereof;
b. Plan/strategize on how would the Municipality apply the one-time cleansing and incorporate it in the PIP to be submitted at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled inventory-taking activities; and
c. Immediately start the application of One-time cleansing of the PPEs.
Reiterated under Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 39 to 42 of Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Inventory taking conducted by the Inventory Committee was incomplete thus, reconciliation with accounting records was not made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile their records and reports and adopt the procedural guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties with acquisition cost of P266,650.00 and to complete the unknown details presented in their report to prevent further deterioration of the asset and decongest the storage area, in accordance with existing rules and regulations and in compliance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Undertake inventory of all PPE and reconcile with the records of the Inventory Committee. Appraisal and Disposal Committee to prepare documents for the disposal of unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was incomplete thus, reconciliation with accounting records was not made.

observations.recommendation: Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to, in cooperation with all offices concerned, prioritize the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPEs, including reconciliation of accounting records versus the physical count pursuant to the provisions and guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and to properly accomplish the prescribed format of Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) provided in Annex 49 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was incomplete thus, reconciliation with accounting records was not made.

15. Management submits the (a) Waste Material Report on the demolished municipal plaza, and (b) the contract and all its integral parts in relation to the project: Improvement/ Rehabilitation of the Municipal Plaza.

16. Management notifies the audit team of any plan of demolition and submit the necessary documents in support of the request.

19. Management submits justification on the deficiencies noted above, duly supported with appropriate documents for validation.

20. Management submits the Approved Budget for the Contract and Detailed Prices per Item to support the total amount indicated in the Purchase Order.

24. Municipal Engineer ensures that the building would be fully completed and utilizable for storage space of the Municipality’s documents, and the pathway to the storage area safe for everyone to pass through.

25. BAC Chairman and the Secretariat furnishes copies of the perfected contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof to the audit team for the various projects enumerated in this audit observation, in compliance to COA Circular 2009-001.

30. Management initiates the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section 4.2 of NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

31. The local chief executive requires the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.

34. Management sends written notice to the contractor, requiring them to immediately finish/complete the project, otherwise, cause the termination of the contract pursuant to Annex “I” of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, confiscate the contractor’s performance bond and withhold the 10% retention moneys and use these funds for the completion of the project.

35. Management reviews the operation of the swine breeding center and formulate procedures including the use and/or disposal of the municipality’s share of the offspring, as guided by Title VI of RA 7160.

36. Require the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Auditor the memorandum of agreement entered into by the Department of Agriculture, the municipality and Mr. Silverio T. Miano, for verification.

37. Require the former Officer-in-Charge of the Office of the Municipal Agriculture, Mr. Aguirico T. Bernales to submit valid written justification on the failure of his Office to monitor and supervise the proper implementation of the Dispersal Program.

38. We further recommended that Management require the refund from the persons determined to be accountable and liable for the amount of losses incurred in the improper disposal of government-owned live stocks pursuant to Section 377 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 105 of Presidential Decree 1445, without prejudice to the filing of administrative and/or criminal case, if warranted.

We recommended and the Management for the: 5. Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort on the collection of Loans Receivables– Others, Other Receivables and Due from NGAs; and 6. Municipal Accountant to avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant and unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more and where settlement could no longer be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that a large number of borrowers cannot be located and their status are unknown. Nevertheless, they committed to act on the request for write-off for accounts with available documents. The Municipal Accountant further explained that they have difficulties in securing supporting documents for the request for write-off, including the securing of death certificates and certifications confirming that the accountable officers are either no longer residing in the barangay or have passed away.
observations.reason: Receivable accounts totaling P1,488,355.21 are still outstanding as at December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: 1.7	We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all possible means to locate the records of the dormant receivables in order to substantiate the balance and effect collection. In case all efforts fail, Management may require the Municipal Accountant to request the write-off of the dormant accounts, subject to the requirements and procedures for the proper disposition and derecognition of dormant accounts.

2.8 	We recommended that Management require the GSO to submit the RIS and SSMI relative to the ₱51,042,048.52 inventory, which was already consumed/distributed to the end-users, to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in preparing an adjusting journal entry for fair presentation of the inventory account in the financial statements.

5.7	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant:
To gather the original documents and validate the accounts payable that have been outstanding for more than two years. If found invalid or no original supporting documents can be found, the accountant may revert said payables to the unappropriated surplus; and
To ensure that all supporting documents are complete before preparing the JEV to record the transactions and attach the original supporting documents to the JEV for submission to the Audit Team.

6.5	We recommended that Management instruct the:
Municipal Treasurer to transfer to the General Fund the unexpended balances of trust funds representing agency’s counterpart for completed and indefinitely abandoned projects; and
Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts the said transfer of funds.

8.5 	We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to regularly furnish the Municipal Assessor with a report on the building permits and occupancy permits issued to be used as bases for assessment and updating of the Assessment Rolls;
Direct the Municipal Assessor to:
• conduct a tax-mapping to verify whether all real properties within the Municipality are properly listed in the assessment rolls, specifically those newly constructed or renovated; and
• to exert due diligence on the newly issued building permits so that newly constructed properties are included timely in the assessment roll for efficient collection of taxes.

9.8 	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Cause the collection of the deficient business taxes totaling ₱565,761.74, inclusive of interest and surcharges; and
Examine the accuracy of the declared gross receipts of taxpayers by considering all available evidence to ensure that tax assessment is accurate and fair.

10.7	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to adopt geo-tagging applications for all civil works and infrastructure projects to ensure that geographic coordinates and locations are properly recorded, leading to more transparent, accurate and reliable reports of project accomplishments.  It will also help prevent project duplication and overlapping of projects.  Geo-tagged photos should be attached to the disbursement vouchers as part of the supporting documents.

12.6	We recommended that Management see to it that all resources of the government be managed, expended or utilized efficiently and effectively in order to prevent loss or wastage. We, likewise, recommend that Management conduct a thorough feasibility study prior to the commencement of a project in order to realize the full potential of the project and maximize the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

14.9	We recommended that the MTO, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor’s Office, reconcile the discrepancies in assessed values and fully implement the Pangasinan Real Property Taxation System to standardize procedures, improve operational efficiency, and ensure accurate, real-time monitoring and reporting of real property tax transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the members of the Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive
physical count and inspection of all PPE items to fully verify their existence, and
accurately incorporate the results into the RPCPPE, ensuring that only those
PPE duly accounted for during the inventory count are included in the report;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office and the MGSO to address any
deficiencies that may arise out of the conduct of complete inventory-taking of
all PPE items owned by the Municipality, and to update and maintain the
required Property Cards/ Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment in their
custody, for easy reference during the conduct of inventory-taking; and
c. Adopt and fully implement the one-time cleansing procedures of PPE accounts
incorporated into the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to
properly address the missing/unaccounted/undocumented PPE items that have
been in the books for several years, for a valid reporting and presentation of
PPE accounts balances that are verifiable, accurate and in existence, thus, attain
fairness of presentation of all PPE accounts in the Financial Statements of the
Municipality at every reporting period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary adjustments on the reported balances of RPT and SEF Tax Receivables
dropping the recorded penalties of P17,156,241.52 from each book of accounts as such
penalties shall be recognize only upon receipt of tax payments by the taxpayers,
thereby reflecting only the accurate receivable balances of real property taxes in the
books of the General and Special Education Funds, in accordance with the provisions
of NGAS Manual for LGUs on the proper recognition of penalties from tax
delinquencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to
correct the Net Book Value of PPE and the Government Equity accounts by
P1,698,115.56 and commence the recognition of depreciation of completed
projects on the date that such PPE becomes available for use, and not on the
date of final payment; and
b. Municipal Engineer to immediately submit to the Office of the Municipal
Accountant the copies of Certificate of Project Completion of all projects
completed so that immediate recognition of appropriate asset and expense
accounts can be taken up in the book of accounts for consistency of the
application of accounting rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Finance Committee to
cause the immediate reversion of savings from completed projects into an
unappropriated surplus so as to finance other developmental programs, projects and
activities that are truly beneficial to its constituents, thus attain the purpose of 20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the MDRRMC:
a. cause the appropriate and thorough evaluation of every project funded
under the LDRRMF and ensure its readiness for immediate implementation
and completion so as to contribute greatly to the prevention/mitigation of
any disaster-related events that have adverse effects on the lives of its
constituents, pursuant to Section 12(c) of RA No. 10121;
b. require the MDRRMO to include in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be charged
against the unexpended appropriation of previous years and continuing
appropriations as a guide for the MDRRMC in the prioritization of LDRRMF;
c. prioritize the inclusion of projects/programs/activities that are
implementation-ready, without any restrictions or prohibitions prior to its
inclusion in the LDRRMF budget so that funds allocated thereof would be
utilized to the fullest within the timelines set by the MDRRMC thus contribute
to achieving the objectives of setting-up LDRRMF; and
d. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to effect the necessary
journal entries in the accounting books and Registry of Appropriation,
Allotment and Obligations, respectively, for the reversion of LDRRMF lodged
in the trust fund books, which remained unutilized for more than five years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the accountable officers to liquidate cash advances within the
period specified in COA Circular No. 2012-001 supported with the complete
and proper documentation.
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from
processing disbursement vouchers for a new cash advance of an employee
until their previous cash advance has been settled; and
c. designate additional disbursing officers, if needed, to prevent granting of
various cash advances to one disbursing officer only.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the justifications for the need of the
Change Order of the aforementioned projects and perform detailed site
investigations prior to commencement of on-site work to minimize the
occurrence of variation orders; and
b. Comply with the guidelines provided in Annex E of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No.
9184 in the preparation and submission of variation orders to show that the
same are justified, necessary and in order.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Local School Board submit the
Quarterly and Annual SEF Utilization Reports for CY 2024, and in succeeding years, to
the DepEd Central Office, through appropriate channels, and furnish copies thereof to
the Sangguniang Bayan and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, pursuant to Item
6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017, to ensure transparency and accountability
in reporting the allocation and utilization of the Special Education Fund to the intended
users of information.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Disposal Committee and
Municipal General Services Officer to prioritize the disposal of unserviceable
properties, in accordance with the COA – DBM JC No. 2024-1 to avoid further
deterioration leading to lesser resale value, and thus eliminate the continuing storage
cost for the government.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to implement a system to regularly monitor and ensure the timely submission of billing statements and supporting documents from contractors. This may include setting clear deadlines for submissions and establishing a follow-up process for any delayed documents.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to facilitate the transferring of the CIP accounts to the related Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) accounts for the completed projects, and record the related depreciation expense.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the semi-expendable properties from the asset account to the appropriate expense account by preparing the following adjusting journal entry:
The Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the correct journal entries for transactions in the following year.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Heath Officer provide a copy of the Promissory
Notes to the Municipal
Accountant to be used as a basis
on the recording of the Notes
Receivable.
b. The Municipal Health Officer
instruct the midwives to properly
accomplish the Promissory Notes.
c. The Sangguniang Bayan revisit
the Municipal Ordinance No.
2009-04 and issue guidelines/policies/legislation
addressing the non-collection of
birthing fees as discussed above.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following proposed journal entry below to reclassify the accounts to their proper account titles to ensure a fair presentation of the financial statements:
Donations						2,263,003.00
Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses	2,263,003.00
To record reclassification of expenses

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the discrepancies noted above and strengthen its review mechanism by reconciling subsidiary ledgers vis-à-vis the Alphalist, ensuring that these records are complete and accurate.

Recommendations:
1.10. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Engineering Office submit to the General Services Office the
Certificates of Project Completion and other supporting documents of the
completed infrastructure and other projects for recording;
b. Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal adjusting entries to close the
CIP accounts to its appropriate Property Plant and Equipment accounts and
provide the corresponding depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation,
and submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of the JEV taking up the
reclassification of asset accounts and the provision of the depreciation expense
and the corresponding accumulated depreciation.

Recommendations:
2.6. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
a. make the necessary adjusting entry to Supplies Inventory Account and Supplies
Expense account to fairly present the financial statement, and;
b. establish a proper inventory tracking system, including the maintenance of Stock
Cards, Inventory Ledgers, and Requisition and Issuance Slips (RIS) to properly
monitor supplies.

Recommendations:
3.9. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant :
a. make the necessary adjustments to record the correct balance of PPEs,
accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense and; and
b. ensure proper recording of Infrastructure Projects using the Construction in
Progress account, and only recognize the appropriate PPE account upon its
completion in order to present an accurate financial reporting.

Recommendations:
4.5. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant :
a. classify the deposits as Deferred Credits in the absence of a lease contract
between the LGU and Smart Communications, Inc., and;
b. take up all reconciling items in the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation
Statement to ensure accurate and correct Cash in Bank balance reflected in the
books as at year-end.

Recommendations:
5.6. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
a. prepare an adjusting entry to record the correct amount of PPEs, accumulated
depreciation and depreciation expense using the straight-line method, in
compliance with IPSAS 17;
b. implement a systematic tracking mechanism for all PPE to ensure timely
provision of depreciation and its accurate valuation; and
c. conduct periodic reconciliation between the Municipal Accounting and General
Services Offices to ensure consistency in asset records and depreciation
schedules.

Recommendations:
6.9. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Inventory and Appraisal Committee together with the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Assessor identify all land items owned or controlled by the
Municipality and provide proper valuation thereof;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor reconcile accounting and
assessor’s records, and adjust all discrepancies if there are any; and
c. Municipal Accountant take the necessary steps to record unrecorded/accurate
land assets in the financial statements, ensuring that all properties owned by the
Municipality are properly accounted for in compliance with government
accounting standards and regulations.

Recommendation:
7.10. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant request
for write-off of dormant Receivables which remained in the books, and where
settlement could no longer be ascertained as prescribed in COA Circular 2023-008.

Recommendations:
8.10. We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee conduct a
physical count of inventories semi-annually, submit the RPCI to the Accounting
Office, and reconcile it with the reported account balances in the LGU’s books of
accounts. Consequently, the RPCI should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor
within the reglementary period.
8.11. We also recommended and Management agreed that the GSO ensure that RSI and
RSMI submitted to the MACCO is supported with the required documents to validate
the issuance of inventories during year.

Recommendation:
9.6. We recommended and Management agreed to review the current processes and
implement appropriate measures to ensure proper segregation of duties particularly
in the preparation/processing of payrolls of personnel. Preparation of payroll must
be done by the MHRMO while the processing is handled by the MACCO including
the loan remittances. This will help strengthen internal controls, promote
transparency, and safeguard the LGU’s financial resources.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the prescribed accounting entries in recording the utilization of fund transfer from NGAs in the trust fund for fair presentation of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to monitor the compliance on the posting of the required size and design including the complete information of sign boards during the duration of the project to promote transparency in government projects.  Likewise, we recommended that Management advise the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to require and monitor the attachment of pictures of the actual signboard posted on site to the DVs as part of the supporting documents in the payment of infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC and TWG to verify and validate the SLCCs submitted and ascertain that it is duly supported with Certificate of Final Acceptance and/or Final Rating of at least satisfactory in the CPES including the other eligibility requirements submitted by the bidders to ensure that government contracts are awarded to qualified bidders.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023, Pages 68 to 69 | Management was able to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:

Ensure that all transactions involving procurement of goods undertaken thru shopping or small value procurement be supported with RFQs indicating therein the ABC and other terms and conditions of the contract; 

Require the suppliers to indicate their PhilGEPS Registration Number in the RFQs and to accomplish properly the RFQs before submission to the BAC; and

Prepare an Abstract of Price Quotations based on the submitted price quotations to determine the lowest quotation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023, Pages 71 to 73 | Management was able to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from deducting loans and other payments against the RATA considering that the purpose of the said allowances is to defray representation and transportation expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE limit the hiring of experts to its actual need of consultancy services, taking into consideration the specific work requiring highly specialized or technical expertise in a field of special knowledge or training that cannot be done by the regular staff of the Municipality.

We also recommended that the LCE require the consultants to submit a detailed Accomplishment Report for further assessment by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023, Pages 74 to 78 | Management stopped the hiring of consultants and the contracts of the existing consultants were already cancelled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local LDRRM Council headed by the Municipal Mayor to:
a. submit the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of unutilized balance of LDRRMF amounting to P10,426,467.37; and
b. evaluate and review the LDRRMF Investment Plan with emphasis on the proper identification and selection of specific priority PPAs to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the revised LDRRMF Plan.
observations.reason: Plan was not submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the MGSO to prepare and submit the consolidated PIF listing of all the insurable properties and other assets to GSIS for their evaluation and approval, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor, in compliance to Section 5 of RA No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-002;
b. the MGSO verify and obtain the deed of donations of the insured ambulances mentioned and submit to the Municipal Accountant for the recognition in the book of accounts. Henceforth, the MGSO ensure that the properties to be insured are legally owned by the Municipality; and
c. the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the appropriations the fund for the application of insurance of all the insurable properties subject to the Municipality’s financial capacity.
observations.managementAction: Prepared and submitted the listings of insurable properties and other assets to GSIS for their evaluation and approval, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor. Obtained Deed of Donations for the ambulances. Included appropriation for insurance in the CY 2025 budget.
observations.reason: In CY 2025 budget, an appropriation amounting P1,260,000.00 was appropriated for the application of insurance of the insurable properties of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: Policy guidelines for hiring of JO personnel were derived under Administrative Order No. 01-2024. Set Duties and Responsibilities for JOs hired. Set standardized wage rate for JOs. The HRMO has requested the installation of biometrics. The management is still evaluating/assessing considering the various places of assignment of JOs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to provide justification/ explanation on the findings noted and the corresponding actions taken necessary to correct such deficiency and submit to COA TAD thru the Audit Team for their evaluation. Henceforth, strictly comply with Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the documents for re-evaluation of the TAS. The TAS considered the justifications and documents submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to maximize the utilization of GAD Funds and see to it that GAD related PAPs will fully correspond, addressed and adhere to the basic purpose and objectives for which the funds were provided.

observations.recommendation:
2.9 	We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Accounting Office to establish the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year which amount shall not be limited to the target collections for the year and follow the accrual method in accounting said taxes;
Instruct the Accounting Office to record the delinquent RPT and SET amounting to ₱294,408.14 and ₱236,913.09, respectively; and
Instruct the Treasury Office to facilitate the updating of the database in order to generate a timely and relevant report for recording in the books.

3.5 	We recommended that Management instruct the personnel in-charge in generating monthly billing statement to submit a copy of monthly billings to the Accounting Office as basis in recording income from the BAWASA operations.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the remaining 14 parcels of land without title; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to reconcile their records on Land account in order to determine the correct composition of the unaccounted amount of ₱395,539.21.
Direct the Municipal Assessor to update his records on Land account and to include the other four parcels of land that were recorded in the books.
7.4  	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to treat the operations of BAWASA as a segment and adopt IPSAS 18 for its financial reporting.

8.5  	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the prescribed accounting entries in recording the utilization of fund transfer from NGAs in the trust fund for fair presentation of accounts.

9.10 	We recommended that Management, through the leadership of the LCE, monitor the full implementation of the development programs/projects as programmed so that benefits that could be derived therefrom would be realized optimally and in a timely manner.

10.9 	We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Treasurer:
Require the business establishments paying business taxes to submit a copy of audited financial statements and/or ITR pursuant to the provisions of the pursuant to the provisions of the Municipal Revenue Code of the Municipality in order to serve as basis in the computation of business tax.
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to cause the collection of the deficient business taxes totaling ₱657,303.99 from the suppliers concerned; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to be judicious in assessing the business taxes by considering all data available to come up with the correct assessment.

11.8 	We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to issue official receipt to acknowledge the receipt of checks received from one fund to another and ensure that checks be issued in the name of the LGU for transfer of funds between and among the accounts of the LGU.

14.9  	We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that performance securities posted by winning bidders conform to the requirements laid out in the existing rules and regulations before the awarding and signing of the contract. In case of surety bond, it must be accompanied by a certification duly issued by the Insurance Commission in prescribed form and containing all the required information.

16.4  	We recommended that Management create an Inspectorate Team or the PMC who shall undertake the preliminary inspection and monitoring of all infrastructure projects of the LGU and the preparation of punch-list to document the observation/s noted during the inspection.

17.7  	We recommended that Management require the BAC and TWG to verify and validate the SLCCs submitted and ascertain that it is duly supported with Certificate of Final Acceptance and/or Final Rating of at least satisfactory in the CPES including the other eligibility requirements submitted by the bidders to ensure that government contracts are awarded to qualified bidders.

18.6  	We recommended that Management designate a municipal official or personnel who will be in-charge in overseeing the proper utilization of the motor vehicles for stronger internal control.  We also recommended that the designated official ensure that the required information are duly indicated in the driver’s trip ticket before its issuance and to oblige the driver of each government vehicle to summarize all the trip tickets issued during the month in the Monthly Report of Official Travels in chronological order to support the reasonableness and propriety of gasoline expenses.

19.5  	We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:
Ensure that all transactions involving procurement of goods undertaken thru shopping or small value procurement be supported with RFQs indicating therein the ABC and other terms and conditions of the contract;
Require the suppliers to indicate their PhilGEPS Registration Number in the RFQs and to accomplish properly the RFQs before submission to the BAC; and
Prepare an Abstract of Price Quotations based on the submitted price quotations to determine the lowest quotation.

20.5  	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from deducting loans and other payments against the RATA considering that the purpose of the said allowances is to defray representation and transportation expenses.

21.11 	We recommended that the LCE limit the hiring of experts to its actual need of consultancy services, taking into consideration the specific work requiring highly specialized or technical expertise in a field of special knowledge or training that cannot be done by the regular staff of the Municipality.
21.12 	We also recommended that the LCE require the consultants to submit a detailed Accomplishment Report for further assessment by the Audit Team.

22.6  	We recommended that Management instruct the Market Supervisor, in coordination with Municipal Treasurer, to:
Enforce the collection of delinquent accounts totaling ₱60,579.38 by sending demand letters to the concerned stallholders;
Initiate the termination of lease contract of delinquent stall holders for failure to pay the rental fee for three consecutive months as stipulated in the Contract of Lease; and
Regularly monitor and update the records on payments of the stallholders including the Contract of Lease.

2.9 	We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Accounting Office to establish the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year which amount shall not be limited to the target collections for the year and follow the accrual method in accounting said taxes;
Instruct the Accounting Office to record the delinquent RPT and SET amounting to ₱294,408.14 and ₱236,913.09, respectively; and
Instruct the Treasury Office to facilitate the updating of the database in order to generate a timely and relevant report for recording in the books.

3.5 	We recommended that Management instruct the personnel in-charge in generating monthly billing statement to submit a copy of monthly billings to the Accounting Office as basis in recording income from the BAWASA operations.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the remaining 14 parcels of land without title; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to reconcile their records on Land account in order to determine the correct composition of the unaccounted amount of ₱395,539.21.
Direct the Municipal Assessor to update his records on Land account and to include the other four parcels of land that were recorded in the books.
7.4 	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to treat the operations of BAWASA as a segment and adopt IPSAS 18 for its financial reporting.

8.5 	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the prescribed accounting entries in recording the utilization of fund transfer from NGAs in the trust fund for fair presentation of accounts.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:

a. Instruct the Inventory Committee and the Accounting Unit to perform the
preliminary activities of the one-time cleansing and reconcile their records
thereof;

b. Plan/strategize on how would the Municipality apply the one-time cleansing and
incorporate it in the PIP to be submitted at least 10 calendar days before the
scheduled inventory-taking activities; and

c. Immediately start the application of One-time cleansing of the PPEs.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council headed by the Municipal Mayor to:

a. submit the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of unutilized balance of
LDRRMF amounting to P10,426,467.37; and

b. evaluate and review the LDRRMF Investment Plan with emphasis on the proper
identification and selection of specific priority PPAs to accelerate their
implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding
disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. the MGSO to prepare and submit the consolidated PIF listing of all the insurable
properties and other assets to GSIS for their evaluation and approval, copy
furnish the Office of the Auditor, in compliance to Section 5 of RA No. 656 and
COA Circular No. 2018-002.

b. the MGSO verify and obtain the deed of donations of the ambulances mentioned
and submit to the Municipal Accountant for the recognition in the book of
accounts. Henceforth, the MGSO ensure that the properties to be insured are
legally owned by the Municipality; and

c. the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the appropriations the fund for the
application of insurance of all the insurable properties subject to the
Municipality’s financial capacity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Sangguniang
Bayan, to require the HRMO to:

a. Come up with concrete policy guidelines relative to the hiring and rehiring
of JO personnel to establish a clear expectation and protect the rights of
both the employees and the Municipality;

b. Provide Statement of Duties and Responsibilities of JO personnel to clearly
identify their responsibilities and perform their duties efficiently;

c. Set a standardized wage rate based on their qualifications and suitability for
the job applied, subject to Municipality’s financial capacity; and

d. Request the installation of biometrics system for JO personnel to properly
monitor their DTRs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to provide
justification/explanation on the findings noted and the corresponding actions taken
necessary to correct such deficiency and submit to COA TAD thru the Audit Team for
their evaluation. Henceforth, strictly comply with Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA
9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and
Development Officer (MSWDO) to maximize the utilization of GAD Funds and see to it
that GAD related PAPs will fully correspond, addressed and adhere to the basic
purpose and objectives for which the funds were provided.

We recommended that the Property and Accounting Units conduct the preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking as set out in Section 6.1 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
We also recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory activities and attach property stickers on the counted PPEs.

We recommended that Management implement the Perpetual Inventory Method in the recording of inventory in accordance with Section 114 of the Manual of the New Government Accounting System Manual.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer or the Property Officer prepare and maintain the required reports and documents of the inventory accounts.

We recommended that:
On the uncorrected RPT and SET receivable at the end of the year:
The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the last five (5) years; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary accounting entries to set up the correct Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable based on the report to be submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.
On the non-recording of RPT and SET discount, determine the amount collected from discounts on current year receivables and advance payments for succeeding years and prepare the following adjusting entry:
Dr. Discount on Advance Payments (for advance payments) xxx
Discount on RPT-basic (for current year receivables) 	 xxx
Discount on SET (for current year receivables) 	 xxx
Deferred RPT/SET 				 xxx
Cr. 	RPT/SET Receivable 				 xxx
Due to LGUs					 xxx
RPT/SET Income					 xxx
#To record discounts on advance payments

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following proposed journal entry below to reclassify the Accounts Payable account to the proper Liability accounts to ensure a fair presentation of the financial statements:
Accounts Payable				1,792,291.27
Due to Officers and Employees		1,254,891.27
Other Payables				   537,400.00

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following adjusting journal entry in the General Fund books:
Subsidy to Other Funds			₱1,600,000.00
Transfers of Unspent Current Year
DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds		₱1,600,000.00

We recommended that Management submit the (a) Waste Material Report on the demolished municipal plaza, and (b) the contract and all its integral parts in relation to the project: Improvement/Rehabilitation of the Municipal Plaza.
Henceforth, we recommended that Management notify the audit team of any plan of demolition and submit the necessary documents in support of the request.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that cash advances for Confidential Funds are limited to the requirements for three (3) months and are drawn at the appropriate quarter in compliance with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
We recommended and Management agreed to increase the amount of fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer to sufficiently cover his cash accountabilities.

We recommended that Management submit justification on the deficiencies noted above, duly supported with appropriate documents for validation.
We also recommended the submission of the Approved Budget for the Contract and Detailed Prices per Item to support the total amount indicated in the Purchase Order.

We recommended and management agreed to submit the Approved Budget for the Contract of the above-mentioned infrastructure projects with the format following DPWH Department Order No. 197, Series of 2016.

We recommended and Management agreed that, for ongoing projects, the Municipal Engineer require the contractors to install and update the relevant information on the project billboards based on the status of the project.
We also recommended and Management agreed that they will place appropriate controls to ensure that project signboards are installed at the project site like requiring the attachment of photos of the installed project signboards upon payment of mobilization costs, and every progress billing of a certain project.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure that the building would be fully completed and utilizable for storage space of the Municipality’s documents, and the pathway to the storage area safe for everyone to pass through.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat immediately furnish copies of the perfected contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof to the audit team for the various projects enumerated in this audit observation, in compliance to COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Submit the Schedule of Dormant Accounts as of December 31, 2023 and on the next quarters on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month.
Submit a request for the authority to write-off the dormant accounts following COA Circular No. 2023-008
We recommended and Management agreed to stop the granting of honorarium to the members of the LFC without legal basis for the grant thereof.
We recommend that Management submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form, including the documents to support the action taken by the management, to the audit team for validation.

We recommended and Management agreed for the :
Property Officer to update the records based on the results of the physical inventory and reconcile with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the agency's PPE; and
MACCO and GSO (i) to coordinate with each other and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership, and condition of the properties, and (ii) to prepare and maintain appropriate property records and reports for the PPE account.
We recommended and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to determine and reclassify receivables that belong to non-current assets.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the unreconciled difference of P52,055.75 and to ensure that a periodic reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts must be conducted to minimize accounting error and/or deficiency.

We recommended and the management agreed for the:
Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort on the collection of Loans Receivables–Others, Other Receivables and Due from NGAs; and
Municipal Accountant to avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more and where settlement could no longer be ascertained.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the :
Inventory and Disposal Committee and the General Services Officer to immediately cause the disposal of unserviceable properties to prevent their further deterioration and for management to potentially obtain a higher return through sale or negotiation; and
Accountant and the General Services Officer to consider the one-time cleansing of its PPE accounts by observing the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020, to update the status of the Municipality’s PPE and to fairly present the account in the financial statements.

We recommended and management agreed to secure at all times Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract with the winning bidder/ contractors on behalf of the Municipality before contract perfection for projects under the lump-sum appropriation and fund transfers from various government agencies.

We recommended, and the Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Health Officer to promptly establish comprehensive guidelines and implementing rules and procedures for the storage, reporting, and monitoring of donated drugs, medicines, and medical supplies to ensure transparency, accountability, and optimal utilization of donated resources, thereby enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of healthcare services provided to the public.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the :
MDRRM Officer to diligently adhere to the guidelines set forth in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, on the charging of expenses against the LDRRMF; and
Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that only expenses directly associated with disaster risk management activities and in line with the programs, projects, and activities under the LDRRMF are charged against the fund.

We recommended, and the Management agreed for the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the monitoring of the approval of its Solid Waste Management Plan to ensure a systematic and comprehensive solid waste management program consistent with the national framework and in accordance with the provisions of RA 9003 and of the policies set by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to initiate corrective actions by reviewing the current board membership and appointing individuals who can provide critical perspectives and expertise in order to enhance the inclusivity and effectiveness of the MSWMB, leading to better-informed decision-making processes and more comprehensive Solid Waste Management plans and programs that address the unique concerns of the community.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the parcels of land owned and controlled by the Municipality in the amount of ₱5,785,950.00.

We also recommended that Management create an appraisal committee which will determine the proper valuation of the land of the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Accountant to record the appraised value of real properties in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Management submit justification on the deficiencies noted above.

On the incorrect set-up of RPT and SET receivable at the beginning of the year:
The OIC-Municipal Assessor prepare an updated data on real properties within the municipality, and furnish the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation/updating of the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card.
The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to set-up the actual Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable.

On the non-recording of RPT and SET discount 
Trace transactions on the unrecognized discounts on RPT/SET and make necessary adjustments on the affected accounts.
In the succeeding transactions, record the current year’s discounts as a deduction for advance and prompt payment of Real Property Tax.

Trace the unaccounted difference of RPT and SET receivable at year-end and make necessary adjustments on the affected accounts.

We recommended that Management make representation to the PHO-Bohol for the preparation of Memorandum of Agreement to determine the responsibilities and accountabilities in the distribution/recording of COVID-19 vaccines. Otherwise, donated vaccines are to be recorded in the books of the Municipality.

We further recommended that in the succeeding donations received from another agency, the documents are filled-out and acknowledged properly.

We recommended that the Management submit justification on the very low utilization rate of the 20% Development Fund.

Further, we recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the projects funded by the 20% Development Fund, and ensure that identified projects are implemented within the year in order to deliver the intended benefits and to get the maximum value of the fund considering the increasing prices of materials and labor costs.

We recommended that Management submit written justification on the deficiencies noted above.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Office review the supporting documents of the Obligation Request to determine whether these are proper charges against the LDRRM fund.

We further recommended that trip tickets and all other supporting documents be reviewed by the Municipal Accountant for completeness and propriety to avoid irregular and illegal transactions.
We recommended that Management/persons responsible submit written justification/comment on the deficiencies noted above. Otherwise, unnecessary and excessive expenditure shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the concerned workers be required to submit their individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors, in compliance with Section 4(6) of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Item 4.1.3 of COA Circular 2012-001.

We further recommended that in the succeeding transactions, Management concerned require the submission of accomplishment reports to support the payment of wages of these workers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Finance Committee expedite the updating and amendment of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code in pursuance to Section 191 of R.A. 7160 to provide additional revenue sources to fund the municipality’s developmental projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor the collections and direct all collecting officers to turned over all collections intact daily, pursuant to Section 69, P.D. No. 1445 and Section 29, NGAS for LGU, Volume I.

We recommended that Management ensure that the list of all ongoing government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and the corresponding monthly/quarterly monitoring reports are submitted within the prescribed period in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, as amended by COA Circular No. 2015-006 dated August 20, 2015.

We further recommended that the monitoring report on government projects for the period ending December 31, 2022 be submitted to the Audit Team immediately.

We recommended that Management submit justification for the non-creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.

Further, we recommended that the Management initiate the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section 4.2 of NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommended that Management require all officials and employees concerned to submit immediately the liquidation reports of their outstanding cash advances and/or refund the unexpended cash balance, if any, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them and strictly enforce that no additional cash advance shall be granted until full liquidation of the previous cash advances is made.

We also recommended that Management require the former municipal officials to make follow-up or send tracers for the issuance of Credit Advice from the COA Central Office and monitor the liquidation of the outstanding balance of the Confidential/Intelligence Fund.

We recommended that Management submit written explanation/justifications on the causes of delay in the projects’ completion. We further recommended that henceforth, Management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the current status of the debtors-NGOs/POs and assess the collectability of the outstanding receivables. Furthermore, we recommended that demand letters be sent to each debtors/NGOs/POs requiring immediate liquidation of the funds transferred to evaluate whether the purposes therefor were successfully attained.

We recommended that Management direct all accounting office personnel to assist the current municipal accountant in the identification of the receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.

We recommended that Management submit written sufficient justification in pursuing the conduct of a fund-raising activity despite the absence of a resolution from the Local Sanggunian authorizing the collection of fees, otherwise personnel involved thereto will be made to answer accordingly.  

We also recommended that Management refrain from conducting similar activities and charging fees without the approval and authority from the Local Sanggunian pursuant to Section 132 of the Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommended that Management require the LMP, VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the Section 264 of Republic Act No. 8424 or the National Internal Revenue Code of the Philippines.

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer not to accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.

We recommended that Management make written representation to the BIR in order to (a) ascertain whether the activities are taxable or not, (b) apply for an Authority to Print (ATP) for receipts or invoices and pay the Annual Registration Fee, if necessary, (c) determine the taxes due, if applicable.

Require Mr. Gaudioso T. Libres, the designated Caretaker of the Swine Breeding Center of the municipality, to submit valid written justification on disposing three (3) government-owned pigs in violation to Section 356 of RA 7160 and Title V of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management.

Require the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Auditor the memorandum of agreement entered into by the Department of Agriculture, the municipality and Mr. Silverio T. Miano, for verification.

Require the former Officer-in-Charge of the Office of the Municipal Agriculture, Mr. Aguirico T. Bernales to submit valid written justification on the failure of his Office to monitor and supervise the proper implementation of the Dispersal Program.

Conduct re-evaluation on the reasonableness of the weight on each pig as declared, taking into consideration their ages/number of months as reflected in the Animal Release Orders and require immediate remittance of the proceeds to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for proper accounting and recording of government funds.

We further recommended that Management require the refund from the persons determined to be accountable and liable for the amount of losses incurred in the improper disposal of government-owned live stocks pursuant to Section 377 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 105 of Presidential Decree 1445, without prejudice to the filing of administrative and/or criminal case, if warranted.

We recommended that Management refrain from incurring expenditures for the Organic Demonstration Farm until an ordinance regulating the overall operations of the economic enterprise shall have been crafted and passed by the Local Sangguniang Bayan.

Municipal officials and employees invited to attend seminars and workshop with a private training institution should ensure that the event is properly authorized by the Department of Interior & Local Government (DILG).

To secure from Bernales Leadership & Research Institute an accreditation of the convention or seminar design or training module by the Local Government Academy.
