observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management adopt the minimum requirements of the laws and pertinent accounting and reporting guidelines, particularly applicable to in-kind donations received by the Municipality from various donors with the following courses of actions, to wit:
                                                                                                                               1.Direct the concerned GSO to adopt the minimum requirements of law in order to establish a clearly written set of policies stating the well-defined duties and responsibilities for a specific level of authority to be assigned in the recording, distribution, inventory management, storage and reporting of donated/procured items.   

2.Require the designated Property/Supply Officer to:
a.Submit Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances complete with complete supporting documents.
b.Submit Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM only for the remaining balances each kind of relief goods using the prescribed format.                                 

3. Provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements stating information on the donations received, distributed to end-users and other relevant information as may be required under the prevailing guidelines for accounting and reporting purposes.

We recommended that Management: 

1.Require the Bids and Awards Committee and other responsible officials to ensure that purchase order and other pertinent documents should be properly accomplished with the necessary information stating the date of acceptance or receipt by the supplier of its commitment to deliver the goods and services. Likewise, require the supplier to cause refund for delays incurred in the delivery of the procured items, where applicable. 

2.Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the veracity and completeness of similar transactions which must be signed and approved only when the required data are complete in order to compel the supplier of its obligation to deliver the goods and services as per written agreement.

We recommended that Management direct the officials concerned to submit the required relief distribution sheets that should be properly accomplished with adequate information sating the specific item description and volume of goods received, complete address and signature of the actual recipient in order to determine whether goods were actually received by the designated beneficiaries.  

Moreover, it is recommended that Management observe adherence to the prescribed standards and procedures issued by the COA in order to establish accountability of the responsible officials/employees should there be any lapses in the distribution of goods in any relief operation of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Unit, Inventory Committee, and Property Custodian to take actions on the following:

a)Perform periodic reconciliation to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts;

b)Prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balances;

c)Maintain complete and accurate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger   Card   (PPELC)   for   each   category   of   plant,   property   and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards (PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; and,

d)Create an Inventory as well as Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.

e)Execute an executive order to commence a one-time cleansing of the Municipality’s Property, plant and equipment account and to established the itemized listing of properties that should be recorded therein; and,

f) Derecognize unserviceable properties in the PPE accounts after complying to the disposal procedures provided under Section 267 of the Local Government Accounting Manual and Sections 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Memorandum Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile discrepancies noted, and provide additional information to be presented on the notes to financial statements which must be concise but briefly discussed using a narrative and descriptive formats, taking into consideration that the effects of financial transactions of significant amount. 

To ensure that the same is clearly understandable to its interested users, disclosure of such relevant information must be duly supported with corroborating schedules that must be consistent with the reported balances shown in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Government Equity.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as Chairperson of the Municipal Development Council:

1.Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to revisit the screening and prioritization criteria in the selection and approval of proposed projects for funding under 20% Development Fund in full compliance with the requirements embodied in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure fair and equitable allocation of funds only for eligible development projects; and,

2.Provide legal bases on charging to the 20% Development Fund the cost of equipment rental for the utilization of heavy equipment owned by the Municipality in the implementation of road rehabilitation projects and the like.
observations.managementAction: The management provided justification on the cost of equipment rental charged to the fund in lieu of providing the legal bases. Likewise, the personnel concerned committed that the practice of charging inappropriate expenses to the fund should be discontinued.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure that MDRRMFIP should be prepared with comprehensive details and relevant information, integrating the components of the funds, namely, the Current and Continuing Appropriation in the General Fund Budget, Special Trust Funds and from other sources in compliance with the requirements as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.



We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to cause the preparation of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of MDRRM Fund duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant. Likewise, submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA audit team assigned to review the financial transactions charged to LDRRMF of the Municipality through its MDRRM Council and Municipal Development Council, a copy of which shall be furnished to the Office of the Civil Defense.
observations.managementAction:
The management lacked awareness as to the proper presentation of the required information based on  existing guidelines.



The management 
submitted on a regular basis the required report on MDRRMF utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council to include projects to be funded by 20% Development Fund in full compliance with requirements as prescribed in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 that is geared towards the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental goals that would uplift the quality of life of its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Although the management have generally complied with the issuance by DILG and DBM on proper utilization of the 20% Development Fund, the management still failed to comply with a portion of the legal provisions due to inclusion of ineligible projects in the approved budget

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management shall require the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to ensure that the necessary information stating the date of acceptance or receipt by the supplier of its commitment to deliver the goods and services are indicated in the purchase orders and other pertinent documents.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that the same must be signed and approved only when the required data are complete to ensure that the supplier will deliver the goods in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract.
observations.managementAction: The management still incurred the same deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to direct that LDRRMFIP should be properly accomplished with adequate and relevant information integrating the components of the funds, consisting of those held in the Current and Continuing Appropriation  in the General Fund, Special Trust Funds and from other sources.
observations.managementAction: The management was able to formulate plan as basis for monitoring the utilization of LDRRMF and the implementation of the related projects. However, the preparation was not in accordance with the form prescribed by the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this audit report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall submit the lacking and other necessary documents relative to the procurement activities and progress payments for audit purposes and final inspection and evaluation of the project to avoid suspension in audit of the project
observations.managementAction: The management already submitted the documents except for some transactions
observations.reason: The management were preoccupied with other equally important tasks, thus the submission of documents required in audit was not facilitated.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to monitor the collection and transporting of wastes by its waste collectors

Ensure compliance of wearing of prescribed outfit during collection of wastes. Direct all the waste collectors to undergo training to be conducted by the MHO for proper handling and disposal of wastes and adequate personal hygiene
observations.managementAction: The management did not fully comply with the minimum requirements of RA 9003 up to this date.
observations.reason: Not Implemented | The management had meager budgetary allocation to fund trainings as well as other activities associated with the solid waste management operation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

b.) Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to (i) maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger   Card   (PPELC)   for   each   category   of   plant,   property   and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards (PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same (ii) reconcile their records for PPE accounts and determine the causes of the variance thereof and cause proper disposition; (ii) identify all PPE items without ARE and the employees accountable for the items to pave the way for the issuance of the ARE to all employees/officials concerned.

c.) Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office already installed a system to generate the PPELC as of this date. On the other hand, the GSO had maintained in partial the use of Property Card for the PPE.






Management already created the Appraisal and Disposal Committee.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office asserts that recording of PPEs on the PPELC requires ample time. However, they committed to record all their PPEs  on PPELC in the succeeding year and to facilitate reconciliation between accounting and property records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to determine and reclassify the actual amounts that are long overdue or beyond 1 year as non-current assets and to take the following actions.

1.Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances/
receivables and strictly comply with provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting and liquidation of cash advances, otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.

2.Avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more and where settlement could no longer be ascertained
observations.managementAction:
Management still failed to act on this recommendation as of this date.





Management already implemented the “No additional granting of cash advance without  liquidation of previous cash advance granted” policy.



Management did not avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more.
observations.reason:
This was overlooked by management. However, the Accounting Office is considering withholding of salary of employees who have outstanding cash advances as of 12/31/2019


The management still found it more difficult, particularly due to travel restrictions posed by the COVID-19 pandemic in terms of locating most of the accountable officers/debtors and its last known addresses because these loans/cash advances were granted several years ago. Vital documents in availing such remedy are also lacking and needs time and financial resources to obtain from other concerned agencies.

However, they were again reminded to avail of the remedy in the writing off of receivables even starting to those receivables belonging to persons who are dead several years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council to formulate projects to be funded by 20% Development Fund in consonance with the requirements prescribed in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 that is geared towards the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental goals that would uplift the quality of life of its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Although the management stressed its commitment to let the Municipal Development Council strictly follow the minimum requirements for the formulation of projects, programs, and activities in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1 issued by DILG and DBM, there were still ineligible projects with budgetary allocation chargeable to the 20% Development Fund during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management that accounting and reporting guidelines should be strictly observed.
Justify in writing as to why charges made to the Special Trust Funds for unexpended balance should not be disallowed in audit. Unless the premises found to be meritorious, these shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO partially complied with the prescribed format of LDRRMFIP as of this date.  Moreover, the personnel already provided an explanation in writing as to charges sourced from the fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this audit report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for proper recording of unexpended balance of LDRRM funds in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already drawn the journal entry voucher during the year. As far as the unutilized DRRM Fund during the year, the same error is committed agency.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office asserted that they cannot immediately transfer the Cash from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund of the DRRM Fund balance at the end of year because there are still unrecorded expenses and unliquidated cash advances that hinders them to establish the accurate amount to be transferred at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMO should comply with the minimum standards for disaster preparedness volume 2 on items marked as either non-operational or non-existing.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO had complied only to a certain extent with the National Disaster Preparedness and Mitigation Plan 2011-2028 as prescribed even if there are programs, projects and activities by the office that may be aligned with both Provincial and National Disaster Preparedness Plan. However, status of implementation of related projects will be validated by the audit team once Quarantine restrictions were already lifted.
observations.reason: Since LDRRMFIP was not properly accomplished in form and in content, relevant information may not be readily available as ready reference in planning PPAs intended for disaster preparedness.

observations.recommendation:
1.We recommended that a sufficient budget be set aside for the construction and development of sanitary landfill facility as final disposal site for solid wastes. 





2. Pending the relocation to another area for establishment and operation of sanitary landfill, exhaust all legal remedies necessary to adopt specific measures to implement proper waste reduction/ segregation.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE and MENRO was still in the stage of complying the documentary requirements in application for loan to fund the construction of at least one chamber in a standard sanitary landfill.



The management exhausted its means to cope with the requirements for environmental compliance but the prevailing conditions still needs significant improvement.
observations.reason:
The Municipality was still complying with the documentary requirements intended for application of loan with the bank in order to finance the construction and development of sanitary landfill facility.


Implementation of the measures for proper segregation of wastes is barred by stubbornness of the waste generators.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to:                                                                                                        
                                                                                                              1.Coordinate with the SB to pass a resolution revisiting and formulating a doable policies and guidelines on the imposition of fines and penalties, taking into consideration the specific functions and duties and their limitations of the proposed collectors to be identified as enforcers.

2.Provide the adequate supply of resource whether it is in financial terms and adequate technical manpower equipped with skills and able to deliver the required services in support of Community Education and Public Awareness campaign, collections of fees and other fines and penalties and other duties and functions deemed necessary in the operation.
observations.managementAction: The management is still considering the need to review their solid waste management plan.
observations.reason: As assessed by the management, they claimed that the Solid Waste Management Plan needs to be thoroughly reviewed and updated to make it more proactive and applicable. Only after thorough review can policies, processes, and procedures meet the intended outcomes.

observations.managementAction: The deficiencies noted on the 2017-2018 Financial Statements were already rectified but there are still deficiencies prevailing in the 2019 Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

a. Implement Section 296(a) of the RRC on dealing with the delinquent market vendors for the timely collection of market rentals and to prevent the eventual increase of uncollected surcharges and interests.

b. To execute the provisions of Section 301 in awarding the declared vacant market stall through the adjudication process. The same process is expected to be also applied when there is transfer of rights over the market stall from previous occupant to the new one.





c. Revisit the steps/procedures on the monitoring of the market operations especially on the collection of monthly rentals and how to ensure that the surcharges and interests already incurred by the vendors will be collected. Determine the deficiencies noted and take necessary steps on how to improve the monitoring and collection system.



d. Stop the practice of allowing the original lessees of market lots to sub-lease their constructed buildings to other business operators.
observations.managementAction:
The management admitted they had imposed the penalty for late payments in the last quarter of CY 2018 and some delinquent vendors requested deferment of payments of monthly rentals to be paid on renewal of their business permits on CY 2019. 

There was no transfer of rights transpired  for the market stalls for CY 2018.

They tried to enforce some procedures in attempting to increase on collection of market rentals but still fell short of their expectation.

The management had initially discussed the provisions of existing policies and guidelines in the monitoring of their market operation that need improvement.
Furthermore,
the management is implementing its policy that the delinquent  account is paid before giving of clearance as a requirement in the renewal of the business permits.


For effective monitoring of collection, the management requested to re-organize and assign personnel from the office of the treasury, to focus on the collection of lot and stall rentals, since the Economic     Enterprise Office is under the Treasurer’s Office’s jurisdiction.
observations.reason:
Management consented to vendors’ personal consideration
and due to lack of personnel.

In the Calendar Year 2017, the management had not yet implemented the Section 296 of the RRC on dealing with delinquent market vendors due to no proper turnover on how the collection process should be made since the newly designated personnel started to report in the Public Market. Likewise it had not imposed the said Revenue. However, they find ways to fix and execute or implement its policy on the imposition surcharges and interest as much as they could in the last CY 2018 up to present.

The same rules are being applied for the new applicant of vacant market stall lessee. The Economic.Enterprise Manager informed that the deficiency had been corrected and implemented to a certain degree the guidelines for the right processing to own the stall vacant. So far, in the CY 2018 to present, the management undertake the same measures such that the new lessee would pay for the occupancy fee, signing of contract, getting of business permits, signing of waiver to the previous lessee to waive his right and give the favor to the new lessee to avoid conflict in a coming year.
The owner of the buildings and the sub-lease had their internal arrangement to transaction as to the building without informing /formal notice to the Econ. Enterprise Manager. The Economic Enterprise. Manager only knew when the buildings are run and operated by another person.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to remit on time the shares of the Provincial Government on the additional 1% tax on real property accruing to the Special Education Fund so that the Provincial Government can timely utilize the collections to finance various projects, programs, and activities beneficial to the constituents of the Province of Compostela Valley. 

Observe religiously the deadline set for the remittance of provincial shares which falls on every 15th day of the following month as mandated.
observations.managementAction: The management had made their remittance  before deadline started only on July 2019

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government to prepare and enter into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the recipient beneficiaries of the agricultural, fisheries and seaweed projects under the Bottom-Up Budgeting Program. 

Make sure that the duties and responsibilities of binding parties are clearly defined and established in order to at least ensure the smooth implementation, effectiveness, and sustainability of the projects. 

Stop the practice of not entering into a MOA with the intended beneficiaries when implementing priority programs and projects.

Follow up the delivery of the remaining 16 cattle to ensure the success of the cattle production project. Impose appropriate liquidated damages for the delayed deliveries.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to prepare Memorandum of Agreements.
observations.reason:
MOA preparation is impractical to prepare at this time due to the following reasons in the implementation of the projects:

1.Corn Production Enhancement-This was not sustained due to long drought that hit at the start of the project.
2.Seaweed Stilt Drier-Not productive due to ice-ice disease.
3.Bangus Production in Marine Fish cage- Not productive because the area is silted that affects its operation.
4. Cattle Production/Re-Stocking- this was not realized due to failure of bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government, the immediate usage of donated lot for the operation and management of Eco-Park with Alternative Technology Approach for its final waste disposal and for the purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes to sustain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan.

Prioritization of the funding for these properties should be immediately done so that the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste would not be hampered.

We recommended also the stoppage of the operation of the present disposal site and the strict implementation of the waste segregation policy of the Municipality from the source, in order to successfully attain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, management was not able to utilize another area of their lot for the operation of new controlled dumpsite. The purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes was not also realized due to lack of funds.
observations.reason: Not Implemented | Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
Avoid the practice of delaying remittances of the various trust liabilities since these are budgeted and included in the annual appropriations of the municipality. 
  
Instruct the personnel-in-charge of the trust liability accounts to conduct monthly reconciliation of same to determine the correct amounts that should be remitted within the prescribed period provided in the applicable rules and regulations so that the concerned government agencies could utilize the funds due them for their programmed activities and incurrence of penalties, interest and surcharges by the municipality relative thereto could be avoided. 

If there are penalties and surcharges incurred for late remittances, the same should not be charged against the funds of the municipality instead it should be treated as personal liability of the personnel responsible for the delay.
observations.managementAction:
As of this date, the management still incurred delays in their remittance to BIR and GSIS. 


Even after remittance, balances  representing unremitted  deductions is still appearing in the subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation:
Exhaust all measures to collect receivables from the grantees of credit and financing program even those receivables from NGAS and other receivables.
    
Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue regular statement of accounts and prompt verification of accounts in the books of the creditors.        Make entries to Prior Year’s Adjustments on the amount pertaining to those accounts which recovery is already impossible due to death or disability fully supported with necessary supporting documents.  

Reclassify and adjust accounts in consideration of unrecorded repayments to reflect the correct balances duly confirmed with the concerned debtors.  

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of receivables using the billing statements from the Accounting Department in order to recover government funds and utilize collection to finance the present thrusts and priorities of the Municipality.

Exhaust all measures to gather the documents pertaining to the receivables and collect from debtors.

There should a meeting between the LGU concerned and the loan recipients , to sit down and confer together in order to discuss matters of collection and payments to finally settle the accounts and the possibility of continuing this financing scheme in a more effective way for the benefit of the LGU and its constituents.

The Municipal Accountant provided copy of Statement of Accounts as basis of the Municipal Treasurer in obtaining the collections due to the Municipal government.
observations.reason: The concerned accountable officers still refuse to settle their obligations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to exercise due diligence in the performance of her duties and functions and be vigilant in the checking and evaluation of all the documents of the procurement transactions of the Municipality before certifying as to the completeness of the supporting documents in all the disbursement vouchers. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the closing of bank accounts with the private rural bank and avail the services of the Land Bank of the Philippines in order to protect the interest of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to follow up the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan which shall serve as the foundation, guide and timetable in the identification, implementation and operation of solid waste management programs. 

We also recommended that Management instruct the Waste Officer Designate to strictly observe the criteria provided in Sections 41 and 42 of RA 9003 in the establishment and operation of Sanitary Landfill to ensure the protection of public health and its environment.

We further recommended that Management require the Waste Officer Designate to immediately utilize the Plastic Melter Densifier and the Multipurpose Shredder to avoid deterioration of the same and attain the benefits intended to it constituents.
