observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to include the PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget that will fully address gender related issues and to monitor the implementation of the programs to attain the main purpose in promoting gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfillment of women’s rights and the promotion of women’s economic empowerment.
observations.managementAction: GAD programs and projects were not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the HRMO to cause the issuance of contract of service to the JO workers and to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 4(6) and 4(7) of PD No. 1445 and the CSC Resolution.
observations.managementAction: Management is in process of preparing contracts of service to be issued to its JO workers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the:
Municipal Engineering Office to submit to the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as the basis for the recognition in the books of accounts.
General Services Officer to prepare the Local Road Inventory and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components; and secure a copy of the Road Map from the MPDO.
Inventory Committee to comply with the prescribed template on the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as illustrated in Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008.
Municipal Accountant to prepare the ledger card of PPE accounts including road networks and henceforth, observe the proper accounting and reporting of road networks and the appropriate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the total road network system as per COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
submits justification on its failure to ensure ownership by securing TCT for lot purchases.
requires the General Service Officer to exhaust all possible legal remedies to hasten the processing of the title of the said lot in the name of the Municipality to assure its protection from possible third party claims in the future;
instructs the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosure of information on the recorded lot acquired pending the release of transfer of title to the land in favor of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare Development Office to ensure that well-defined policies and guidelines be established in writing on the grant of donation/financial assistance containing but not limited to the following:
Types of financial assistance (medical, education, burial, etc.) together with the rate of authorized rate for a specific case, including the frequency of grants to an individual
Eligibility requirements to comply with the grant of donation and the related documentary requirements for a specific type of financial assistance.
Duties and responsibilities of personnel to be assigned in conducting a thorough evaluation or a case study before the grant of financial aid/donation.
require the MSWDO to conduct an in-depth evaluation of each case and prepare a corresponding case study report to be attached as supporting documents to the transaction before the release of funds for donation/aid to individuals.
Instruct the MSWDO to maintain a data bank of all the information to be obtained from target individuals in their locality as ready reference in allocating their funds for future claims.
Provide the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the promulgated policies and procedures in the granting of financial assistance to affected individuals.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Heads of the respective Implementing Offices/units of various Programs/Projects/Activities to submit the list of on-going government PPAs and those to be implemented during the year for inclusion in the Quarterly reports.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all concerned officials to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, failing which shall be dealt with in consonance with Section 4 of the Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
BAC and the BAC Secretariat to submit justifications on its failure to comply with the minimum requirements in the preparation and submission of the APP and other procurement reports prescribed by the GPPB, including that of the APP for Common-Use Supplies and Equipment as required by the DBM.
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that all the requirements of the procurement law and other related issuances in the preparation and submission of the APP are satisfactorily complied with.
BAC to be abreast and updated with procurement law, rules and regulations through self-study, training and seminars.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall make sure that all procurements activities of the Municipality comply with the procurement law, otherwise known as RA 9184 and all applicable GPPB issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the Members of the Bids and Awards Committee to follow the procurement procedures as embodied in the IRR of RA 9184.
discourage the practice of procurement through reimbursement.
submit justification on the adoption of the procurement process through reimbursement.
direct the Municipal Accountant to compute for the applicable taxes on payments made to suppliers for the purchase of goods and services as above enumerated and remit the amount to the BIR, in compliance with prevailing BIR Revenue Regulations.
require the members of the BAC to update themselves on the Procurement Law and abide by it.
enjoin all the Municipal officials and employees concerned to abide by the requirements of the procurement law to avoid disallowance in the future.

We recommended that the management:
Submit justification for the non-withholding of the 10% Creditable Withholding Tax on the allowances and/or honorarium paid to judges, clerks of courts, and public attorneys.
Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to deduct the applicable withholding tax on allowances, honorarium, and similar payments subject to withholding tax as required under Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98, as amended by RR No. 11-2018 and RR No. 14-2018.

We recommended that the Management direct the MDRRMO and other concerned offices to review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan incorporating the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years to ensure that the Municipality becomes a disaster-resilient community.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to assist the Municipal Engineering Office in adopting doable strategies and coordinated mechanisms to facilitate timely implementation of the approved PPAs as included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP).
require the Municipal Engineering Office to submit justification for the delayed/non-execution of the above stated programmed projects
require the Municipal Engineering Office in coordination with the Municipal Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to expedite the preparation of POW and other basic requirements based on realistic estimates, taking into consideration that only feasible PPAs , free from any issues of ownership and road right of way are included in the Annual Investment Plan;
d)  	provide   general   supervision   and   control over all PPAs of the municipal government by ensuring that all those related programmed expenditures are properly planned, executed/implemented and completed within the timeframe set to boost its economic condition and efficiency in local governance which benefits will redound to its constituents.
e) direct all the executive Municipal officials and employees   concerned, including but not limited to the Municipal Planning Development Office and Municipal Engineer’s office to faithfully discharge their duties and functions as provided by law and cause disciplinary or administrative sanctions to those erring officials and employees, if proven guilty of any unjustified failure to implement those approved projects on a timely basis.

We recommended that the management ensures full utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Funds for the attainment of its objectives which is to build a disaster-resilient community and to reduce disaster risks.

Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee
to:
a. in cooperation with all offices concerned, prioritize the implementation of the
one-time cleansing of PPEs, including reconciliation of accounting records
versus physical count pursuant to the provisions and guidelines set forth in
COA Circular No. 2020-006.
b. to properly accomplish the prescribed format of RPCPPE provided in Annex 49
of NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
c. create a Disposal and Appraisal Committee which shall inspect unserviceable
equipment and property, set the final appraised value of all disposable
properties, recommend the disposal to the Local Chief Executive for his
approval and the manner of disposal, and forward the approved document to
the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustments in the PPE.

Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management (MDRRM) Officer to ensure timely implementation
of identified mitigation projects, abide by the required annual preparation and
updating of the LDRRMFIP with defined projects or activities, and strictly comply
with the requirement before the release and use of the unexpended previous year’s
DRRM balance accruing to the special trust fund of the Municipality.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that all Request for Quotations, the
Procurement and Supply Management Committee for Purchase Orders and the
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) Inventory Committee for Inspection and
Acceptance Reports be properly accomplished and complete in detail. We also
recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the noted lacking
documentation.

Henceforth, we recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and the Sangguniang Bayan to submit a reasonable
explanation/justification and basis for the PEI rate given to its employees otherwise,
such transaction shall be subject to refund. Also, strictly adhere to DBM provisions
and guidelines in the granting of PEI to avoid disallowance in audit.

Considering the above deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
require the BAC to perform the following:
a. ensure that there is an Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for all procurement
activities to be undertaken;
b. ensure compliance with the documentary requirements and the prescribed form
and contents of Bidding Documents under Revised IRR of RA 9184 and
Philippine Bidding Documents;
c. posting of the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS, the
website of the Procuring Entity, if any, and any conspicuous places in the
premises of the Procuring Entity within 3 calendar days after its issuance to
ensure the transparency in the procurement process; and
d. ensure compliance with the preparation, posting and submission of PMR and
APCPI Results.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and Tourism Officer to ensure intact and timely remittance of
collections from dive passes;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit shares on the dive pass
collection to Municipalities of Tingloy and Bauan on a weekly and monthly basis,
respectively, in compliance with the MOA dated July 5, 2002, and January 15, 2015; and
c. Include in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Procurement Plan the 2020 and
2021 projects relative to the conservation rehabilitation, protection, and management
of the aquatic coastal resources, and ensure their implementation for optimal fund
utilization, may they be funded by the Special Account for Conservation or local
income in the General Fund of the Municipality;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare
Officer to:
a. consider proposing revision of the guidelines in the implementation of Aid to
Individuals in Crisis Situation to include assistance relative to COVID-19
pandemic along with its pertinent condition and requirements and direct strict
adherence thereto; and
b. conduct thorough assessment (social case study report) of the situation and
qualifications of the individuals seeking for financial assistance before making any
recommendation in accordance with the guidelines.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to effect the refund of the LDRRMF from the General Fund
for the amount disbursed for AICS amounting to P342,500.00 and avoid utilization
of the LDRRMF other than its purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairmen of Committees of
Procurement and Supply Management and of PPE Inventory to furnish COA copy
of the Purchase Orders not later than five (5) working days from issuance together
with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation and
to provide notification of deliveries supported with copies of accomplished
Inspection and Acceptance Reports.

We recommend that management submit the lacking supporting documents and written justification on the deficiencies noted above.

Further, strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of public funds, specifically on the grant of COVID-19 AHDP and COA Circular 2012-001, among other pertinent provisions.

We recommend that the management submit the lacking supporting documents noted above.

Further, strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on documentary requirements in the disbursement of public funds, pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommend that the management submits written explanation on the payment of claims for the delivery of equipment that does not conform to the specification called for, and the reference to brand names as indicated in the Purchase Request.

We further recommend that management strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of public funds specifically on Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Revised IRR of RA 9184, among other pertinent provisions. Otherwise, irregular/illegal expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the procurement of medical supplies with prices exceeding the price ceiling provided by the DOH. Otherwise, require the supplier to refund the excess amount computed based on the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0413 and 2021-0223, and DOH Department Circular No. 2021-0291.

We further recommend that management observe the DPRI and other related issuances of the DOH in the succeeding procurement of medicines and medical supplies pursuant to Republic Act No. 7581 and DOH Administrative Order No. 2014-0001.

We recommend the management to strictly implement the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of its inventory of PPE thru the creation of an Inventory Committee who shall:
Prepare, in coordination with Property Supply Office, and Accounting Office, and submit the PIP for approval to the Head of the Agency 	to ensure that the conduct of inventory is properly and adequately planned;
Submit a Copy of the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities; and
Complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensures that only the current year’s unexpended Quick Response Fund (QRF) and LDRRMF-MOOE will be recorded as Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds in order to present the correct Surplus/Net Income of the current year. If the transfer to the Special Trust Fund was not done at year-end, prior year’s unexpended QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE is to be recorded as Prior Period Adjustments in the succeeding year.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund Books at year-end. Further, prepare the required adjusting entries to correct the account balances.

We recommend that management submit justifications on the deficiencies noted, and additional evidence to warrant the suspension of works on days with workable weather conditions, otherwise, liquidated damages will be recalculated.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute and deduct liquidated damages from the retention money pursuant to Item 8 Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

We recommend that management prioritize the implementation of the projects funded by the 20% Development Fund.  Further, ensure that identified projects are implemented within the year in order to deliver the intended benefits and to get the maximum value of the fund considering the increasing prices of materials and labor costs.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the delay of release of Basic RPT shares to the barangay.

Further, we recommend that the Municipality immediately release the barangay shares from RPT collections on a quarterly basis, as provided for under Section 271(d) of RA 7160.
We recommend that management require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to submit the SAAOB on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.

We recommend that management require the LDRRM Officer to promptly submit the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that management assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its AAPSI for the AAR 2020.

Further, we recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR in its ensuing years.

We recommended that Management render submission of a document stating the details of project requirements, subject to the guidelines in the GPPB Circular 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 as prescribed under RA 11469 to prove the necessity and propriety of the transactions that were undertaken on emergency procurements, otherwise the same shall be suspended in audit;

it shall likewise adhere to the prevailing rules and regulations in the preparation of project requirements during emergency situations in order to facilitate smooth implementation of its procurement activities as planned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:                                                                                                                                                                                               (1) instruct the BAC Secretariat, Budget Officer and End-Users or Implementing Units to coordinate and ensure the preparation of APP in accordance with the prescribed format and that additional procurement projects, if any, to be conducted during the year should be incorporated in the revised APP, duly approved by the HoPE or the second-ranking official designated as authorized representative/signatory to act on her behalf; and, 

(2) require the concerned BAC officials to facilitate the submission of the APP in its original and revised form on procurement projects undertaken in the CY 2020

We recommended the following to Management:

1.Require that the BAC officials to explain in writing as to non-submission of the documentary requirements prior to award of contract or before payment is made; and,

2.Instruct the Accounting personnel concerned to review the completeness of documents submitted before processing claims of the supplier as embodied in Section 4.1 of GPPB Circular 01-2020.

We recommended that Management obtain authority from the SB granting the LCE the permission to accept donations whether in cash or in-kind from local or foreign sources.

Likewise, we recommended the submission of resolutions, stating that the acceptance of donations received were authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant explain in writing their lapses in recording the proceeds arising from payment made by the Municipality itself for equipment rental charges to the 20% Development Fund for its own properties. 

Moreover, we recommended that Management adhere strictly to the accounting standards in the proper treatment of similar transaction, taking into consideration the underlying accounting principles of substance over form in the financial report preparation.
observations.managementAction: The management already acted on this.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

1.Require the Head of the Municipal Health Officer case or the authorized representative occupying a regular plantilla position, if any to render a report stating the volume of donated items utilized or the number of issuances to end-user units as basis for the Accounting Office to record the transactions relative to the utilization/distribution of the donated drugs, medicines and medical supplies that shall be treated as expenses for a given reporting date.

2.Unless the personnel-in-charge is able to provide a report on the receipt and utilization of donated drugs, medicines and medical supplies from DOH, he/she will be held liable for whatever pilferages or wastages incurred from non-compliance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations for property and supply management and that the related transactions shall likewise be suspended in audit.

3.Establish accounting requirements/guidelines or implementing rules and regulations that are deemed applicable in the storage, reporting and monitoring of donated drugs, medicines and medical supplies from DOH and/or other government agencies.
observations.managementAction: The management already acted on this.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the LDRRMO to render submission of purchaser’s and dealer’s permit from the NTC as well as the registration from the Secretary of Commerce Industry in order to establish the validity and legality of the transaction
observations.managementAction: The management already provided the required documents as basis for validating the legality of the transaction.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Committee on Appropriation conduct a thorough deliberation of needs that are relevant in its endeavors geared towards the mitigation of disaster impacts or the establishment of disaster-resilient communities. 




Likewise, it was recommended that the personnel concerned explain in writing as to the basis/reason for allowing the said transaction in audit.
observations.managementAction:
The management conducted consultation with the MDRRMC to formulate policies and plans that are relevant in carrying out its commitments geared towards disaster-related preparedness.

Moreover, the personnel already provided an explanation in writing as to charges sourced from the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

1.Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to conduct a comprehensive and periodic assessment of the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management operation against the MDRRMFIP prior to submission to the MDRRMC for approval and cause changes whenever necessary, subject to availability of funds; 

2.Exhaust all means to adopt practices and strategies of other agencies to be considered in the planning and budgeting of the MDRRMF; and,

3.Limit the utilization of the MDRRMF to projects and activities which are deemed relevant in mitigating the risks of disasters.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO coordinated with the MDRRMC to facilitate periodic assessment  of its operations for monitoring and planning purposes.

observations.managementAction: The management had undertaken measures to reprogram sets of priority  projects for development purposes that can be implemented within reasonable timeframe.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:            


b. Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.



c. Require the Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to (i) maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for each category of plant, property and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards (PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same (ii) reconcile their records for PPE accounts and determine the causes of the the variance thereof and cause proper disposition; (ii). identify all PPE items without AREand the employees accountable for the items to pave the way for the issuan ce of the ARE to all employees/officials concerned.

We recommended that management shall:

a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile and effect the necessary adjusting entries to rectify the discrepancies noted.

b) Require the officer-in-charge or caretaker of biological assets to (i) maintain a Biological Assets Property Card (BAPC) to account any additions/reductions in the biological assets; (ii) prepare and submit a Quarterly Report of Biological Asssets which shall aid the proper recording and reporting of biological assets to promote better accountability.
observations.managementAction:
The management already established the Appraisal and Disposal Committee for this purpose.






The management committed to maintain the prescribed form in the recording of the properties; and to facilitate reconciliation between accounting and property records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall direct the Municipal Accounting Office to require the supplier to provide the necessary warranty security on the procured equipment, either in the form of cash or equivalent to at least 1% of the P74,909,000.00
observations.managementAction: The warranty period had already lapsed and the equipment is still found to be operating in good condition until present.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to direct all the accountable officers to settle and liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances and stop the practice of granting cash advance unless previously granted amount was liquidated
observations.managementAction: Management acted on the noted deficiency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Planning and Development and Budget Offices to install feasible mechanisms and appropriate measures in the planning and monitoring of Projects, Programs and Activities to be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to maximize the benefits due to its beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The management had undertaken measures, which included either realignment/reappropriation/augmentationof funds for regular projects or COVID-19-related undertakings  that be implemented within reasonable timeframe.

observations.recommendation:
a. Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to conduct a comprehensive and periodic assessment of the Municipality's disaster risk reduction and management operation against the LDRRMFIP before its submission to the MDRRMC for approval and cause changes whenever necessary, subject to availability of funds and



b. Exhaust all means to integrate and apply best practices of other agencies to be considered in planning and budgeting of the MDRRMF.



c. Limit the utilization of the LDRRMF to projects and activities which are deemed relevant in mitigating the risks of disasters
observations.managementAction:
The management secured the approval of MDRRMC with authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan for the changes in the list of projects to be implemented as chargeable to the Special Trust Funds, and augmented items of MOOE budget in the Current Appropriation as one of the sources of COVID-19 Funds.

The MDRRMO coordinated with the MDRRMC to facilitate periodic assessment  of its operation for monitoring and planning purposes.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the personnel-in-charge of the remittance to secure the NOSA on  a regular basis for the preparation of the required ARA to forestall any delay in its remittances

Ensure that any difference in salary due to step increment or other causes should be made before any remittance is made

Enjoin the personnel-in-charge to make reconciliation with the cumulative balances which is still due for remittance as soon as possible

Also, all monies being withheld due the GSIS should be immediately remitted for the corporate's timely usage of funds for the fulfillment of its plans, programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: The management instructed the personnel-in-charge to facilitate the timely remittance of the GSIS premium contribution

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Planning and Development and Budget offices to ensure effective and efficient implementation of Projects, Programs and Activities to be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to maximize the benefits due to its beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction:
The management had undertaken measures to reprogram sets of priority  projects for development purposes that can be implemented within reasonable timeframe. Moreover, the Project Monitoring Committee was established to monitor the implementation of projects. 
The project monitoring committee was likewise created to facilitate the monitoring of project implementation,

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government, the immediate usage of donated lot for the operation and management of Eco-Park with Alternative Technology Approach for its final waste disposal and for the purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes to sustain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan.

Prioritization of the funding for these properties should be immediately done so that the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste would not be hampered.

We recommended also the stoppage of the operation of the present disposal site and the strict implementation of the waste segregation policy of the Municipality from the source, in order to successfully attain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: The management had undertaken measures to comply with the requirements in the preparation for the establishment and operation of the required sanitary landfill.
The management acted on this to promote public awareness as to the proper implementation of the solid waste management plan of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

a. Exert more effort in observing the timely implementation of the PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The management had undertaken legal remedies in the proper identification of priority projects which can be implemented within the reasonable timeframe.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly follow the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004, particularly on the use of tarpaulin signboards in accordance with the prescribed specifications, and the posting of the required information on such signboards.
observations.managementAction: Tarpaulins were placed on the project site  based on ocular inspection.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management create an inventory committee to conduct physical count of inventories semestrally and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
We also recommended that Management require concerned offices to submit to the GSO-Designate the supporting documents on the issuances of the inventories they received which shall serve as basis in preparing the SSMI and submit the said reports to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the transactions affecting the balance of Continuing Appropriation and to make the necessary adjustments on the discrepancy between the ending balance in CY 2020 and forwarded balance in CY 2021 in the amount of ₱817,902.01; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to revert the unexpended balances of appropriations for MOOE amounting to ₱6,069,883.65 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe compliance with the provision of the MNGAS for LGUs by recording purchases of supplies and materials to the appropriate inventory accounts using the perpetual inventory method to present the accurate balances of the inventory accounts in the FS.

We recommended that Management ensure adherence to the provision of Section 20 of MNGAS for LGUs by requiring  the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to maintain Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card and duly certified list of taxpayers to provide an accurate basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property and special education taxes at the beginning of the year and to facilitate the reconciliation of accounts for fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to request for write-off the dormant receivable accounts consistent with the requirements and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Inventory Committee to complete the details of PPE included in the RPCPPE to facilitate the reconciliation of said report with the accounting records; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledger for each group of PPEs including work and other animals and the Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain equivalent Property Cards for PPE and reconcile the same with accounting records for check and balance.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to verify the Accounts Payable account and submit copies of perfected contracts and documents showing that the goods/services were actually delivered and received but not yet paid to establish the validity of the accounts payable account amounting to ₱521,408.83; otherwise, revert the said amount to the government equity of the General and Special Education Funds.

We recommended that Management ensure that expenditures are incurred pursuant to enacted appropriations and to ensure that these are utilized for public purposes.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 305(a) and 305(b) and Sections 4(1) and 4(2) of RA No. 7160 and PD No. 1445, respectively.

We recommended that Management require the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to stop the practice of granting the requests for monetization of municipal officials and employees who have not accumulated 15 days of vacation leave credits and monetization of sick leave credits except on justifiable reasons.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to correct the prior period errors by restating the comparative amounts for prior periods presented in which the error occurred or by restating the opening balances of assets, liabilities and net assets/equity for the earliest prior period presented for fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local Finance Committee to:
refrain from using the appropriations for PS, MOOE and CO to augment the appropriation of any expense item belonging to another expense class;
avoid the reversion of appropriations unless it pertains to reversion of unexpended balances of appropriations to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year and the reversion of continuing appropriations unless obligations therefor have been fully paid or otherwise settled; and
prepare the Supplemental Budget in accordance with the requirements of Section 321 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Chairman of the BAC to require from suppliers the submission of the mandatory documents in the use of alternative method of Small Value Procurement to ensure that the most advantageous price to the Municipality are obtained from suppliers of known qualifications; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents and see to it that all of the documentary requirements are complied with to ensure transparency and propriety of the procurement transactions.

We recommended that Management ensure full remittance of premiums to the GSIS by requiring the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) to prepare and transmit promptly the required forms in updating the records of the personnel to the GSIS to update the personal records of employees both in the Municipality and GSIS.
We also recommended that Management, through the HRMO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, inform in writing the Head of the GSIS of the discrepancies between the personnel records in the Municipality and GSIS database to come up with corrective measures which will enable the remittance of premiums in full to the GSIS and secure the social security benefits of municipal employees.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to discontinue the practice of issuing checks prior to the approval of the disbursement vouchers and to ensure that all checks are issued only upon approval of DVs in future payments of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Head of the BAC Secretariat, to submit the lacking documents for further review and evaluation of the Audit Team.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to perform and submit results of Test Analysis and conduct inspection for every progress billing by the contractor and submit the inspection report to the Municipal Accountant as supporting document to the DV.

We recommended that Management require the Chairman of the BAC with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, to submit immediately the contract and supporting documents of one project with total contract cost of ₱450,000.00 to the Audit Team and to observe the prescribed period of submission of five working days from the execution of a contract to facilitate the review and evaluation of the contracts.

We recommended that Management continue its tax campaign programs and information drive to ensure collection of the delinquent accounts on real property taxes and stall rentals through the Municipal Treasurer, to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the Municipality geared towards the welfare of the constituents.
We further recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to include the payment of delinquent accounts for stall rentals in the policies and procedures of the Municipality before approving the renewal of Business Permits and Licenses of stall owners to intensify the collection efforts on delinquent stall rentals.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to facilitate the setting up and maintenance of GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive, planning, programming and policy formulation in order to serve the need of its constituents relative to gender-related issues; and
Instruct the GAD Focal Person to request the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to update the LGU GAD Code to support LGU’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s empowerment and gender equality.

We recommended that Management require the Head of the Solid Waste Management Board to ensure adherence to the provision of Section 16 of RA No. 9003 by causing the immediate completion and approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan which shall serve as the foundation, guide and timetable in the identification, implementation and operation of solid waste management programs.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Waste Officer Designate to strictly observe the criteria provided in Sections 41 and 42 of RA No. 9003 in the establishment and operation of Sanitary Landfill to ensure the protection of public health and its environment.
We further recommended that Management require the Waste Officer Designate to immediately utilize the Plastic Melter Densifier and the Multipurpose Shredder to avoid deterioration of the same and attain the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

In view of the regulation on the one-time cleansing of PPE, we proposed to, subject to the Management to approval, the PPE Inventory Committee to dedicate a period of the year, as guided by a Physical Inventory Plan, on which complete and thorough physical count of the PPEs will be conducted in accordance to the said Circular. After which, require the immediate reconciliation of count results with the accounting records to facilitate the review of the noted variances, effect the necessary adjustments, provision of necessary disclosures in the notes to financial statements, and the necessary disposition for those non-existing/missing and disposal of unserviceable items.
We also recommended and the Management agreed to require the PPE Inventory Committee to tag with complete information the newly acquired properties before issuance to the requesting offices with corresponding Property ledger cards and Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) to establish their property accountability and maintain property cards while the Accounting Office also maintain and regularly update their PPE ledger card.
observations.managementAction:
The Management is hopeful that the limitation on conducting the physical count of all PPE items will be addressed by the proposed institution of a General Service Office. They agreed to undertake the preliminary activities of reconciling accounting records with the partial count results to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE.
However, the Management has no concrete plans regarding the one-time cleansing and the Committee that will facilitate the process was not created.
Acknowledgement Receipts (PAR) are being issued to accountable officers for current purchases. PPE Property Ledger cards are maintained by the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Cleansing of PPE was not among the priorities for 2021.

observations.managementAction: Income for the Trust Fund was recorded and reflected as Transfers from National Government Agencies and Grants Donarions account. Expenses were likewise recorded.

Documents were submitted to the Audit Team on June 24, 2021 and were found in order.
The Management was able to ensure completeness of the procurement documents and documentation before accepting deliveries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Human Resource Officer ensure that in the adoption of any of alternative work arrangements (AWA) in the Municipality while on community quarantine, schedules of reporting to work are determined and office orders are approved prior to the actual render of service.
observations.managementAction: Refund was effected in December 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the MSWDO, in coordination with the barangay officials, to continuously work on maintaining/updating profile of families under its jurisdiction to properly and readily identify the eligible beneficiaries of various programs/projects which may be initiated either by government or private sector and provide justification on the delay of submission of liquidation documents. We also recommended improvements to the distribution process and timely submission of liquidation.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO continuously work on updating the profile of families as a database for national and local amelioration programs. Inconsistencies and complaints noted in the first tranche of SAP was addressed during the second tranche of distribution of DSWD.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a. refrain the officials and employees from resorting to payment through disbursement and disbursement through reimbursement basis and comply strictly the relevant provisions of RA 9184 pay the suppliers directly by check;
b. require the disbursing officers to the timely liquidation of cash advances for operating expense, advances for travel and advances to special disbursing officer in line pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
c. consider designating more disbursing officers and to require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to ensure that no additional cash advances shall be granted to the disbursing officers unless previous cash advances are settled.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office was able to monitor the cash advances, and hence liquidation was made within the prescribed period. The Management complied with the recommendation and refrained from simultaneous cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Property Officer maintain subsidiary ledgers for Property and Equipment, maintain property cards that will carry details, such as the property identification, description, and cost, as well as the date when it is acquired or disposed, independent from the records of the Accounting Office to preserve the check and balance of the two Offices with respect to the Municipality’s property and equipment. We likewise recommended that the latter office maintain their own subsidiary ledger cards for a reliable reconciliation with results of inventory.
observations.managementAction:
The Property and Office maintains Property and Equipment. Subsidiary Ledger for Property and Equipment.
The Accounting Office maintains Property Ledger Cards for road networks but which were however not indicative of the road components and their distinct costs.
observations.reason: The PPE Inventory Committee does not maintain property cards.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer had provided only the Road Network Map. The Accounting Office maintains Property Ledger Cards for road networks but were however not indicative of the road components and their distinct costs.
observations.reason: The matter was not given priority by the concerned officials. Physical count of PPE, including road networks was not undertaken as of year-end of 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to provide reconciliation on the noted variance. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ascertain the completeness of the real property collections made, posted in its records and that the Municipal Accountant record receivables gross upon collection.
observations.managementAction: The set-up of RPT Receivable was based from the Tax Roll submitted by the Municipal Assessor and adjusted for collections made by the Treasury Office. Also, subsidiary ledgers per taxpayer are not maintained to facilitate reconciliation with the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index card maintained by the Treasury Office.
observations.reason: Receivables were recorded net of discounts as discounts granted for early collections made by taxpayers remain not reflected in the financial statements of CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all Municipal officials, Sangguniang Bayan Members, PRAISE Committee who approved the transaction and the recipients of the incentive to refund the amount of P3,532,500.00 to the Municipality. We also recommended the PRAISE Committee to strictly observe the basic policies stated in PRAISE and CSC Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2001, and ensure that the general and specific objectives of the program were achieved.
observations.managementAction:
Refund was effected.
A Notice of Suspension (NS) No. 100(2017), Office/Individual Performance and Commitment Review board at P35,000.00 each (OPCR/IPCR) were submitted. However, identification and nomination of specific accomplishments and their evaluation against established success indicators as basis for the PRAISE incentive as well as the annual report on the awards and incentives system duly received by CSC Regional Office No. IV remain not provided.
observations.reason: Refund remains not complied with.

observations.managementAction: The BPLO verified and reconciled the discrepancies noted in the computation of business tax for a total of P48,823.35. The remaining P11,471.50 is yet to be collected. The BPLO was not able to fully validate the continuing operations of those entities which did not secure permits for 2021.
observations.reason: Matter has not been given due attention by the BPLO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BPLO be required to ensure that all entities conducting business within the Municipality must obtain the required permit and duly pay the tax and fees prescribed by the Code. Also, verify the amount unpaid and collect them accordingly. Prospectively, require the BPLO to ensure all contractors secure necessary permits before award and collection of taxes.
observations.managementAction: The BPLO sent collection letters dated September 17, 2020 to various business owners. Two business owners were communicated to suspend transacting with the Municipality may be suspended, 4 out of the 7 noted contractors were awarded contracts in 2021 without settling their unpaid previous year’s business taxes. The BPLO was not able to fully validate the continuing operations of the entities which did not secure permits for 2021.
observations.reason: Collection was not duly enforced by the BPLO due to lack of manpower and the continuing pandemic restrictions for 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the submission and completeness of Driver’s Trip Ticket and ensure that the travel and consumption are properly supported prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: All offices of the Municipality were required to strictly comply. As a result, gasoline expenses were paid directly to suppliers. Drivers Trip Tickets were duly accomplished and supporting documents were attached to vouchers for the grant of fuel supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to come up with a formal and uniform policy with respect to grant, utilization, manner of payment and monitoring of telephone allowance and to directly contract telecommunication companies for procurement of reasonable postpaid plans under the name of the agency so that monitoring will be systematic and effective. Consumption of this allowance through prepaid cards must be put in halt.
observations.managementAction: Consumption of telephone allowance through prepaid cards were put in halt. Mobile expenses were paid directly to telecommunication companies. The Management complied and refrained from charging expenses that may not be considered for public purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management improve its planning and consolidation for procurement of the agency to minimize frequent but small purchases leading to payment through reimbursement and prepare the necessary documents that will support the transactions beforehand and not thereafter. Also require that the Head of offices use Stock Availability Inquiry (SAI) for inquiry of supplies availability and Requisitioning and Issue Slip (RIS) for issuance. Direct payment to suppliers is strictly observed.
observations.managementAction: Purchases for various offices are now directly paid to suppliers. The practice of reimbursement was reduced and regular supplies of various offices were procured on a consolidated basis. The Management is considering creation of a General Services Office (GSO) for property and supply management.
observations.reason: POs remain not consolidated by the Supplies and Procurement Committee. Lack of consolidation remains due to organizational setup; creation of GSO is proposed and subject to Sanggunian approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Barangay Officials in coordination with the Municipal Agriculture Office to prioritize funding for the establishment in their respective Barangays Material Recovery Facilities in compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor take the necessary actions to implement the plans and programs embodied in the approved Ten Years Solid Waste Management Plan. Update the Audit Team on the status of its proposed Sanitary Landfill.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has implemented the plan and programs embodied in the approved Ten Years Solid Waste Management Plan. The Eco-Waste Processing Center is operational and produces bricks for use in public schools in the Municipality. MENRO conducts monthly monitoring of MRFs and coastal cleanup in coordination with barangays.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through its GAD Focal Point System (GFPS):
 a. Facilitate the updating of its existing GAD database to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, with gender programming and policy formulation. Refer to Annex B of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-02 for identification of data requirements.
 b. Undertake thorough gender analysis, based on relevant data requirements for proper identification of existing gender issues, to ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are responsive to address the same.
observations.managementAction: The Council convened and implemented the planned relevant programs for 2021. Projects grouped by gender issues addressed: Client Focused GAD Programs had P9,737,000.00 budget and P6,364,620.00 expenditure. Organization Focused GAD Programs had P850,000.00 budget and P0 expenditure. Attribution to GAD budget was P10,587,000.00 with P6,364,620.00 expended.
observations.reason: Issues identified remain not based on actual events noted; relevant data to support discrimination and gender sensitive programs are not yet processed in relation to identification of gender sensitive programs/activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
 a. facilitate the LCPC by fully discharging its functions, duties and responsibilities;
 b. direct the members of the LCPC to prepare a Local Development Plan for Children, Local Investment Plan for Children and Local Code for Children; and
 c. ensure that the fund allocated for LCPC was fully maximized and the programs, projects and activities were implemented for the protection of children.
observations.managementAction: The LCPC implemented the plan and projects for 2021. A total of P1,397,748.87 was allotted and utilized for projects in relation to the protection of children.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the material discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due from and Due to LGUs and submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) of the adjustments made. Reconciliation especially at year-end must be followed-up in writing for documented verification of discrepancies and to elicit immediate response and action.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is being done with the Provincial Accounting Office. Differences in balances pertain to amounts that had no supporting records and are subject for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials and employees who were granted the incentives to refund the amount of P4,700,500.00. We also recommended that the PRAISE Committee ensure the nomination, identification and evaluation of superior performance against established success indicators as basis for the PRAISE incentive and submit the annual report on the awards and incentives system duly received by CSC Regional Office No. IV.
observations.managementAction: A Notice of Suspension (NS) No. 100(2017) was issued. Office/Individual Performance and Commitment Review board (OPCR/IPCR) were submitted. However, documentation on identification of specific accomplishments, innovations, inventions, superior deeds and evaluation against established success indicators remain not provided.
observations.reason: Refund remains to be complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the members of the Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: Storage room is being allotted for newly constructed building. Completion and actual transfer of documents could not yet be undertaken. Other fixtures are yet to be installed.
observations.reason: Physical completion and actual transfer of documents to the storage building could not yet be undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer/Property Officer be instructed to prepare Purchase Request for supplies and materials needed for the quarter based on the approved annual procurement program, prepare and maintain stock cards, issue supplies based on RIS and prepare SSMI and forward to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording, accept delivered items before inspection of the Municipal Inspector and submit RPCI. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record purchases of office supplies, inventory upon purchase and maintain perpetual inventory system and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for each supplies and materials and update for every delivery when DVs are prepared and issuances based on SSMI. Lastly, we recommended that the Head of offices use the Stock Availability inquiry (SAI) for inquiry of supplies availability and the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) /Purchase Request in requesting for supplies.
observations.managementAction: The supply and property management are coursed thru their respective administrators which transferred to the Municipal Administrator which later was transferred to the Municipal Property office. PARs were issued to end-users of supplies. Requisition and Issue Slips are issued to users and recorded. Supplies were issued outright upon direct issuance to end-users. Expenses recorded by the Accounting Office are based on SSMI.
observations.reason: Physical count of inventory of supplies starting 2017 was assigned and delegated to the Office of the Property Officer; physical count was not undertaken. Some practices remain inconsistent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials refund the relevant amounts and that the Municipal Accountant examine and verify liquidation documents submitted and refrain from granting cash advances without authority to travel and detailed basis.
observations.managementAction: Documents were attached to some of the vouchers for the grant of cash advance and other documents were submitted during the audit. All cash advances were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials refund the amount of P906,474.53 (excluding P44,160.00) and refrain from claiming for expenses that do not serve any public purpose and which are irregular, unnecessary, and extravagant. We also recommended improved controls to avoid recurrence.
observations.managementAction: The Management opined that said expenditures were for public purpose in the discharge of their functions. The Management complied and refrained from charging comparable expenses for subsequent periods.

The Sanggunian Bayan granted authority to the Municipal Mayor to secure a loan for the construction of the landfill. The Eco-Waste Processing Center is operational and produces bricks. MENRO conducts monthly monitoring and coordination with barangays.
observations.reason: The construction did not materialize as financing from loan was not released; selected sites/locations are not viable for a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant adopt the perpetual inventory method in the purchase of inventories and to create control procedures on its receipt and issuances.

We also recommend that the Property Custodian submit the actual inventory of the supplies at year-end and that the corresponding asset and expense account be adjusted by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: Supplies inventory reports were submitted to the COA Audit Team.
observations.reason: In CY 2022, the LGU is recording supplies inventory instead of an outright expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce a more vigorous and effective tax campaign to attain 100% accomplishment or more in the collection of its targeted income for the year, so that identified programs, projects and activities can be fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: Strictly enforce the collections of revenues in CY 2021.
observations.reason: In CY 2021, the collections of the Municipality exceeded its targeted income by 2,616,238.96.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management submit the lacking supporting documents noted above.

Further, strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on documentary requirements in the disbursement of public funds, pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required supporting documents to the COA Audit Team.
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit written justification on the non-inclusion of warranty clause in the contracts/purchase orders. Further, strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the GPPB Circular No. 01-020 pertaining to warranty clause in order to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Submitted written justification and other supporting documents.
observations.reason: Required the suppliers to issue warranty clause in their contracts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management instruct the property custodian to trace the details of the recorded properties including the date acquired/completed in order to determine the remaining useful life of each asset. 

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation on all depreciable assets booked under the PPE in order to present fairly the account balances of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation expenses were provided in CY 2022. Prior period errors, in relation to the prior years’ depreciation expenses, were also recognized.
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit justification on the low collection ratio on RPT and SET; and in the difference of the balances of the two accounts.

We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction:
The low collection ratio is due to the COVID-19 pandemic. However, in CY 2022, collections were increased.

Also, reconciliation has been made to correct the RPT and SET.
observations.reason: In CY 2022, collections from RPT and SET increased to ₱268,700.77 and ₱203,619.74, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management submits:

a. Written explanation on the payment of claims for the delivery of equipment that does not conform to the specification called for and the substantial price difference;
b. Purchase Request
c. Invitation to Bid posted in PhilGEPS for the opening of bids conducted on June 16, 2020 

We further recommend that management strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of public funds specifically on Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-003, among other pertinent provisions. Otherwise, irregular/illegal/unconscionable expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: The audit team issued ND No. 21-001-200(20).

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments for these financial assistance/counterparts and require the recipients to submit the needed liquidations.

Moreover, record similar future transactions as Due from LGUs, a receivable account, to be recognized as expense only upon submission of liquidation by the implementing agency.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made to correct the Due from LGUs account. Also, liquidation reports were required from the barangays to submit to the COA Audit Team.
observations.reason: CY 2022 projects counterparts to the barangays are recorded under the Due from LGUs account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned accountable officers to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor a request for relief from accountability on properties lost/destroyed by fire, duly supported with all the required documentations, so that they can be relieved from any liability, and the cost of the properties could be dropped from the books of account of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Request for relief from accountability has been submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Request for relief has been submitted and is subject to review.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make appropriate revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.
observations.managementAction: Revisions on the GAD Plan and Budget in CY 2021 has been made.
observations.reason: Examination of the budget for CY 2021 disclosed that the Municipality allocated ₱ 6,598,365.20 or 5% of the total annual appropriations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the OIC Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge and record the wages of the contractual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services.

We also recommend that management limit the hiring of casuals/job order employees for intermittent, emergency jobs or specific piece of work with short duration as guided by CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and maximize the services of the permanent employees for regular agency functions.
observations.managementAction:
Management has directed the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to record wages of casuals/job order employees to its appropriate account. 
 
Stream-lining of employees is also highly considered in order to observe austerity measures.
observations.reason: Management has started the charging and recording of casual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services

observations.recommendation:
Use the funds received in accordance with the project proposals on a timely manner to maximize the beneficial use of government projects.

Effect the proper liquidation to the concerned agencies and remit the remaining     balances of the funds downloaded after the completion of the project unless these are not required to be returned to the trustor.

Review all balances specifically the excess funds for fully implemented projects.  Once ascertained, return the excess to source agencies or request authority to retain the amount for other purposes.
observations.managementAction: Remaining balances of unused funds after implementation of the desired projects were return to source agency.
observations.reason: Unutilized amounts were returned to the Source Agency,

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to persons who still has an outstanding balances urging settlement of their outstanding balances and request write-off of the balances for the other receivables that cannot be substantiated after exhausting all possible means and efforts.
observations.managementAction: Management has sent demand letters to various recipients for the account receivables.
observations.reason: Partial payments/refunds were made in the amount ₱2,350.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit justification for the non-creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.

Further, we recommend that the management initiate the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section 4.2 of NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC JMC No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: Two mandatory positions are created in 2022 that is for review to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.
observations.reason: The audit team requested a copy of the documents, once approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the current status of the debtors-NGOs/POs and assess the collectability of the outstanding receivables. Furthermore, we recommend that demand letters be sent to each debtors/NGOs/POs requiring immediate liquidation of the funds transferred in order to evaluate whether or not the purposes therefor were successfully attained.
observations.managementAction: Management has sent demand letters to various recipients for the account receivables.
observations.reason: Partial payments/refunds were made in the amount ₱49,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require all officials and employees concerned to submit immediately the liquidation reports of their outstanding cash advances and/or refund the unexpended cash balance, if any, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them and strictly enforce that no additional cash advance shall be granted until full liquidation of the previous cash advances is made. 

We also recommend that Management require the former municipal officials to make follow-up or send tracers for the issuance of Credit Advice from the COA Central Office and monitor the liquidation of the outstanding balance of the Confidential/Intelligence Fund.
observations.managementAction:
Liquidation reports for the Confidential and Intelligence Fund have been submitted to the COA Central Office.
 
The Municipal Accountant has sent demand letters to persons liable on the other outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Credit Notices from COA Central Office for the Confidential Fund has not been received by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Finance Committee expedite the updating and amendment of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code in pursuance to Section 191 of R.A. 7160 to provide additional revenue sources to fund the municipality’s developmental projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: In 2020, a joint session between Sangguniang Bayan and the Department Heads has already been conducted for the initial deliberation on the amendments of the Local Revenue Code.
observations.reason: Copy of the updated Local Revenue Code was not yet submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.
observations.managementAction: Required persons liable to immediately settle their disallowances.
observations.reason: Partial settlements were made on disallowances amounting to ₱743,038.90.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Property Officer initiate the preparation of stock/property cards for all PPE of the Municipality. 

We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory and Disposal Committee has been created by management.
observations.reason: Physical inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment is on-going. (Reiterated in Finding No. 4)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit written explanation/justifications on the causes of delay in the projects’ completion. We further recommend that henceforth, Management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.
observations.managementAction: Out of the five (5) infrastructure projects, only one (1) project has not been completed as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason:
Only the Construction of Water System and Sanitary Facilities at Schools, LGU-Mabini, Bohol (26 schools) has not been completed with a 95.53% accomplishment as of December 31, 2019.

No updates were received by the audit team as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct all accounting office personnel to assist the current municipal accountant in the identification of the receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has identified the identity of the debtor-organizations and have sent demand letters to debtor-organizations/recipients.
observations.reason:
List and names of debtor-organizations/recipients has been identified.

Demand letters were sent to some identified recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Supply Officer Designate to prepare the Inventory an Inspection Report of Unserviceable/Unused Properties and Equipment and facilitate the immediate disposal thereof in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties was submitted on February 20, 2019.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties was not supported with complete documentation.

Disposal of these unserviceable properties were not yet done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit written sufficient justification in pursuing the conduct of a fund raising activity despite the absence of a resolution from the Local Sanggunian authorizing the collection of fees, otherwise personnel involved thereto will be made to answer accordingly.   

We also recommend that Management refrain from conducting similar activities and charging fees without the approval and authority from the Local Sanggunian pursuant to Section 132 of the Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: No fund raising activity was conducted in CY 2019.
observations.reason: Management has not submitted any written justification in the conduct of the fund raising activity as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and the related DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the prompt and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the implementation of priority development projects in order to optimally attain the socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of projects under the 20% Development Fund was hampered due to lack of personnel in the Engineering Department to prepare the Plans and Program of Works.
observations.reason: In CY 2021, projects, with the total amount of ₱15,426,589.80, that are not yet implemented by the Municipality. (Reiterated in Finding No. 8)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the description of accounts in the Chart of Accounts prescribed under the GAM for LGUs in recording future financial transactions for proper presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entries to recognize the distribution of the inventories, to end-users and the purchase of equipment and construction/repair of evacuation center and Subsidy from National Government accounts in the financial statements.
