observations.managementAction:
Management complied with the audit recommendation.






Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entries to reclassify the Power Supply System in the amount of ₱699,870.00 to the account Machinery and the Insurance Expense in the amount of ₱24,997.48 to the account Taxes, Duties and Licenses in order to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to observe the description of accounts in the Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-004 dated January 31, 2020 in preparing the municipal annual budget and in recording future financial transactions, respectively, for proper presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

observations.managementAction:
Management complied with the audit recommendation.






Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to support the claims.

	We also, recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims are duly supported with complete documentation before affixing his signature in the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the practice of granting benefits such as per diem except for those specifically provided by laws, rules and regulations to the JO workers/COS, be discontinued and to strictly adhere to the regulations of the CSC Resolution.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the MSWDO to cause the refund of the financial subsidy amounting to ₱88,000.00 given to 16 beneficiaries who were not listed in the Certificate of Eligibility for the ESP of the DSWD; and 

Require the MSWDO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to submit the copy of valid ID, Barangay Certification with specimen signature of the beneficiary and authorization letter of the beneficiaries provided in the schedule for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to discontinue the practice of allowing the payment of snacks and meals as representation expenses to the Municipal officials and to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 343 of RA No. 7160 and the GAA.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Require the BAC to submit the complete supporting documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and

Instruct the BAC to ensure that the utilization of the funds is in accordance with Annex B of GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020.
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted the required documents.




Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: That officials and employees refrain from resorting to reimbursement and improve planning and consolidation for procurement; procure ordinary and regular office supplies from PS-DBM; and support claims with the required documents prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursement were still allowed. Purchases remain not consolidated.
observations.reason: Management did not give due attention. Frequent and minute purchases were still observed.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Treasurer prioritize and take proper monitoring action on the disbursements of the trust funds and that the Municipal Accountant provide disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements regarding the probability or status of recovery of such receivables.
observations.managementAction: No action is taken regarding the collectability of said receivables. Hence, information on the probability of collection is not disclosed.
observations.reason: Non-availability of documents hinders the recovery and assessment of collectability of such receivables.

observations.recommendation: a. Meticulously and judiciously plan procurement for each quarter and incorporate into the Annual Procurement Plan; b. Comply with procurement regulations and procure ordinary and regular office supplies from PS-DBM; c. When shopping from private suppliers, secure certification from PS-DBM that items are not available.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality does not consistently procure from PS-DBM and piecemeal purchases were still observed.
observations.reason: Procurement remains decentralized to different offices and piecemeal purchases were still observed.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Still no complete and updated records of PPEs maintained. Reiterated in Observation No. 3, pages 34 to 36 of this report.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The LGU maintained subsidiary ledger for PPE, however due to incomplete records, reconciliation of the account balances in the financial statement could not be made. The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the GSO will exert effort for the preparation of RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials concerned demand from the HLURB the issuance of Official Receipt for the payment of registration/training fee during the Finalization of the Draft Comprehensive Land Use Plan / Zoning Ordinance writeshop / worksh op to support the government expenditure incurred to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: Management made follow-up with the organizer of the seminar for the issuance of Official Receipt (OR).
observations.reason: Management was not able to submit the official receipt for the payment of registration / training fee during the Finalization of the Draft Comprehensive Land Use Plan / Zoning Ordinance writeshop / worksh op Hence, expenses for the seminar was not completely documented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, upon completion of physical inventory of properties, require the General Services Officer to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and initiate the proper disposal of the unserviceable property in accordance with the requirements and procedure provided in Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management
observations.reason: For CY 2020, there was no disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management cause the immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the lots owned by the Municipality to facilitate the transfer and registration of ownership with the Land Registration Authority in accordance with Section 39 of PD No. 1445 and Section 449 of GAAM Volume I to establish absolute ownership and protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership still on-going.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 5, pages 38 to 40 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with the provisions of Sections 2.3, 4.1, 4.2 and 5.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure that the funds appropriated for development projects are optimally utilized towards the achievement of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes which are beneficial to the constituents of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Management require the Municipal Engineer to submit Program of Works, Detailed Cost Estimates and other documents necessary to support the recorded transaction.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Wages of laborers were still charged to 20% DF. The Municipal Engineer did not submit required documents. Reiterated in Observation No. 7, pages 41 to 43 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to the provision of Section 20 of MNGAS by addressing the concern on accounts because inadequacy of manpower and by requiring the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to maintain Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card and duly certified list of taxpayers to provide an accurate basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property and special education taxes at the beginning of the year and to facilitate the reconciliation of accounts for fair presentation of its accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management informed the Audit Team that the Municipal Treasurer submitted a certification indicating the amount to be collected for the year as the basis for setting up receivable at the beginning of the year. This is so since the Municipal Accountant was not receiving assessment rolls from the Municipal Assessor’s Office in the previous years. Management committed to provide the necessary document for the RPT and SET Receivables for the adjustment of the receivable accounts.
observations.reason: The Audit Team could not verify the reliability of the balances because we found no schedules or details of taxpayers and the amount of tax due to support the reported balances. Reiterated in Observation No. 6, pages 40 to 41 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should enforce the segregation of wastes and avoid using the open dumpsite, instead, provide for an appropriation for the development of sanitary landfill for the disposal of the sanitary wastes collected in conformity with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 and to involve the participation of the barangays in proper disposal of wastes.
observations.managementAction: Management already enforces segregation of waste and provided areas for segregation. The Municipality collected and segregated waste in the public market area.
observations.reason: Management noted that not all provided areas enforce segregation of waste especially on the public market area. Reiterated in Observation No. 18, pages 60 to 62 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with Sections 121 and 124, Volume I of MNGAS on the proper accounting of inventories by directing the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required records and to immediately prepare/submit the required RSMI with the original copies of the RIS for CY 2019 to the Accounting Division/Unit as basis in the recording/adjustments in the books of accounts. We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain ledger cards and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain stock cards, conduct physical count of inventory items and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to be reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Management was not able to comply with the audit recommendation. The Municipal Accountant maintained ledger cards. Validation of the inventory account could not be made due to lack of stocks cards maintained by the Municipal Treasurer and no RPCI to be reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide a motor pool or well-secured garage as a measure to protect and safeguard the motor vehicles owned and maintained by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to provide well-secured garage for its motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that the LGU officials and employees concerned exert extra effort to intensify collection of delinquent stall rentals and real property and adopt the necessary measures provided in Sections 254 and 256 of RA No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991, to enable the collections of real property taxes. These will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to the concerned taxpayers and stall holders for the delinquent taxes and rentals. Tax campaign programs and tax information drive were conducted by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Management informed that demand letters were sent and tax campaign programs were conducted and promised they will exert more effort in the collection of delinquent property taxes and penalties on stall rentals. Remedies for collection were not strictly imposed by Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 17, pages 59 to 60 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure efficient, effective and economical use of government resources by requiring the immediate utilization of the Plastic Melter Densifier and the Multipurpose Shredder in the Material Recovery Facility, train personnel to operate the said equipment and provide adequate safeguard for the equipment against possible loss due to theft.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: The Plastic Melter Densifier and the Multipurpose Shredder remained idle/deteriorating and not in use in the MRF and did not attain the benefits intended to its constituents. Reiterated in Observation No. 18, pages 60 to 62 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the HRMO to cause the issuance of contract of service to the JO workers and to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 4(6) and 4(7) of PD No. 1445 and the CSC Resolution.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that they will comply with CSC Resolution 021480 Policy Guidelines for Contract of Service.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management send written notice to the contractor, requiring them to immediately finish/complete the project, otherwise, cause the termination of the contract pursuant to Annex “I” of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, confiscate the contractor’s performance bond and withhold the 10% retention moneys and use these funds for the completion of the project.
observations.reason: No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately return/remit the unexpended balances of the Due to National Agencies account to agencies concerned and secure Official Receipts pertaining thereof.
observations.reason: No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management review the operation of the swine breeding center and formulate procedures including the use and/or disposal of the municipality’s share of the offspring, as guided by Title VI of RA 7160.
observations.reason: No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management-

Require Mr. Gaudioso T. Libres, the designated Caretaker of the Swine Breeding Center of the municipality, to submit valid written justification on disposing three (3) government-owned pigs in violation to Section 356 of RA 7160 and Title V of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management. 

Require the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Auditor the memorandum of agreement entered into by the Department of Agriculture, the municipality and Mr. Silverio T. Miano, for verification.

Require the former Officer-in-Charge of the Office of the Municipal Agriculture, Mr. Aguirico T. Bernales to submit valid written justification on the failure of his Office to monitor and supervise the proper implementation of the Dispersal Program 

Conduct re-evaluation on the reasonableness of the weight on each pig as declared, taking into consideration their ages/number of months as reflected in the Animal Release Orders and require immediate remittance of the proceeds to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for proper accounting and recording of government funds.

We further recommend that Management require the refund from the persons determined to be accountable and liable for the amount of losses incurred in the improper disposal of government-owned live stocks pursuant to Section 377 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 105 of Presidential Decree 1445, without prejudice to the filing of administrative and/or criminal case, if warranted.
observations.reason: No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management conduct physical inventory count of the Work/Zoo Animals recorded in the books and to submit to the Auditor the report on the physical count thereof not later than January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.  Likewise, we further recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) as provided under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.reason: No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management refrain from incurring expenditures for the Organic Demonstration Farm until an ordinance regulating the overall operations of the economic enterprise shall have been crafted and passed by the Local Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: Management has not acted on the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The municipality has not crafted an ordinance to regulate the overall operations of the Organic Demonstration Farm, a local economic enterprise.

observations.recommendation:
Municipal officials and employees invited to attend seminars and workshop with a private training institution should ensure that the event is properly authorized by the Department of Interior & Local Government (DILG).

To secure from Bernales Leadership & Research Institute an accreditation of the convention or seminar design or training module by the Local Government Academy.
observations.reason: Required documents still not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council to formulate projects to be funded by 20% Development Fund in consonance with the requirements prescribed in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 that is geared towards the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental goals that would uplift the quality of life of its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Management still included some projects which are not within the list of priority projects under the 20% Development Fund during the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board may convene with the Local Development Council in the next budget period and consider additional resources that may be provided for the ultimate establishment of Sanitary Landfill the soonest possible time to prevent potential risk that may endanger the lives of neighboring residents.
observations.managementAction: The management had been studying ways on how to comply with the requirements in the establishment and operation of the required sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Currently, only a unit of garbage compactor is operating as well as the lack of sufficient garbage vehicle, thus hindering the full operation of a residual containment area

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to:

1. Coordinate with the SB to pass a resolution revisiting and formulating a doable policies and guidelines on the imposition of fines and penalties, taking into consideration the specific functions and duties and their limitations of the proposed collectors to be identified as enforcers.

2. Provide the adequate supply of resource whether it is in financial terms and adequate technical manpower equipped with skills and able to deliver the required services in support of Community Education and Public Awareness campaign, collections of fees and other fines and penalties and other duties and functions deemed necessary in the operation.
observations.managementAction: The management is still considering the need to review their solid waste management plan.
observations.reason: As assessed by the management, they claimed that the Solid Waste Management Plan needs to be thoroughly reviewed and updated to make it more proactive and applicable. Only after thorough review can policies, processes and procedures meet the intended outcomes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government to prepare and enter into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the recipient beneficiaries of the agricultural, fisheries and seaweed projects under the Bottom-Up Budgeting Program. 

Make sure that the duties and responsibilities of binding parties are clearly defined and established in order to at least ensure the smooth implementation, effectiveness, and sustainability of the projects. 

Stop the practice of not entering into a MOA with the intended beneficiaries when implementing priority programs and projects.

Follow up the delivery of the remaining 16 cattle to ensure the success of the cattle production project. Impose appropriate liquidated damages for the delayed deliveries.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to prepare Memorandum of Agreements.
observations.reason:
MOA preparation is impractical to prepare at this time due to the following reasons in the implementation of the projects:

1.Corn Production Enhancement-This was not sustained due to long drought that hit at the start of the project.
2.Seaweed Stilt Drier-Not productive due to ice-ice disease.
3.Bangus Production in Marine Fishcage- Not productive because the area is silted that affects its operation.
4. Cattle Production/Re-Stocking- this was not realized due to failure of bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government, the immediate usage of donated lot for the operation and management of Eco-Park with Alternative Technology Approach for its final waste disposal and for the purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes to sustain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan.

Prioritization of the funding for these properties should be immediately done so that the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste would not be hampered.

We recommended also the stoppage of the operation of the present disposal site and the strict implementation of the waste segregation policy of the Municipality from the source, in order to successfully attain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, management was not able to utilize another area of their lot for the operation of new controlled dumpsite. The purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes was not also realized due to lack of funds.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the regulation on the one-time cleansing of PPE, we recommended and
the Management agreed to consider mandating the PPE Inventory Committee to
dedicate a period of the year, as guided by a Physical Inventory Plan, on which
complete and thorough physical count of the PPEs will be conducted in accordance
to the said Circular. After which, require the immediate reconciliation of count
results with the accounting records to facilitate the review of the noted variances,
effect the necessary adjustments, provision of necessary disclosures in the notes
to financial statements, and the necessary disposition for those non-
existing/missing and disposal of unserviceable items.
We also recommended and the Management agreed to consider requiring the PPE
Inventory Committee to tag with complete information the newly acquired
properties before issuance to the requesting offices with corresponding Property
Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) to establish their property accountability and
maintain property cards while the Accounting Office also maintain and regularly
update their PPE ledger card.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. record income and expenses in the Trust Fund books upon utilization of funds
received from national agencies or any other sources as well as from trust
liability for unspent disaster fund from previous years that are considered as
non-exchange transactions;
b. prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial
Performance for the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of the
Municipality.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO), Municipal Social
Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) and Municipal Accountant to submit the
following:
a. Tax Returns (Annual Income Tax Return of the preceding tax year) or Business
Tax Returns (Value Added Tax or Percentage Tax Return covering the previous
six months) and original copy of Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) and Mayor’s
Permit of the suppliers for those transactions above ₱500,000.00;
b. Posting of Notice of Award in GPPB Online Portal for information purposes;
c. Purchase Order for the 300 (25kls) of rice procured from Bueno Rice Store under
Check No. 72109 dated April 7, 2020, indicating therein the kind of rice that was
purchased;
We also recommended that the Procurement and Supply Management Committee
that procurement documents and contracts are in order before accepting deliveries.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the BAC ensure that
brands/specifications along with the pertinent supplier details are indicated in the
canvass forms and Purchase Orders.
Lastly, we recommended that the BAC provide justification for the inconsistencies
on the purchase documents of 1,000 sacks (25kgs) Dinorado amounting to
₱1,165,000.00 from MRWN Rice Trading Corp and in the application of the
quotations for the purchases of 100 bags (25kgs) Sinandomeng Rice from Jojo
Balbuena Rice Mill costing ₱1,350.00/sack. Absence of justification may warrant the
disallowance of the noted excess price.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the refund of the double
and excess rate of COVID-19 Hazard Pay totaling ₱136,775.82.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Human
Resource Officer ensure that in the adoption of any of the alternative work
arrangements in the Municipality while on community quarantine, schedules of
reporting to work are determined and office orders are approved prior to the actual
render of service.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to require the bidders/suppliers
to indicate the brand/model/specifications in their bid/quotation to establish difference of items for fair comparison of prices which will be the basis of Purchase
Order/Contract.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to exercise due diligence in the performance of her duties and functions and be
vigilant in the checking and evaluation of all the documents of the procurement
transactions of the Municipality before certifying as to the completeness of the
supporting documents in all the disbursement vouchers.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Procurement & Supply
Management Committee to ensure that all the pertinent and specific information of
the items being procured are correctly indicated in the Purchase Order.
Lastly, we also recommended that the PPE Inventory Committee be required to
locate the noted missing item and submit to the Audit Team status of the remaining
items not presented in audit and to provide justification for accepting delivery of
duplicating machine and ink supplies not in conformity with the Purchase Order.

To foster improvements, we recommended and the Management agreed to require
the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO), in coordination
with the barangay officials, to continuously work hand-in-hand in
maintaining/updating profile of families under its jurisdiction to properly and readily
identify the eligible beneficiaries of various programs/projects which may be
initiated either by government or private sector and provide justification on the
delay of submission of liquidation documents.

Therefore, to reduce the risk of wastage of government funds for this undertaking,
we recommended and the Management agreed to require the Local Development
Council to diligently assess the technical, social and financial viability of the project
based on results of the study conducted.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a. refrain the officials and employees from resorting to payment through cash
advances and make payments directly to the suppliers;
b. require the disbursing officers to timely liquidation of cash advances for
operating expense, advances for payroll and advances to special disbursing
officer in line and pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
c. consider designating more disbursing officers and to require the Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer to ensure that no additional cash advances shall be
granted to the disbursing officers unless previous cash advances are settled.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the description of accounts in the Chart of Accounts prescribed under the
GAM for LGUs in recording future financial transactions for proper presentation of
accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
to draw journal entries to recognize the distribution of the inventories, to end-users
and the purchase of equipment and construction/repair of evacuation center and
Subsidy from National Government accounts in the financial statements.

The following is the suggested journal entry:
Welfare Good Expense 7,999,400.00
Drugs and Medicines Expense 999,190.00
Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Expense 348,642.00
Food Supplies Expense 167,541.50
Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expense 132,456.95
Rent Expense 83,375.00
Other Supplies and Materials Expense 23,600.00
Buildings 1,000,000.00
Other Property, Plant and Equipment 554,600.00
Other Machinery and Equipment 45,000.00
Subsidy from National Government 11,353,805.45

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
draw journal entries to reclassify the Power Supply System in the amount of
₱699,870.00 to the account Machinery and the Insurance Expense in the amount of
₱24,997.48 to the account Taxes, Duties and Licenses in order to present the correct
balances of the said accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer and the Municipal Accountant to observe the description of accounts in the
Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-004 dated January 31,
2020 in preparing the municipal annual budget and in recording future financial
transactions, respectively, for proper presentation of accounts in the financial
statements.

The following are the suggested journal entries:
Machinery 699,870.00
Depreciation Expense – Machinery 58,322.50
Accumulated Depreciation – Power Supply System 58,322.50
Power Supply System 699,870.00
Depreciation Expense – Power Supply System 58,322.50
Accumulated Depreciation – Machinery 58,322.50
Taxes, Duties and Licenses 24,997.48
Insurance Expense 24,997.48

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant adopt the perpetual inventory method in the purchase of inventories and to create control procedures on its receipt and issuances.
We also recommend that the Property Custodian submit the actual inventory of the supplies at year-end and that the corresponding asset and expense account be adjusted by the Municipal Accountant.

We recommend that the management submit the lacking supporting documents noted above.
Further, strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on documentary requirements in the disbursement of public funds, pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the non-inclusion of warranty clause in the contracts/purchase orders. Further, strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the GPPB Circular No. 01-020 pertaining to warranty clause in order to protect the interest of the government.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the procurement of medical supplies with prices exceeding the price ceiling provided by the DOH. Otherwise, require the supplier to refund the excess amount computed based on the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0144A and 2020-0250A.
We further recommend that management observe the DPRI and other related issuances of the DOH in the succeeding procurement of medicines and medical supplies pursuant to Republic Act No. 7581 and DOH Administrative Order No. 2014-0001.

We recommend that the Property Officer initiate the preparation of stock/property cards for all PPE of the Municipality.
We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that the management instruct the property custodian to trace the details of the recorded properties including the date acquired/completed in order to determine the remaining useful life of each asset.
We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation on all depreciable assets booked under the PPE in order to present fairly the account balances of the LGU.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit justification on the low collection ratio on RPT and SET; and in the difference of the balances of the two accounts.
We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We recommend that the management submits:
Written explanation on the payment of claims for the delivery of equipment that does not conform to the specification called for and the substantial price difference;
Purchase Request
Invitation to Bid posted in PhilGEPS for the opening of bids conducted on June 16, 2020
We further recommend that management strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of public funds specifically on Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-003, among other pertinent provisions. Otherwise, irregular/illegal/unconscionable expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments for these financial assistance/counterparts and require the recipients to submit the needed liquidations.
Moreover, record similar future transactions as Due from LGUs, a receivable account, to be recognized as expense only upon submission of liquidation by the implementing agency.

We recommend that the BAC, through the Chairperson, submit the lacking documents for the payment of honoraria to establish the validity and regularity of the claims and the payment thereof; and to facilitate audit of the said transactions.
We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents and refrain from processing claims of BAC honoraria if it is not supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that management submit justification for the non-creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.
Further, we recommend that the management initiate the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section 4.2 of NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommend that management assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its AAPSI for the AAR 2019.
Further, we recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR in its ensuing years.

We recommend that management make written representation to the BIR in order to (a) ascertain whether the activities are taxable or not, (b) apply for an Authority to Print (ATP) for receipts or invoices and pay the Annual Registration Fee, if necessary, (c) determine the taxes due, if applicable.

We recommend that management make appropriate revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.

We recommended that Management render submission of a document stating the details of project requirements, subject to the guidelines in the GPPB Circular 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 as prescribed under RA 11469 to prove the necessity and propriety of the transactions that were undertaken on emergency procurements, otherwise the same shall be suspended in audit; it shall likewise adhere to the prevailing rules and regulations in the preparation of project requirements during emergency situations in order to facilitate smooth implementation of its procurement activities as planned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (1) instruct the BAC Secretariat, Budget Officer and End-Users or Implementing Units to coordinate and ensure the preparation of APP in accordance with the prescribed format and that additional procurement projects, if any, to be conducted during the year should be incorporated in the revised APP, duly approved by the HoPE or the second-ranking official designated as authorized representative/signatory to act on her behalf; and, (2) require the concerned BAC officials to facilitate the submission of the APP in its original and revised form on procurement projects undertaken in the CY 2020

We recommended the following to Management:
Require that the BAC officials to explain in writing as to non-submission of the documentary requirements prior to award of contract or before payment is made; and,
Instruct the Accounting personnel concerned to review the completeness of documents submitted before processing claims of the supplier as embodied in Section 4.1 of GPPB Circular 01-2020.

We recommended that Management obtain authority from the SB granting the LCE the permission to accept donations whether in cash or in-kind from local or foreign sources. Likewise, we recommended the submission of resolutions, stating that the acceptance of donations received were authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that Management adopt the minimum requirements of the laws and pertinent accounting and reporting guidelines, particularly applicable to in-kind donations received by the Municipality from various donors with the following courses of actions, to wit:
Direct the concerned GSO to adopt the minimum requirements of law in order to establish a clearly written set of policies stating the well-defined duties and responsibilities for a specific level of authority to be assigned in the recording, distribution, inventory management, storage and reporting of donated/procured items.
Require the designated Property/Supply Officer to:
Submit Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances complete with complete supporting documents.
Submit Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM only for the remaining balances each kind of relief goods using the prescribed format.
Provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements stating information on the donations received, distributed to end-users and other relevant information as may be required under the prevailing guidelines for accounting and reporting purposes.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee and other responsible officials to ensure that purchase order and other pertinent documents should be properly accomplished with the necessary information stating the date of acceptance or receipt by the supplier of its commitment to deliver the goods and services. Likewise, require the supplier to cause refund for delays incurred in the delivery of the procured items, where applicable.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the veracity and completeness of similar transactions which must be signed and approved only when the required data are complete in order to compel the supplier of its obligation to deliver the goods and services as per written agreement.

We recommended that Management direct the officials concerned to submit the required relief distribution sheets that should be properly accomplished with adequate information sating the specific item description and volume of goods received, complete address and signature of the actual recipient in order to determine whether goods were actually received by the designated beneficiaries. Moreover, it is recommended that Management observe adherence to the prescribed standards and procedures issued by the COA in order to establish accountability of the responsible officials/employees should there be any lapses in the distribution of goods in any relief operation of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Unit, Inventory Committee, and Property Custodian to take actions on the following:
Perform periodic reconciliation to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts;
Prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balances;
Maintain complete and accurate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger   Card   (PPELC)   for   each   category   of   plant,   property   and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards (PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; and,
Create an Inventory as well as Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.
Execute an executive order to commence a one-time cleansing of the Municipality’s Property, plant and equipment account and to established the itemized listing of properties that should be recorded therein; and,
Derecognize unserviceable properties in the PPE accounts after complying to the disposal procedures provided under Section 267 of the Local Government Accounting Manual and Sections 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Memorandum Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile discrepancies noted, and provide additional information to be presented on the notes to financial statements which must be concise but briefly discussed using a narrative and descriptive formats, taking into consideration that the effects of financial transactions of significant amount. To ensure that the same is clearly understandable to its interested users, disclosure of such relevant information must be duly supported with corroborating schedules that must be consistent with the reported balances shown in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Government Equity.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant explain in writing their lapses in recording the proceeds arising from payment made by the Municipality itself for equipment rental charges to the 20% Development Fund for its own properties. Moreover, we recommended that Management adhere strictly to the accounting standards in the proper treatment of similar transaction, taking into consideration the underlying accounting principles of substance over form in the financial report preparation.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Head of the Municipal Health Officer case or the authorized representative occupying a regular plantilla position, if any to render a report stating the volume of donated items utilized or the number of issuances to end-user units as basis for the Accounting Office to record the transactions relative to the utilization/distribution of the donated drugs, medicines and medical supplies that shall be treated as expenses for a given reporting date.
Unless the personnel-in-charge is able to provide a report on the receipt and utilization of donated drugs, medicines and medical supplies from DOH, he/she will be held liable for whatever pilferages or wastages incurred from non-compliance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations for property and supply management and that the related transactions shall likewise be suspended in audit.
Establish accounting requirements/guidelines or implementing rules and regulations that are deemed applicable in the storage, reporting and monitoring of donated drugs, medicines and medical supplies from DOH and/or other government agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as Chairperson of the Municipal Development Council:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to revisit the screening and prioritization criteria in the selection and approval of proposed projects for funding under 20% Development Fund in full compliance with the requirements embodied in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure fair and equitable allocation of funds only for eligible development projects; and,
Provide legal bases on charging to the 20% Development Fund the cost of equipment rental for the utilization of heavy equipment owned by the Municipality in the implementation of road rehabilitation projects and the like.

We recommended that Management ensure that MDRRMFIP should be prepared with comprehensive details and relevant information, integrating the components of the funds, namely, the Current and Continuing Appropriation in the General Fund Budget, Special Trust Funds and from other sources in compliance with the requirements as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to cause the preparation of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of MDRRM Fund duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant. Likewise, submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA audit team assigned to review the financial transactions charged to LDRRMF of the Municipality through its MDRRM Council and Municipal Development Council, a copy of which shall be furnished to the Office of the Civil Defense.

We recommended for the LDRRMO to render submission of purchaser’s and dealer’s permit from the NTC as well as the registration from the Secretary of Commerce Industry in order to establish the validity and legality of the transaction.

We recommended that the Committee on Appropriation conduct a thorough deliberation of needs that are relevant in its endeavors geared towards the mitigation of disaster impacts or the establishment of disaster-resilient communities. Likewise, it was recommended that the personnel concerned explain in writing as to the basis/reason for allowing the said transaction in audit.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to conduct a comprehensive and periodic assessment of the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management operation against the MDRRMFIP prior to submission to the MDRRMC for approval and cause changes whenever necessary, subject to availability of funds;
Exhaust all means to adopt practices and strategies of other agencies to be considered in the planning and budgeting of the MDRRMF; and,
Limit the utilization of the MDRRMF to projects and activities which are deemed relevant in mitigating the risks of disasters.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to undertake the following courses of actions.
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer , together with the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Local Development Council  shall include in the Annual Investment Program only feasible projects  which can be implemented within the target date of completion with the proposed budget allocation, subject to the budgeting and other applicable standards for planning and thorough identification and proper scheduling of all related tasks in carrying out projects; to regularly monitor the status of project implementation in order to introduce the necessary interventions at the earliest time should there be any noted lapses during the implementation stage;
Instruct the BAC and the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to undertake the necessary steps for adequate planning of resources and proper scheduling of procurement activities to pave way for timely completion of the projects within the approved time and schedule.
Convene the Municipal Development Council in order to review all projects that remain unimplemented and determine whether the same can be implemented in the succeeding year. Likewise, consider the option to cause immediate realignment or reversion in the unappropriated surplus of the general fund for re-appropriation to other development projects of unutilized funds that are derived from savings from completed projects and all projects which are no longer needed for inclusion in the Annual Investment Program in the next budget preparation.

We likewise recommend that the latter office maintain their own subsidiary ledger cards for a reliable reconciliation with results of inventory.
We also recommended the inventory committee to conduct complete physical count of its PPE or sufficient enough to cover substantially all of the said assets and to dispose unserviceable properties in accordance with the regulations on disposal.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken to address the issues noted.

observations.recommendation: a. GSO to prepare and maintain Report on Local Road Network based on the report submitted by the Municipal Engineer; b. Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation for all road projects; c. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year; and d. Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards with complete details and disclose all the required information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer had provided only the Road Network Map but not the required description and cost segregation for all road projects. The Municipal Accounting Office cannot establish ledger cards without the detailed information on each road that is to be provided by the Municipal Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to provide reconciliation on the difference with the tax variance. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ascertain the completeness of the receivables by the Municipal Accounting Office and that the Municipal Accounting Office maintain property ledger cards that will carry details, such as the identification, description, and cost, as well as the date when it is acquired or disposed, independent from the render records of the Accounting Office to preserve the check and balance of the two Offices with respect to the Municipality’s property and equipment.
observations.managementAction: The Partially implemented 2020 collection has not provided justification on the non-collection on the said matter.
observations.reason: The Partially implemented 2020 collection has not provided justification on the non-collection on the said matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the PRAISE Committee strictly observe the basic policies stated in the Municipal PRAISE Model and ensure that the general and specific objectives of the program were achieved. We also recommended the Management to require the PRAISE Committee to strictly observe the basic policies stated in the Municipal PRAISE Model.
observations.managementAction: Refund was not made. The Municipal officials and employees has already stop distributing PRAISE incentive to municipal officials and employees.
observations.reason: Considering that family income is greatly affected by the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: a. levy and collect taxes and fees in accordance with the Municipality’s Revenue Code; b. require the submission of sworn statement of gross receipts/sales or certified copies of income tax return so as to come-up with an accurate basis for the computation of business taxes; c. demand and disclose all the information necessary for the assessment and collection of taxes and fees; and d. verify, assess and validate the noted undercollection of ₱60,294.85 and consequently demand the collection/payment thereof. We also recommended that the BPLO provide justification on the non-collection of taxes from certain operators.
observations.managementAction: The Partially Implemented 2020 collection has not provided justification on the non-collection on the said matter.
observations.reason: The Partially implemented 2020 collection has not provided justification on the non-collection on the said matter.

observations.recommendation: That the BPLO verify, assess and collect unpaid business taxes and fees from contractors awarded infrastructure projects, and prospectively ensure that all entities conducting business within the Municipality obtain the required permit and duly pay the tax and fees prescribed by the Code.
observations.managementAction: The BPLO already sent collection letters to the various business owners dated September 17, 2020. Two business owners have communicated and paid amounts but no further actions documented.
observations.reason: Verification on the sampled 2020 collection revealed that only one (1) contractor of the Municipality secured business permit. The BPLO did not exercise further actions to impose taxes and penalties on other contractors.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of the documents authenticating the claims for reimbursement prior to its approval and processing.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is now ensuring the completeness of the documents.

observations.recommendation: That all official travels made by the Municipal personnel be supported by properly accomplished driver's trip ticket and that the Municipal Accountant ensure the submission and completeness of the same prior to approval of payment for the fuel consumption monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The Management practices still has incomplete details. Management did not prepare the monthly report regarding the gasoline expense.
observations.reason: Some of the driver’s trip ticket details were incomplete. The Municipality did not prepare the monthly report for fuel consumption.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor come up with a formal and uniform policy with respect to the grant, utilization, reimbursement and monitoring of telephone and other expenses; direct the Municipal offices to contract with telecommunication companies for procurement of postpaid plans; and require that the officials and employees utilize free communication services where feasible and support claims with necessary documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management opined that said payments were for public purpose. Some documents such as monthly billing and statement of account were provided.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit documents that would show the necessity and public purpose for the food and hotel accommodations and support claims with all the required documentations.
observations.managementAction: The supporting documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor revisit and upgrade the Municipality’s implementing guidelines on the AICS program and separately propose budget for AICS.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO has updated the implementing guidelines for the Municipality’s AICS program and separately proposed budget for AICS.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor take necessary actions to implement the plans and programs embodied in the approved Ten Year Solid Waste Management Plan. Update the Audit Team on the status of its proposed Sanitary Landfill. Establishment of Sanitary Landfill and loan sourcing for necessary funds for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had designated 5 barangays as Material Recovery Facilities in Estrella, Pulong Niogan, Sampaguita, San Jose and San Juan. The Management provided that the Municipality is adopting results of a study conducted by Batangas State University on the proposed sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: The remaining barangays are being advised and reminded to implement the recommendation. The loan to establish its own sanitary landfill along with the acquisition of necessary equipment was approved but not yet supported by a comprehensive design and/or study, detailed cost consideration and long-term plan.

observations.recommendation: That the BAC and Technical Working Group monitor on-going projects to avoid overlapping projects and coordinate with the concerned government agencies and to the Municipality to ensure that the projects are implemented as scheduled. Ensure presence of minutes of pre-bid conferences for projects with ABC of One Million or more.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer maintains a daily log monitoring of on-going projects to avoid overlapping and delay in project accomplishment. Suspension orders/extension of contract time as approved by the head of the municipality were applied.
observations.reason: Some projects were implemented but documentation (minutes of pre-bid conferences) for contracts with ABC of One Million or more were not provided.

observations.recommendation: a. Facilitate the updating of its existing GAD database to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. b. Undertake thorough gender analysis, based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues. c. Prioritize utilization of 20% Development Fund and ensure idle projects are implemented and reflected in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management noted that the Special Education Fund has the high percentage of unobligated amounts. The Local School Board has been monitoring implementation of projects and payments were made in 2019 for projects bid out in 2018. The Municipal Accountant will prepare necessary adjusting entries in 2021.
observations.reason: As of year-end 2020, the hospital is already manned and operating. The Local School Board noted that many SEF projects had high unobligated amounts; payments were made only in CY 2019 for projects implemented in CY 2018. Adjusting entries were not effected as of reporting date; adjustments to be effected in 2021.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to evaluate carefully the programs and projects identified during the year in accordance with the provisions stated in Section 272 of RA 7160 and of the DepEd/DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board had been monitoring implementation and the Municipal management stated that capital outlay under the Special Education Fund has the high percentage of unobligated amounts. Payments for some projects were made in CY 2019. The Municipal Accountant will prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct PPE accounts in 2021.
observations.reason: Most of the projects remain with unobligated balances and some payments were made late. Adjusting entries not yet effected as of reporting date.

observations.recommendation: a. Facilitate the updating of its existing GAD database to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. b. Undertake thorough gender analysis, based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues. c. Provide for a storeroom sufficient to keep documents for ten (10) years for safekeeping.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality provided attachments. The GAD Focal Point System has not yet fully processed the gender sensitive programs/activities.
observations.reason: Issues identified remain not responsive; GAD programs are not yet processed in relation to identification of gender sensitive programs/activities.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor require the LCPC to improve the level of functionality of the LCPC by fully discharging its functions, duties and responsibilities; direct the LCPC members to prepare a Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC) and Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC); and ensure that the budget allocated for LCPC is fully maximized and utilized for the protection for children.
observations.managementAction: A total budget of ₱1,304,152.98 for children protection was allocated but activities were not implemented as planned. The Municipality has not prepared the LIPC. The Municipal management did not give due attention.
observations.reason: The plan was not supported by an investment plan. The Municipality did not give due attention and programs were not implemented as planned.

observations.recommendation: a. The Municipal Accountant to remit to the source agency any unexpended balances of fund transfers after project completion and the Provincial share for building permit fees; b. MSWDO and Procurement to exhaust all possible efforts to collect outstanding balances from source agencies; c. Make necessary reversion after securing a certification from the source agency that you are no longer required to liquidate the fund.
observations.managementAction: Balances remain not reconciled. MSWDO and concerned staff were requested to act but no action taken. Some remittances were made for provincial share on building permits.
observations.reason: Some provincial shares for building permits were remitted. Other balances remained unreconciled and no documented actions made. No request for write-off was submitted.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and the other parties to verify and reconcile the differences and submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) of the adjustments made.
observations.managementAction: Necessary documents are being furnished to the other party for proper recording so as to avoid such material discrepancies.
observations.reason: Reconciliation with the Provincial Accountant is still on-going for the unreconciled balances. Some documents remain unavailable.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor require officials and employees to refund the amount of ₱35,000.00 each and create the PRAISE Committee and Grievance Machinery to perform their responsibilities with documentation of OPCR/IPCR and other required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Refund was not made. The PRAISE Committee and Grievance Committee have been created. OPCR/IPCR submission and evaluation were not consistently conducted; some PRAISE payouts continued across the board and not based on performance.
observations.reason: The PRAISE Committee and Grievance Committee were created; however, OPCR and evaluation of exemplary performance were not fully conducted and PRAISE was still granted across the board without proper basis.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor provide/maintain a storeroom sufficient to keep vouchers and related documents for the preservation period of ten (10) years and ensure proper safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had designated an office for the safekeeping of documents and a building for the preservation period of ten (10) years was provided.
observations.reason: A designated office was provided but full implementation is ongoing to keep all documents for the required preservation period.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor establish the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and fill required personnel positions and discharge duties pursuant to RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: The Disaster Risk Management Office is already manned by the newly created positions including Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction & Management Officer III (SG-18/1).

observations.recommendation: a. Procurement as the need arises should be avoided and purchases should be planned and consolidated; b. supplies should be recorded as inventory using perpetual inventory system and maintain subsidiary ledger cards; c. accept delivered items after inspection and prepare AIR and maintain PARs and SSMI.
observations.managementAction: Only purchases of accountable forms and other supplies were recorded as inventory. Supplies management is decentralized. Inventory taking of supplies was not among priorities.
observations.reason: Inventory taking of supplies was not among the priorities of the Management. Supplies are directly issued to requesting offices and a centralized storeroom was not yet established.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant examine and verify liquidation documents and ensure that grant of cash advances is supported by authority and program/activity design and that liquidation reports are properly accomplished with necessary data.
observations.managementAction: Some of the cash advances were supported by documents; however, the Municipality did not ensure the proper way of liquidation reports. Documents were attached to some vouchers.
observations.reason: Some vouchers and supporting documents were attached but the Municipality did not ensure full compliance and proper liquidation procedures for all advances.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor require concerned officials to refund amounts not properly supported and refrain from incurring unnecessary and extravagant expenses; and to ensure claims are supported by proper evidence.
observations.managementAction: No refund was made. Management opined said expenditures were for public purpose but some claims were reduced.
observations.reason: Management opined that said expenditures were for public purpose; some claims in audit were minimized.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor prioritize the establishment of the Municipality’s own sanitary landfill along with acquisition of necessary equipment and implement programs to achieve RA 9003 objectives: segregation, MRFs and sustainable disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality cited adoption of a study by Batangas State University on the proposed sanitary landfill and noted some actions such as donation and distribution of fertilizers and IEC programs; however, solid waste segregation by barangays not strictly adhered and MRFs were not fully functional.
observations.reason: Some MRFs established and IEC conducted but effective solid waste management not fully achieved; sanitary landfill plan not yet supported by comprehensive design and financial plan.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to cause the closing of bank accounts in private rural banks and avail the services of Land Bank of the Philippines in order to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Approval was granted to maintain account with Land Bank of the Philippines - Bauan Branch. Resolution No. 273-2017 authorized opening of new current account with Land Bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct annual physical inventory of all properties and prepare the required RPCPPE to be reconciled with the accounting records and submit copy of the same to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year. b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated records to facilitate the reconciliation of the balances of the PPE with the accounting records; and c. Ensure compliance with the procedures provided by existing laws, rules and regulations on property and supply management.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee was created by the Management Municipal Mayor complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management advise the BAC to prepare and submit to the Head of the Procuring Entity the Procurement Monitoring Report and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. We further recommended that the BAC furnish the audit team copy of the said report to facilitate monitoring on the status of the procurement process and to be used as basis for our contract review on the procured goods and infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: submitted the required documents. The BAC furnished the required documents.
observations.reason: The Audit Team verified the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert utmost efforts to meet the audit demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation. Verification of records and transmittal of accounts and documents disclosed that Management was able to submit required reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the personnel concerned of the Municipality to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and submit the same to the audit team for verification to ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively controlled.
observations.managementAction: submitted required report on fuel consumption.
observations.reason: Verification of records disclosed that Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat to submit all the necessary documentary requirements in order to support the said contract and for technical evaluation of the project to preclude the issuance of notice of suspension. We further recommended that the BAC require the Contractor to submit Tax Clearance with validity on or before the bidding date or documents to support the renewal thereof; otherwise, submit justification why the Tax Clearance submitted by the bidder valid only until June 27, 2018 was accepted for post qualification of the bids.
observations.managementAction: submitted the required documents. The BAC submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: The Audit Team verified that the required documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the copies of the contracts and purchase orders and all the necessary supporting documents within five (5) days from perfection thereof as required under Sections 3.1.1 to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Copies of contracts and purchase orders were submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure strict compliance with the provisions of Section 5.5.1 of NDRRMC–DBM–DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1, particularly in the utilization of LDRRMF. We further recommended that Management require the BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat to submit all the necessary documentary requirements to support the contract and payment thereof, to avoid issuance of notice of suspension.
observations.managementAction: submitted the required documents. The required documents were submitted.
observations.reason: The Audit Team verified the submission of the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with the procedures provided by existing laws, rules and regulations on Local Road Network System and: a. Create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct physical inventory of all road networks and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the local government unit; b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant, the Engineer GSO/Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Engineer to maintain and prepare the required records and reports and to facilitate the recording of the road networks in the books; and c. Take appropriate action against the concerned accountable official/s for non-compliance and repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements pursuant to Section 127 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management created an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all the properties of the LGU. The Municipal Engineer submitted the inventory report of local road networks of the LGU.
observations.reason: The Municipal committee was created and submitted inventory of local road networks. However the inventory report was incomplete. Reiterated in Observation No. 13, pages 51 to 53 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should state the functions and duties in the Contract of Service to be performed by the consultant in the Municipality. In addition, it is further recommended that the following required documents be submitted by consultant concerned and the Municipal Accountant pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 for further evaluation of the Audit Team: a. Accomplishment Reports of the Consultant for the period January to December 2019; and b. Copy of reports or outputs submitted by the Consultants to the Local Chief Executive to support the LGU.
observations.managementAction: already included the functions and duties to be performed by the consultants in the Contract of Service. Accomplishment Reports and outputs were submitted by the Consultants to the LGU.
observations.reason: Accomplishment Reports and outputs were submitted. The Audit Team verified the submitted outputs and accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit justification in writing on why financial assistance under the AICS Program were granted to clients despite the absence of supporting documents as enumerated in Section 4.2  of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 15, Series of 2014.  

Also, direct the Municipal Social Welfare Officer to submit the records of the cases and all other basic supporting documents relative to the claims for further evaluation, otherwise the transactions will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Management directed all persons liable/responsible to refund the whole amount of financial assistance granted. | Notice of disallowance has been issued. Also, refund has been taking place on installment basis. 

Total amount refunded as of December 31, 2020 is ₱353,950.00.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Management require all officials and personnel who have participated in the Benchmarking Activity to refund the amount so received, otherwise the officials who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable.
observations.managementAction: Motion for Reconsideration dated May 30, 2016 was filed at the Office of the Regional Director on the Notice of Disallowance No. 15-001-100(15) amounting to ₱1,333,000.00.
observations.reason:
Notice of disallowance has been issued.

Partial refund of persons liable has been taking place on installment basis.  As of December 31, 2020, the total amount refunded is ₱389,088.90.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the concerned official submit justification on using the unliquidated cash advance in her subsequent travels.

We further recommend that management enjoins all officials and employees to adhere to Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: An Appeal was already submitted to COA Regional Office on the Notice of Disallowance No. 2016-001(15) BF on said travel.
observations.reason: Notice of disallowance has been issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned employees to strictly adhere with the required time frame on the submission of accounts and other financial reports to the Commission on Audit pursuant to PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-005, in order for the Auditor to review and audit the transactions recorded in the books and assure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Timely submission by management of the required monthly/quarterly/semestral and annual reports were not religiously adhered to.
observations.reason:
Trial Balances were prepared but were not submitted by the Municipal Accountant on time.

Year-end financial reports were submitted promptly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit explanation and/or justification why the procurement of equipment rentals were split as discussed.

We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee adheres to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations to ensure that all procurement activities of the municipality are in accord therewith. 

It is also recommended that the management instruct the municipal treasurer to see to it that all required data and information needed in the supporting documents shall be completely filled up before processing the transaction for payment.
