observations.managementAction: Management did not submit explanation and/or explanation on the splitting of requisition in the procurement of equipment rentals.
observations.reason: A Notice of Disallowance No. 16-001-100(16) has been issued on said transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to persons who still has an outstanding balances urging settlement of their outstanding balances and request write-off of the balances for the other receivables that cannot be substantiated after exhausting all possible means and efforts.
observations.managementAction: Management has sent demand letters to various recipients for the account receivables.
observations.reason: Partial payments/refunds were made in the amount ₱2,350.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned accountable officers to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor a request for relief from accountability on properties lost/destroyed by fire, duly supported with all the required documentations, so that they can be relieved from any liability, and the cost of the properties could be dropped from the books of account of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Request for relief from accountability has been submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Additional requirements were requested for the request of relief.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make appropriate revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations
observations.managementAction: The total appropriations used as base in the computation of the GAD Plan and Budget does not include the revenue and receipts from the local economic enterprises, such as the Market Operations, the Slaughterhouse Operations and the Municipal Waterworks Systems. Revision will be made in 2021 budget.
observations.reason: Computation for the Annual GAD Plan and Budget has to be 5% from the total approved appropriations of the local government unit. (Reiterated in Finding No. 14)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the current status of the debtors-NGOs/POs and assess the collectability of the outstanding receivables. Furthermore, we recommend that demand letters be sent to each debtors/NGOs/POs requiring immediate liquidation of the funds transferred in order to evaluate whether or not the purposes therefor were successfully attained.
observations.managementAction: Management has sent demand letters to various recipients for the account receivables.
observations.reason: Partial payments/refunds were made in the amount ₱49,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require all officials and employees concerned to submit immediately the liquidation reports of their outstanding cash advances and/or refund the unexpended cash balance, if any, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them and strictly enforce that no additional cash advance shall be granted until full liquidation of the previous cash advances is made. 

We also recommend that Management require the former municipal officials to make follow-up or send tracers for the issuance of Credit Advice from the COA Central Office and monitor the liquidation of the outstanding balance of the Confidential/Intelligence Fund.
observations.managementAction:
Liquidation reports for the Confidential and Intelligence Fund have been submitted to the COA Central Office.
 
The Municipal Accountant has sent demand letters to persons liable on the other outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Credit Notices from COA Central Office for the Confidential Fund has not been received by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce a more vigorous and effective tax campaign to attain 100% accomplishment or more in the collection of its targeted income for the year, so that identified programs, projects and activities can be fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2020, collection of revenues is strictly monitored and enforced. However, due to the COVID-19 pandemic, collections were minimal.
observations.reason: In CY 2020, the collection of revenue is still lower.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Finance Committee expedite the updating and amendment of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code in pursuance to Section 191 of R.A. 7160 to provide additional revenue sources to fund the municipality’s developmental projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: In 2020, a joint session between Sangguniang Bayan and the Department Heads has already been conducted for the initial deliberation on the amendments of the Local Revenue Code.
observations.reason: Copy of the updated Local Revenue Code was not yet submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.
observations.managementAction: Required persons liable to immediately settle their disallowances.
observations.reason: Partial settlement were made on disallowances amounting to ₱743,038.90.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, initiate the preparation of stock/property cards for new purchases.

We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory and Disposal Committee has been created by management.
observations.reason: Physical inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment is on-going. (Reiterated in Finding No. 5)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit written explanation/justifications on the causes of delay in the projects’ completion. We further recommend that henceforth, Management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.
observations.managementAction: Out of the five (5) infrastructure projects, only one (1) project has not been completed as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: Only the Construction of Water System and Sanitary Facilities at Schools, LGU-Mabini, Bohol (26 schools) has not been completed with a 95.53% accomplishment as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct all accounting office personnel to assist the current municipal accountant in the identification of the receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has identified the identity of the debtor-organizations and have sent demand letters to debtor-organizations/recipients.
observations.reason:
List and names of debtor-organizations/recipients has been identified.

Demand letters were sent to some identified recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Supply Officer Designate to prepare the Inventory an Inspection Report of Unserviceable/Unused Properties and Equipment and facilitate the immediate disposal thereof in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties was submitted on February 20, 2019.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties was not supported with complete documentation.

Disposal of these unserviceable properties were not yet done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit written sufficient justification in pursuing the conduct of a fund raising activity despite the absence of a resolution from the Local Sanggunian authorizing the collection of fees, otherwise personnel involved thereto will be made to answer accordingly.   

We also recommend that Management refrain from conducting similar activities and charging fees without the approval and authority from the Local Sanggunian pursuant to Section 132 of the Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: No fund raising activity was conducted in CY 2019.
observations.reason: Management has not submitted any written justification in the conduct of the fund raising activity as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and the related DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the prompt and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the implementation of priority development projects in order to optimally attain the socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of projects under the 20% Development Fund was hampered due to lack of personnel in the Engineering Department to prepare the Plans and Program of Works. | Management has employed one (1) additional personnel to help in the preparation of the Plans and Program of Works for infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the municipality fast tract the development of a Sanitary Landfill/Dumpsite as a final garbage disposal site of the local government unit for the protection of the health and environment hazards of its constituents.

We further recommend that Management activate its Solid Waste Management Board to ensure the viability and effective implementation of the Local Government Ecological Waste Management Plan as required under Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management (MESWM) 10-Year Plan of the Municipality of Mabini was approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) through NSWMC Resolution No. 1133, Series of 2018, dated October 29, 2018. | Management will negotiate with the DECS for the use of a lot owned by the municipality located within the vicinity of the school compound for the Ecological Waste Facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.
observations.managementAction:
Management and the Vice-Mayor’s League of the Philippines has not submitted any justification as to the legal basis on the release and receipt of financial aid. | It was agreed upon during the conference of LGS COA officials and the Union of Local Authorities of the Philippines (ULAP) that official receipts of the leagues were allowed to be used in acknowledgement of payments made by its members until stock on hand were all issued.

To refer/raise this issue to higher authorities of the League.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the LMP, VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the Section 264 of Republic Act No. 8424 or the National Internal Revenue Code of the Philippines. 

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer not to accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.
observations.managementAction: It was agreed upon during the conference of LGS COA officials and the Union of Local Authorities of the Philippines (ULAP) that official receipts of the leagues were allowed to be used in acknowledgement of payments made by its members until stock on hand were all issued.
observations.reason: To refer/raise this issue to higher authorities of the League.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the OIC Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge and record the wages of the contractual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services.

We also recommend that management limit the hiring of casuals/job order employees for intermittent, emergency jobs or specific piece of work with short duration as guided by CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and maximize the services of the permanent employees for regular agency functions.
observations.managementAction:
Management has directed the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to record wages of casuals/job order employees to its appropriate account. 
 
Stream-lining of employees is also highly considered in order to observe austerity measures.
observations.reason: Wages of job order employees assigned in various departments were charged to Personnel Services except for a few, and those that were assigned in the field were charged to the project funds.

observations.recommendation:
Use the funds received in accordance with the project proposals on a timely manner to maximize the beneficial use of government projects.

Effect the proper liquidation to the concerned agencies and remit the remaining     balances of the funds downloaded after the completion of the project unless these are not required to be returned to the trustor.

Review all balances specifically the excess funds for fully implemented projects.  Once ascertained, return the excess to source agencies or request authority to retain the amount for other purposes.
observations.managementAction: Request for realignment on some financial assistance were sent to donor agencies.
observations.reason: Remaining balances of unused funds after implementation of the desired projects were not yet return to source agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management should:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry voucher to take up the cancellation of the stale checks. Likewise, direct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the stale checks and to submit the original copies of the cancelled checks.

b) Require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer towards the implementation of the proper recording and reporting of supplies and materials to promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.

c) Provide additional information to be presented on the notes to financial statements which must be concise but briefly discussed using a narrative and descriptive formats, taking into consideration that the effects of financial transactions of significant amount. To ensure that the same is clearly understandable to its interested users, disclosure of such relevant information must be duly supported with corroborating schedules that must be consistent with the reported balances shown in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Government Equity.

d) Require the Municipal Account to submit Financial Statements per Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office already prepared journal entry voucher to record the cancellation of the stale checks during the year.












Additional information and disclosures were already provided by the management in the Notes to Financial Statements which has relevant effects in the financial statements for CY 2019.








Submission of Financial Statements per fund was already complied by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

a.) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of PPE to determine the actual existence of all assets owned by the Municipality; and to furnish the Accounting Office with copies of reports on PPEs which were already waste, and those PPE’s whose control of the risks and rewards are no longer in the municipality.

b.) Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to (i) maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger   Card   (PPELC)   for   each   category   of   plant,   property   and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards (PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same (ii) reconcile their records for PPE accounts and determine the causes of the variance thereof and cause proper disposition; (ii) identify all PPE items without ARE and the employees accountable for the items to pave the way for the issuance of the ARE to all employees/officials concerned.

c.) Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee created conducted partially the physical count of its PPE during the year.



The Accounting Office already installed a system to generate the PPELC but they have not yet started  recording their PPE acounts in the system. On the other hand, The GSO had not started maintaining the Property Card for the PPE.





Management already created the Appraisal and Disposal Committee during the year.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II of this audit report.


Management claimed that this is due to time constraints but committed to complete an inventory count on a particular category of their PPE this CY 2020.



The Accounting Office asserts that recording of PPEs on the PPELC requires ample time. However, they committed to record all their PPEs  on PPELC in CY 2020 and reconcile the same with the GSO records when they start maintaining their Property Cards in CY 2020. 



The Appraisal and Disposal Committee have not submitted any report on their unserviceable properties during the year.

observations.recommendation:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to fully recognize the RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of tax payers’ amount due and collectible for the year to be prepared by the Municipal Treasurer in order to record in the books not the mere income estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes and to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statements. 

2. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile regularly the accounting and treasury records to verify the accuracy and validity of their records based on certified list of taxpayers and immediately provide corrective action for discrepancies noted.

3. Advice the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to consider purchasing a computer software to expedite the treasury processes and explore possible option for an enhanced operational quality of its recording system that readily provides report required upon demand by the oversight agencies.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office already recognized the RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of tax payers’ amount due and collectible for the year.





During the year, the Accounting Office and the Treasury Office had reconciled their records regularly.






The management had purchased computer software-the E-Tracks System- for the recording and control of transactions pertaining to collections of RPT/SET receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to determine and reclassify the actual amounts that are long overdue or beyond 1 year as non-current assets and to take the following actions.

1. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances/receivables and strictly comply with provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting and liquidation of cash advances, otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.

2. Refrain from granting additional cash advances unless previous cash advances are liquidated. Implement withholding of salaries for those who failed to liquidate within the time frame set by regulations.

3. Avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more and where settlement could no longer be ascertained
observations.managementAction:
Management did not act on this recommendation in CY 2019.







Management already implemented the “No additional granting of cash advance without  liquidation of previous cash advance granted” policy.





Management did not avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more.
observations.reason:
This was overlooked by management. However, the Accounting Office is considering withholding of salary of employees who have outstanding cash advances as of 12/31/2019




They have difficulty in locating most of the accountable officers/debtors and its last known addresses because these loans/cash advances were granted several years ago. Vital documents in availing such remedy are also lacking and needs time and financial resources to obtain from other concerned agencies. However,      they were again reminded to avail of the remedy in the writing off of receivables even starting to those receivables belonging to   persons who are dead several years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that the LGU, its BAC and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) meticulously and judiciously plan all of its procurement activities, particularly the procurement of infrastructure projects, taking into consideration the selection of ideal period for its implementation, and prepare the required Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for  consolidation  to  the  Annual  Procurement  Plan  (APP)  in  accordance  with  the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management already acted positively  on this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant concurred with the observation and assured the audit team to withhold the amount from the final billing to the Contractor for the project on Concreting of Core Local Access Road project on March 7, 2019. In addition, he pledged to deduct the remaining amount to be withheld, if any from the final billings of the concerned project/s.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant concurred with the observation and assured the audit team to withhold the amount from the final billing to the Contractor for the project on Concreting of Core Local Access Road project on March 7, 2019. In addition, he pledged to deduct the remaining amount to be withheld, if any from the final billings of the concerned project/s.




Management already withheld the 10% retention money from the final billing to the concerned Contractor for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Planning and Development and Budget offices to ensure that the Projects, Programs and Activities to be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) shall be expressed in specific terms and refrain from using generic or lump-sum appropriation to ensure timely implementation. Likewise, the disbursement should be duly supported with an authority from the Sannguniang Bayan Officials concerned to make these transaction not illegal, otherwise this would be a valid ground for the issuance notice of disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management already specified each project, its equivalent location and corresponding project cost  in their 2019 Annual Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management that accounting and reporting guidelines should be strictly observed.
Justify in writing as to why charges made to the Special Trust Funds for unexpended balance should not be disallowed in audit. Unless the premises found to be meritorious, these shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO partially  complied with the prescribed format of LDRRMFIP since 2019.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO admitted misunderstanding on how to document their LDRRMFIP with the required information using the prescribed format under the prevailing guideline

observations.recommendation: We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for proper recording of unexpended balance of LDRRM funds in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already drawn the journal entry voucher during the year. But for CY 2019 balance of DRRM Fund, the same error is committed by the agency.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office asserted that they cannot immediately transfer the cash from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund the DRRM fund balance at the end of year because there are still unrecorded expenses and unliquidated cash advances that hinders them to establish the accurate amount to be transferred at the end of the year

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMO should comply with the minimum standards for disaster preparedness volume 2 on items marked as either non-operational or non-existing.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO had complied only to a certain level with the National Disaster Preparedness and Mitigation Plan 2011-2028 as prescribed even if there are programs, Projects, and Activities by the office that may be aligned with both Provincial and national Disaster Preparedness Plan. However, status of implementation of related will be subjected to further validation by the audit team once Enhanced Community Quarantine is lifted.
observations.reason: Due to not properly accomplished LDRRMFIP with the required information in form and in content, relevant information may not be readily available as ready reference in planning PPAs intended for disaster preparedness.

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommended that a sufficient budget be set aside for the construction and development of sanitary landfill facility as final disposal site for solid wastes. 



2. Pending the relocation to another area for establishment and operation of sanitary landfill, exhaust all legal remedies necessary to adopt specific measures to implement proper waste reduction/segregation. 



3. Abrupt abandonment of the existing dumpsite should be undertaken the soonest possible time with due consideration on the guidelines prescribed by the DENR. Likewise, ensure operation and design of a desired design of sanitary landfill subject to applicable guidelines mentioned in the same Act and its IRR.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE and MENRO is currently planning to loan sufficient amount in order to finish the construction of at least one chamber in a standard sanitary landfill.




The management exhausted its means to cope with the requirements for environmental compliance but the prevailing conditions still needs  significant improvement.
observations.reason:
The compliance of the Municipality of having a functional sanitary landfill is still pending due to insufficient funds in converting the current ECO-park and utilizing it for the operation of controlled dumpsite in the municipality.


Implementation of the measures for proper segregation of wastes is barred by stubbornness of the waste generators.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to cause the immediate disposal of these accountable forms by destruction in the presence of a Disposal Committee duly witnessed by a COA auditor or his/her authorized representative, subject to the prevailing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management already shredded the obsolete accountable forms duly witnessed by the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Engineer to explain and justify in writing the noted deficiencies and be directed to submit the lacking documentary requirements for further evaluation in audit. Likewise, cause the rectification of the noted deficiencies, where applicable in close coordination with the COA audit team, taking into consideration the guidelines prescribed under prevailing procurement law and auditing rules and regulations.

Re-evaluate the ratio of manpower assigned in the Municipal Engineering Office that is deemed applicable for the implementation of number of projects in a given timeframe. Likewise, require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to conduct close monitoring of the projects in accordance with approved development plans.
observations.managementAction: The management had already acted on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Planning and Development and Budget offices to ensure effective and efficient implementation of Projects, Programs and Activities to be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to maximize the benefits due to its beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The management was able to establish a Project Monitoring Committee and had managed to prepare the Program of Works required for Projects in the CY 2019 given the number of projects reduced to certain level in order to prioritize the loan payment.
observations.reason: Some projects were either partially/remained unimplemented due to limited number of staff and numerous programs of work to be prepared in the Muncipal Engineer’s Office, including those projects funded by National Government Funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a) Integrate in the financial reports the qualitative characteristics such as reliability and comparability, in order to be useful to wide range of decision makers;


b) Provide only relevant but sufficient information, may it be narrative and descriptive and/or corroborating schedules in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and

c) Ensure that the submitted reports are free from substantial inconsistencies that undermine reliability of information.
observations.managementAction:
The deficiencies noted on the 2017-2018 Financial Statements were already rectified but there are still deficiencies prevailing in the 2019 Financial Statements.




The noted deficiencies  were already rectified in the 2019 Financial Statements as discussed in the preceding audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:

a. Strictly comply with the provision of Section 22 (c) of RA 7160.

b. Inform and as much as possible, require the Municipal Development Council to propose projects for implementation that are specific and sufficiently detailed. 

c. In cases where there are projects, or goods and services mentioned in generic terms, properly secure the required authorization/
approval of the Sangguniang Bayan Council before entering into any contract.
observations.managementAction:
For the current year, there are still projects expressed in generic terms with lump-sum appropriation where authorization/
approval of the Sangguniang Bayan Council were not secured.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR.

observations.managementAction:
The Office In-charge 
is conducting periodic information and education campaign on waste management  best practices especially on the proper operation of respective MRFs of the barangays. They claimed they had encouraged the LSWMB to monitor the proper implementation of proper waste management in their respective barangays. At present, however, the MENRO in close coordination with the Barangays had been implementing a strict observance of no segregation no garbage collection policy.




The management is still operating an open dumpsite.
observations.reason:
It can be assessed from their claims that there is a need set new objectives which can address the current issues besetting their locality, thus review and eventual revision of the solid waste management plan of the Municipality is highly required for this purpose.




Lack of sufficient funds for the construction and maintenance of the required sanitary landfill as well as the stubbornness of the waste generators hinders the implementation of controlled dumpsite and its related activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

a. Exert more effort in observing the timely implementation of the PPAs.

b. Monitor the status of the implementation of the projects especially those PPAs enumerated in the AIP.

c. Revise the AIP as often as necessary especially when for those PPAs which could not be implemented during the year. This should be brought up in a meeting with the Municipal Development Council so that appropriate actions could be done.
observations.managementAction:
Management was not able to implement these projects as at yearend. 



For CY 2018, there are still capital outlay projects that remained unimplemented also as at yearend.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

a. Implement Section 296(a) of the RRC on dealing with the delinquent market vendors for the timely collection of market rentals and to prevent the eventual increase of uncollected surcharges and interests.

b. To execute the provisions of Section 301 in awarding the declared vacant market stall through the adjudication process. The same process is expected to be also applied when there is transfer of rights over the market stall from previous occupant to the new one.



c. Revisit the steps/procedures on the monitoring of the market operations especially on the collection of monthly rentals and how to ensure that the surcharges and interests already incurred by the vendors will be collected. Determine the deficiencies noted and take necessary steps on how to improve the monitoring and collection system.


d. Stop the practice of allowing the original lessees of market lots to sub-lease their constructed buildings to other business operators.
observations.managementAction:
The management admitted they had imposed the penalty for late payments in the last quarter of CY 2018 and some delinquent vendors requested deferment of payments of monthly rentals to be paid on renewal of their business permits on CY 2019. 

There was no transfer of rights transpired  for the market stalls for CY 2018.

They tried to enforce some procedures in attempting to increase on collection of market rentals but still fell short of their expectation.

The management is implementing its policy that the delinquent  account is paid before giving of clearance as a requirement in the renewal of the business permits.




For effective monitoring of collection, the management requested to re-organize and assign personnel from the office of the treasury, to focus on the collection of lot and stall rentals, since the Economic.Enterprise Office is under the Treasurer’s Office’s jurisdiction.
observations.reason:
Management consented to vendors’ personal consideration
and due to lack of personnel.

In the Calendar Year 2017, the management had not yet implemented the Section 296 of the RRC on dealing with delinquent market vendors due to no proper turnover on how the collection process should be made since the newly designated personnel started to report in the Public Market. Likewise it had not imposed the said Revenue. However, assurance was made by management for full disclosure on information in publicity in CY 2019.

The owner of the buildings and the sub-lease had their internal arrangement to transaction as to the building without informing /formal notice to the Econ. Enterprise Manager. The Econ.Enterprose. Manager only knew when the buildings are run and operated by another person.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to remit on time the shares of the Provincial Government on the additional 1% tax on real property accruing to the Special Education Fund so that the Provincial Government can timely utilize the collections to finance various projects, programs, and activities beneficial to the constituents of the Province of Compostela Valley. 

Observe religiously the deadline set for the remittance of provincial shares which falls on every 15th day of the following month as mandated.
observations.managementAction: The management had made their remittance  before deadline started only on July 2019
observations.reason: This was not properly monitored during the year, but they assured that they will not incur any delay in their remittance in the  CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly follow the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004, particularly on the use of tarpaulin signboards in accordance with the prescribed specifications, and the posting of the required information on such signboards.
observations.managementAction: Tarpaulins were placed on the project site but complete information regarding programs/projects/ activities (PPAs) was not provided in all instances by the management.
observations.reason: The management have not closely monitored the implementation with regards to the requirements on the information and publicity of programs/ projects/ activities pursuant to the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004. However, assurance was made by management for full disclosure on information in publicity in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
Avoid the practice of delaying remittances of the various trust liabilities since these are budgeted and included in the annual appropriations of the municipality. 
 
Instruct the personnel-in-charge of the trust liability accounts to conduct monthly reconciliation of same to determine the correct amounts that should be remitted within the prescribed period provided in the applicable rules and regulations so that the concerned government agencies could utilize the funds due them for their programmed activities and incurrence of penalties, interest and surcharges by the municipality relative thereto could be avoided. 

If there are penalties and surcharges incurred for late remittances, the same should not be charged against the funds of the municipality instead it should be treated as personal liability of the personnel responsible for the delay.
observations.managementAction:
For CY 2019, the management still incurred delays in their remittance to BIR and GSIS. 



Even after remittance, balances  representing unremitted  deductions is still appearing in the subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II of this AAR.



The concerned accountable officers still refuse to settle their obligations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management review the operation of the swine breeding center and formulate procedures including the use and/or disposal of the municipality’s share of the offspring, as guided by Title VI of RA 7160.
observations.reason: No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management-
Require Mr. Gaudioso T. Libres, the designated Caretaker of the Swine Breeding Center of the municipality, to submit valid written justification on disposing three (3) government-owned pigs in violation to Section 356 of RA 7160 and Title V of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management. 

Require the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Auditor the memorandum of agreement entered into by the Department of Agriculture, the municipality and Mr. Silverio T. Miano, for verification.

Require the former Officer-in-Charge of the Office of the Municipal Agriculture, Mr. Aguirico T. Bernales to submit valid written justification on the failure of his Office to monitor and supervise the proper implementation of the Dispersal Program 

Conduct re-evaluation on the reasonableness of the weight on each pig as declared, taking into consideration their ages/number of months as reflected in the Animal Release Orders and require immediate remittance of the proceeds to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for proper accounting and recording of government funds.

We further recommend that Management require the refund from the persons determined to be accountable and liable for the amount of losses incurred in the improper disposal of government-owned live stocks pursuant to Section 377 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 105 of Presidential Decree 1445, without prejudice to the filing of administrative and/or criminal case, if warranted.
observations.reason: No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management conduct physical inventory count of the Work/Zoo Animals recorded in the books and to submit to the Auditor the report on the physical count thereof not later than January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.  Likewise, we further recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) as provided under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.reason: No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management refrain from incurring expenditures for the Organic Demonstration Farm until an ordinance regulating the overall operations of the economic enterprise shall have been crafted and passed by the Local Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: Management has not acted on the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The municipality has not crafted an ordinance to regulate the overall operations of the Organic Demonstration Farm, a local economic enterprise.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.
observations.managementAction: Management and the Vice-Mayor’s League of the Philippines has not submitted any justification as to the legal basis on the release and receipt of financial aid.
observations.reason:
It was agreed upon during the conference of LGS COA officials and the Union of Local Authorities of the Philippines (ULAP) that official receipts of the leagues were allowed to be used in acknowledgement of payments made by its members until stock on hand were all issued.

To refer/raise this issue to higher authorities of the League.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the LMP, VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the Section 264 of Republic Act No. 8424 or the National Internal Revenue Code of the Philippines. 

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer not to accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.
observations.managementAction:
Official Receipts of the League were being issued in acknowledgement of payments made from its members.

To refer/raise this issue to higher authorities of the League.
observations.reason: It was agreed upon during the conference of LGS COA officials and the Union of Local Authorities of the Philippines (ULAP) that official receipts of the leagues were allowed to be used in acknowledgement of payments made by its members until stock on hand were all issued.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit explanation and/or justification why the procurement of equipment rentals were split as discussed.

We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee adheres to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations to ensure that all procurement activities of the municipality are in accord therewith. 

It is also recommended that the management instruct the municipal treasurer to see to it that all required data and information needed in the supporting documents shall be completely filled up before processing the transaction for payment.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit explanation and/or explanation on the splitting of requisition in the procurement of equipment rentals.
observations.reason: A Notice of Disallowance No. 16-001-100(16) has been issued on said transactions and as of 12/31/16, no refund has been made or Motion of Appeal has been filed to the COA Regional Office on this regard.

observations.recommendation:
Municipal officials and employees invited to attend seminars and workshop with a private training institution should ensure that the event is properly authorized by the Department of Interior & Local Government (DILG).

To secure from Bernales Leadership & Research Institute an accreditation of the convention or seminar design or training module by the Local Government Academy.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by management.
observations.reason: Required documents still not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council to formulate projects to be funded by 20% Development Fund in consonance with the requirements prescribed in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 that is geared towards the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental goals that would uplift the quality of life of its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | Management still included some projects which are not within the list of priority projects under the 20% Development Fund during the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board may convene with the Local Development Council in the next budget period and consider additional resources that may be provided for the ultimate establishment of Sanitary Landfill the soonest possible time to prevent potential risk that may endanger the lives of neighboring residents.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | The management had been studying ways on how to comply with the requirements in the establishment and operation of the required sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Currently, only a unit of garbage compactor is operating as well as the lack of sufficient garbage vehicle, thus hindering the full operation of a residual containment area

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to:

1. Coordinate with the SB to pass a resolution revisiting and formulating a doable policies and guidelines on the imposition of fines and penalties, taking into consideration the specific functions and duties and their limitations of the proposed collectors to be identified as enforcers.

2. Provide the adequate supply of resource whether it is in financial terms and adequate technical manpower equipped with skills and able to deliver the required services in support of Community Education and Public Awareness campaign, collections of fees and other fines and penalties and other duties and functions deemed necessary in the operation.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | The management is still considering the need to review their solid waste management plan.
observations.reason: Not Implemented | As assessed by the management, they claimed that the Solid Waste Management Plan needs to be thoroughly reviewed and updated to make it more proactive and applicable. Only after thorough review can policies, processes and procedures meet the intended outcomes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government to prepare and enter into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the recipient beneficiaries of the agricultural, fisheries and seaweed projects under the Bottom-Up Budgeting Program. 

Make sure that the duties and responsibilities of binding parties are clearly defined and established in order to at least ensure the smooth implementation, effectiveness, and sustainability of the projects. 

Stop the practice of not entering into a MOA with the intended beneficiaries when implementing priority programs and projects.

Follow up the delivery of the remaining 16 cattle to ensure the success of the cattle production project. Impose appropriate liquidated damages for the delayed deliveries.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 | Management was not able to prepare Memorandum of Agreements.
observations.reason:
MOA preparation is impractical to prepare at this time due to the following reasons in the implementation of the projects:

1.Corn Production Enhancement-This was not sustained due to long drought that hit at the start of the project.
2.Seaweed Stilt Drier-Not productive due to ice-ice disease.
3.Bangus Production in Marine Fishcage- Not productive because the area is silted that affects its operation.
4. Cattle Production/Re-Stocking- this was not realized due to failure of bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government, the immediate usage of donated lot for the operation and management of Eco-Park with Alternative Technology Approach for its final waste disposal and for the purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes to sustain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan.

Prioritization of the funding for these properties should be immediately done so that the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste would not be hampered.

We recommended also the stoppage of the operation of the present disposal site and the strict implementation of the waste segregation policy of the Municipality from the source, in order to successfully attain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 | For CY 2018, management was not able to utilize another area of their lot for the operation of new controlled dumpsite. The purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes was not also realized due to lack of funds.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct annual
physical inventory of all properties and prepare the required
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) to be reconciled with the accounting records and
submit copy of the same to the auditor concerned not later than
January 31 of each year;
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
maintain complete and updated records to facilitate the
reconciliation of the balances of the Property, Plant and
Equipment with the accounting records; and
c. Ensure compliance with the procedures provided by existing laws,
rules and regulations on property and supply management.

We recommended that the municipal officials concerned demand
from the HLURB the issuance of Official Receipt for the payment of
registration/training fee during the Finalization of the Draft Comprehensive
Land Use Plan / Zoning Ordinance writeshop/workshop to support the
government expenditure incurred to preclude the issuance of Notice of
Suspension.

We recommended that the Management, upon completion of physical
inventory of properties, require the General Services Officer to prepare the
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and
initiate the proper disposal of the unserviceable property in accordance with
the requirements and procedure provided in Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Management cause the immediate retrieval
of all necessary documents of ownership of the lots owned by the
Municipality to facilitate the transfer and registration of ownership with the
Land Registration Authority in accordance with Section 39 of PD 1445 and
Section 449 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I to
establish absolute ownership and protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance
with the provisions of Sections 2.3, 4.1, 4.2 and 5.0 of DILG DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure
that the funds appropriated for development projects are optimally utilized
towards the achievement of the desirable socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes which are beneficial to the constituents of the
Municipality.

We also recommended that the Management require the Municipal
Engineer to submit Program of Works, Detailed Cost Estimates and other
documents necessary to support the recorded transaction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to
the provision of Section 20 of Manual on the New Government Accounting
System by addressing the concern on inadequacy of manpower and by
requiring the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal
Assessor, to maintain Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index
card and duly certified list of taxpayers to provide an accurate basis of the
Municipal Accountant in setting-up of receivables for real property and
special education taxes at the beginning of the year and to facilitate the
reconciliation of accounts for fair presentation of its accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommended that Management should enforce the segregation of
wastes and avoid using the open dumpsite, instead, provide for an
appropriation for the development of sanitary landfill for the disposal of
collected wastes in conformity with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003
and to involve the participation of the barangays in proper disposal of
wastes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance
with Sections 121 and 124, Volume I of MNGAS on the proper accounting of
inventories by directing the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required
records and to immediately prepare/submit the required Report of Supplies
and Materials Issued with the original copies of the Requisition and Issue
Slips for CY 2019 to the Accounting Division/Unit as basis in the
recording/adjustments in the books of accounts.

We also recommended that the Management require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain ledger cards and the Municipal Treasurer to
maintain stock cards, conduct physical count of inventory items and prepare
the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories to be reconciled with the
accounting records.

We recommended that Management advise the Bids and Awards
Committee to prepare and submit to the Head of the Procuring Entity the
Procurement and Monitoring Report and submit the same to the GPPB
pursuant to IRR of RA 9184.

We further recommended that the BAC furnish the audit team copy
of the said report to facilitate monitoring on the status of the procurement
process and to be used as basis for our contract review on the procured
goods and infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management provide a motor pool or well-
secured garage as a measure to protect and safeguard the motor vehicles
owned and maintained by the Municipality.

We recommended that Management exert utmost efforts to meet the
demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts to avoid undue
delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit and rendering of the
required reports in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
personnel concerned of the Municipality to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption and submit the same to the audit team for verification to
ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively
controlled.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC
Chairman and the BAC Secretariat to submit all the necessary documentary
requirements in order to support the said contract and for technical
evaluation of the project to preclude the issuance of notice suspension. We
further recommended that the BAC require the
Contractor to submit Tax Clearance with validity on or before the bidding
date or documents to support the renewal thereof; otherwise, submit
justification why the Tax Clearance submitted by the bidder valid only until
June 27, 2018 was accepted for post qualification of the bids.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Awards Committee to submit the copies of contracts and purchase orders
and all the necessary supporting documents within five (5) days from
perfection thereof as required under Sections 3.1.1 to 3.1.2 of COA Circular
No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer and the Municipal
Budget Officer to ensure strict compliance with the provisions of Section
5.5.1 of NDRRMC–DBM–DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1,
particularly in the utilization of LDRRMF. We further recommended that
Management require the BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat to submit
all the necessary documentary requirements to support the contract and
payment thereof, to avoid issuance of notice of suspension

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance
with the procedures provided by existing laws, rules and regulations on
Local Road Network System and:
a. Create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct physical inventory
of all road networks and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
the Road Network System of the local government unit;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant, the GSO/Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Engineer to maintain and prepare the required
records and reports and to facilitate the recording of the road
networks in the books; and
c. Take appropriate action against the concerned accountable official/s
for non-compliance and repeated unjustified failure to comply with
the requirements pursuant to Section 127 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should state the
functions and duties in the Contract of Service to be performed by the
consultant in the Municipality.

In addition, it is further recommended that the following required
documents be submitted by Consultant concerned and the Municipal
Accountant pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 for
further evaluation of the Audit Team:
a. Accomplishment Reports of the Consultant for the period January
to December 2019; and
b. Copy of reports or outputs submitted by the Consultants to the
Local Chief Executive to support the Accomplishment Reports.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that the
LGU officials and employees concerned exert extra effort to intensify
collection of delinquent stall rentals and real property and adopt the
necessary measures provided in Sections 254 and 256 of Republic Act No.
7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991, to enable the
collections of real property taxes. These will provide additional funds to
support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its
constituents.

Therefore, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Property Officer
maintain property cards that will carry details, such as the property identification,
description, and cost, as well as the date when it is acquired or disposed, independent
from the records of the Accounting Office to preserve the check and balance of the two
Offices with respect to the Municipality’s property and equipment. We likewise
recommend that the latter office maintain their own subsidiary ledger cards for a reliable
reconciliation with results of inventory.

We also recommended and the inventory committee agreed to conduct complete
physical count of its PE or sufficient enough to cover substantially all of the said assets
and to dispose unserviceable properties in accordance with the regulations on disposal.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the:
a. GSO to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and Report on Local
Road Network based on the report submitted by the Municipal Engineer;
b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for all road projects;
c. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road network
systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network
System as of the end of the year; and
d. Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards with complete details on its
components and disclose all the required information for the said property in the Notes
to Financial Statements.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to provide reconciliation on the noted variance.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ascertain the completeness of the real
property units subject of tax, as well as the collections made, posted in its records and
that the Municipal Accountant record receivables gross of discount and the discount only
upon collection.

We recommended the Management to require all Municipal officials, Sangguniang
Bayan Members, and PRAISE Committee who approved and authorized the transaction and the
recipients of the incentive to refund the amount of ₱3,532,500.00.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to require the PRAISE Committee to
strictly observe the basic policies stated in the Agency PRAISE and CSC Memorandum
Circular No. 1 s. 2001, and ensure that the general and specific objectives of the program
were achieved.

Management’s comment:
Recommendation for refund has not been commented upon by the Management as of reporting
date.

a. levy and collect taxes and fees in accordance with the Municipality’s Revenue
Code;
b. require the submission of sworn statement of gross receipts/sales or certified copies
of income tax return so as to come-up with an accurate basis for the computation of
business taxes;
c. demand and disclose all the information regarding the business entities necessary for
 the assessment and collection of taxes and fees; and
d. verify, assess and validate the noted undercollection of ₱60,294.85 and consequently
demand the collection/payment thereof.

We also recommended to provide justification on the non-collection of taxes from certain
operators. Otherwise, assess their dues and collect them accordingly. Absence of
documented action or information to counter the undercollection and non-collection shall
mean the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.

Management’s comment:
Detailed clarification on the non-collection of taxes has not been provided as of reporting date.

Therefore, we recommended the Management to provide explanation on why the
contractors were not required to secure business permits and held liable to pay local taxes
and fees. Otherwise, verify the amount unpaid and collect them accordingly. Absence of
valid and necessary action regarding the matter may result to the issuance of the Notice
of Charge.

Prospectively, we recommended that the BPLO be required to ensure that all entities
conducting business within the Municipality must obtain the required permit and duly pay
the tax and fees prescribed by the Code.

Management’s comment:
Reason why the contractors were not required to secure permits and comments on the
prospective application of the Code’s provision on permits of contractors were not provided as of
the reporting date.

We recommended and the Management agreed to immediately submit the missing
documentary requirements. Otherwise, the amount of ₱117,885.48 may be
suspended/disallowed in audit.

We also recommend and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the completeness of the
documents authenticating the claims for reimbursement prior to its approval and
processing.

Foregoing considered, we recommended and the Management agreed that all official
travels made by the Municipal personnel be supported by properly accomplished Driver’s
Trip Ticket and that the Municipal Accountant ensure the submission and completeness of
the same prior to her approval of payment for the fuel consumption.

Henceforth, we recommended and the Management agreed to come up with a formal and
uniform policy with respect to grant, utilization, manner of payment and monitoring of
telephone allowance so as to address the differences in availing and consuming this
benefit and for everyone to be informed of proper procedures with respect to the matter.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to directly contract with
telecommunication companies for the procurement of basic and reasonable postpaid
plans for its officials, under the name of the agency, so that the monitoring of these
expenses as well as their payment will be more systematic and effective. Consumption of
this allowance through prepaid cards must be put in halt. Further, we strongly advise that
the officials, together with all the employees, utilize the free communication services
brought by different media platforms so as to minimize this expenditure.

Finally, we recommended and they agreed to complete all documentary requirements
necessary for the claims of telephone expenses prior to payment.

We recommended and the Management agreed to refrain officials and employees from
resorting to procurement and disbursement through reimbursement basis and comply
strictly the relevant provisions of RA 9184 pay the suppliers directly by check.

Likewise, we recommended and the Management agreed to improve its planning and
consolidation for the procurement of the agency to minimize frequent but small
purchases leading to payment through reimbursement and prepare the necessary
documents that will support the transactions beforehand and not thereafter.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer submit documents that would show the necessity and public
purpose for which they are incurred, subject to further evaluation. We also recommended
and they agreed to support claims with all the required documentations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to revisit and upgrade the Municipality’s
implementing guidelines on Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation (AICS) program and
separately propose budget for AICS.

We also recommended that the Management adopt clear cut policy for all financial
assistance apart from AICS that will provide standard procedures and ensure that correct
assistance are distributed equally for the benefit of its constituents.

Management’s comment:
Audit observation and recommendation on the adoption of policies for the financial assistance
apart from AICS is still wanting of comments from the Management.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to persons who still has an outstanding balances urging settlement of their outstanding balances and request write-off of the balances for the other receivables that cannot be substantiated after exhausting all possible means and efforts.

We recommend that the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, initiate the preparation of stock/property cards for new purchases.
We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned accountable officers to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor a request for relief from accountability on properties lost/destroyed by fire, duly supported with all the required documentations, so that they can be relieved from any liability, and the cost of the properties could be dropped from the books of account of the municipality.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce a more vigorous and effective tax campaign to attain 100% accomplishment or more in the collection of its targeted income for the year, so that identified programs, projects and activities can be fully implemented.

We recommend that Management send written notice to the contractor, requiring them to immediately finish/complete the project, otherwise, cause the termination of the contract pursuant to Annex “I” of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, confiscate the contractor’s performance bond and withhold the 10% retention moneys and use these funds for the completion of the project.

We recommend that Management require all officials and employees concerned to submit immediately the liquidation reports of their outstanding cash advances and/or refund the unexpended cash balance, if any, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them and strictly enforce that no additional cash advance shall be granted until full liquidation of the previous cash advances is made.
We also recommend that Management require the former municipal officials to make follow-up or send tracers for the issuance of Credit Advice from the COA Central Office and monitor the liquidation of the outstanding balance of the Confidential/Intelligence Fund.

We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 and for the Auditor to refer the same to the Director as required in Section 23 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommend that Management direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee, headed by the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable/Unused Properties and Equipment and facilitate the immediate disposal of these unserviceable assets in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommend that Management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and the related DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the prompt and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the implementation of priority development projects in order to optimally attain the socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality.
We recommend that management immediately return/remit the unexpended balances of the Due to National Agencies account to agencies concerned and secure Official Receipts pertaining thereof.

We recommend that management submit justification on the late submission of the cash advance voucher and supporting documents to COA for post audit. Further, require the Municipal Accountant or his authorized representative to submit all succeeding cash advance vouchers for confidential program/activities within seven (7) working days from release of the check.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Finance Committee expedite the updating and amendment of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code in pursuance to Section 191 of R.A. 7160 to provide additional revenue sources to fund the municipality’s developmental projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the barangay shares from Real Property Tax collections on a quarterly basis as provided for under Section 271(d) of RA 7160.

We recommend that management make appropriate revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Inventory Committee to convene and strategize the conduct of complete physical inventory of PPE to determine the actual existence of all assets owned by the Municipality. They are required  to  submit  the  RPCPPE  on  or  before  January 31  of  each  year,  copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I.
Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.
Require Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to (i) maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger   Card   (PPELC)   for   each   category   of   plant,   property   and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards (PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same (ii) reconcile their records for PPE accounts and determine the causes of the variance thereof and cause proper disposition; (ii) identify all PPE items without ARE and the employees accountable for the items to pave the way for the issuance of the ARE to all employees/officials concerned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Inventory Committee to convene and strategize the conduct of complete physical inventory of PPE to determine the actual existence of all assets owned by the Municipality. They are required  to  submit  the  RPCPPE  on  or  before  January 31  of  each  year,  copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I.
Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.
Require Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to (i) maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger   Card   (PPELC)   for   each   category   of   plant,   property   and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards (PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same (ii) reconcile their records for PPE accounts and determine the causes of the variance thereof and cause proper disposition; (ii) identify all PPE items without ARE and the employees accountable for the items to pave the way for the issuance of the ARE to all employees/officials concerned.

We recommended that management shall:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile and effect the necessary adjusting entries to rectify the discrepancies noted.
Require the officer-in-charge or caretaker of biological assets to (i) maintain a Biological Assets Property Card (BAPC) to account any additions/reductions in the biological assets; (ii) prepare and submit a Quarterly Report of Biological Assets which shall aid the proper recording and reporting of biological assets to promote better accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council to include projects to be funded by 20% Development Fund in full compliance with the requirements as prescribed in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 that is geared towards the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental goals that would uplift the quality of life of its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Management shall require the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to ensure that the necessary information stating the date of acceptance or receipt by the supplier of its commitment to deliver the goods and services are indicated in the purchase orders and other pertinent documents. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that the same must be signed and approved only when the required data are complete to ensure that the supplier will deliver the goods in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract.

We recommended to the management to direct that LDRRMFIP should be properly accomplished with adequate and relevant information integrating the components of the funds, consisting of those held in the Current and Continuing Appropriation in the General Fund, Special Trust Funds and from other sources.

We recommended that the management shall direct the Municipal Accounting Office to require the supplier to provide the necessary warranty security on the procured equipment, either in the form of cash or equivalent to at least 1% of the P74,909,000.00

We recommended the management to direct all the accountable officers to settle and liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances and stop the practice of granting cash advance unless previously granted amount was liquidated.
Send demand letters for immediate settlement of these unliquidated cash advances, and enventually enforce the withholding of salary in case the accountable officer refuses to settle his accounts with the agency.

We recommended that Management shall submit the lacking and other necessary documents relative to the procurement activities and progress payments for audit purposes and final inspection and evaluation of the project to avoid suspension in audit of the project.

Instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to monitor the collection and transporting of wastes by its waste collectors. Ensure compliance of wearing of prescribed outfit during collection of wastes. Direct all the waste collectors to undergo training to be conducted by the MHO for proper handling and disposal of wastes and adequate personal hygiene.

We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer to explain and justify in writing the noted deficiencies and be directed to submit the lacking documentary requirements for further evaluation in audit. Likewise, compel the contractor of the noted deficiencies for a given project, where applicable in close coordination with the COA audit team, taking into consideration the guidelines prescribed under prevailing procurement law and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Planning and Development and Budget offices to install feasible mechanisms and appropriate measures in the planning and monitoring of Projects, Programs and Activities to be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to maximize the benefits due to its beneficiaries.
