Require the Muncipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to conduct a comprehensive and periodic assessment of the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management operation against the LDRRMFIP before its submission to the MDRRMC for approval and cause changes whenever necessary, subject to availability of funds and
Exhaust all means to integrate and apply best practices of other agencies to be considered in the planning and budgeting of the MDRRMF.
Limit the utilization of the LDRRMF to projects and activities which are deemed relevant in  mitigating the risks of disasters.

We recommended that the management (i) direct all responsible officials/implementing unit of the LGU to closely supervise and monitor all project implemented; (ii) conduct an inventory of work accomplished by the contractor and impose liquidated damages at least equal to one-tenth of one percent (0.1%) of the cost of the unperformed portion for every day of delay; (iii) consider the option of blacklisting the contractor from participating in the public bidding process due to his fault or negligence, to mobilize and start work or performance within the specified period in the Notice to Proceed.

Instruct the personnel-in-charge of the remittance to secure the NOSA on a regular basis for the preparation of the required ARA to forestall any delay in its remittances. Ensure that any difference in salary due to step increment or other causes should be made before any remittance is made.
Enjoin the personnel-in-charge to make reconciliation with the cumulative balances which is still due for remittance as soon as possible. Also, all monies being withheld due the GSIS should be immediately remitted for the corporate’s timely usage of funds for the fulfillment of its plans, programs and activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the remittance of the taxes withheld to the Bureau of Internal Revenue and to constantly monitor the monthly status of taxes withheld by the municipal government. Adhere strictly to the provisions in the BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and observe the practice of intact remittance of the monthly obligation to the BIR.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to prepare and submit immediately the GAD AR to the Audit Team for its determination on the extent of implementation of the municipality’s GAD mandates and commitments.
Ensure also to appoint immediately GAD Focal Point person to act immediately on clerical works on the preparation and submission of said report supported by required documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 of DBM and DILG by optimally utilizing the 20% development funds solely for the implementation of projects, programs and activities identified in Section 3.0 of the Joint Memorandum Circular of DBM and DILG.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Budget Officer and the GFPS-TWG Chair to stop from charging expenses that are not related in addressing gender issues, promote women’s empowerment and gender equality as provided in the provisions of the Joint Circular of PCW-NEDA-DBM; and to charge the unrelated expenses to the regular Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit the monthly sources and utilization of the LDRRMF in compliance with the Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management allocate funds in the annual budget for the processing of the Torrens Title for all lots owned by the municipality. We further recommend that Management designate a personnel to work on the titling of these land.
observations.managementAction: Land Titles for seven (7) lots were already approved in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit a written explanation on the necessity of the above-listed disbursements and discontinue the incurrence of excessive and unnecessary catering services for meals and snacks. If found unwarranted, a corresponding disallowance will be issued.
observations.managementAction: Catering services were incurred only during seminars, trainings and other official activities

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the LDRRM Officer to promptly submit the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Report on the Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) were duly submitted to COA monthly.

observations.recommendation:
We require Management to submit a written explanation as to why claims of contractors on progress billings for infrastructure projects were paid despite the non-preparation of a duly verified and certified Statement of Work Accomplished.

We further recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims from contractors on infrastructure projects without the preparation and attachment of a duly certified Statement of Work Accomplished in order to ensure that the total amount claimed and paid, really correspond with the actual accomplishments of the project.
observations.managementAction: Statement of Work Accomplished on the two (2) projects were duly accomplished and submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the municipal officials concerned to adhere with the prescribed period in the submission of financial and other reports. Failure to submit these reports on time shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries pursuant to Sections 122 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: These reports were duly submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to coordinate and conduct meeting with the different LGU offices and national government agencies involved in the implementation of the planned GAD programs and activities; make an evaluation as to the clear-cut causes of the non-implementation of these projects and; formulate strategies in order to ensure the complete implementation of GAD programs and activities in the future.
observations.managementAction: All activities and projects programmed under the GAD in CY 2019 were implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management adhere to the provisions prescribed by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017, amending the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security in the procurement of goods, in order to protect the interest of the government. 

We further recommend that future procurements be deducted with the one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) retention money or require a special bank guarantee which is equivalent to one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the total contract price, otherwise, expenses due to the defects of the goods within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials approving the payment.
observations.managementAction: All procurement of motor vehicles were covered with warranty security.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management appropriate one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment of the municipality for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the Local Council for the Protection of Children.  Also, prepare and submit the necessary reports enumerated in Items No. 1 of Part II of the above-quoted DILG Memorandum Circular for the proper and effective implementation of programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2019, the LGU appropriated the amount of ₱946,770.98 and utilized the amount of  ₱741,228.21 for the Local Council for the Protection of Children.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization and implementation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are duly implemented. We also recommend that management faithfully adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely review of the required LDRRM reports.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2019 of LDRRMF Plan and Budget and the Monthly Reports of the Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund were already submitted to COA on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to persons who still has an outstanding balances urging settlement of their outstanding balances and request write-off of the balances for the other receivables that cannot be substantiated after exhausting all possible means and efforts.
observations.managementAction: Management has sent demand letters to various recipients for the account receivables.
observations.reason: Partial payments/refunds were made in the amount ₱2,350.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the current status of the debtors-NGOs/POs and assess the collectability of the outstanding receivables. Furthermore, we recommend that demand letters be sent to each debtors/NGOs/POs requiring immediate liquidation of the funds transferred in order to evaluate whether or not the purposes therefor were successfully attained.
observations.managementAction: Management has sent demand letters to various recipients for the account receivables.
observations.reason: Partial payments/refunds were made in the amount ₱49,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require all officials and employees concerned to submit immediately the liquidation reports of their outstanding cash advances and/or refund the unexpended cash balance, if any, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them and strictly enforce that no additional cash advance shall be granted until full liquidation of the previous cash advances is made. 

We also recommend that Management require the former municipal officials to make follow-up or send tracers for the issuance of Credit Advice from the COA Central Office and monitor the liquidation of the outstanding balance of the Confidential/Intelligence Fund.
observations.managementAction:
Liquidation reports for the Confidential and Intelligence Fund have been submitted to the COA Central Office.
 
The Municipal Accountant has sent demand letters to persons liable on the other outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason:
Credit Notices from COA Central Office for the Confidential Fund has not been received by management.

There were two (2) cash advances for local travels which remained unliquidated as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.
observations.managementAction: Required persons liable to immediately settle their disallowances.
observations.reason: Partial settlement were made on disallowances amounting to ₱743,038.90.

observations.recommendation: Require the inventory committee/team to conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor in accordance with Section of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume 1.  Likewise, reconciliation between the records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should be made and effect necessary adjustments when appropriate.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory and Disposal Committee has been created by management.
observations.reason: Physical inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit a written explanation/justifications on the causes of delay in the projects’ completion. We further recommend that henceforth, Management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.
observations.managementAction: Out of the five (5) infrastructure projects, only one (1) project has not been completed as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: Only the Construction of Water System and Sanitary Facilities at Schools, LGU-Mabini, Bohol (26 schools) has not been completed with a 95.53% accomplishment as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management revisit its procurement process with the end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in every procurement activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184.

We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee practice wide dissemination of bid opportunities, send invitation to independent observers, extend equal opportunity and issue bid documents to all qualified parties to dispel presumptions of rigged biddings and promote transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: All infrastructure projects were procured thru competitive public bidding.
observations.reason: Some projects were still procured and awarded to lone bidder.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct all accounting office personnel to assist the current municipal accountant in the identification of the receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has identified the identity of the debtor-organizations and have sent demand letters to debtor-organizations/recipients.
observations.reason:
List and names of debtor-organizations/recipients has been identified.

Demand letters were sent to some identified recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Office of the Treasurer and the Accounting Section to maintain and update regularly property cards and property ledger cards, and immediately reconcile any variance detected between the accounting and property records.  Moreover, direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine the causes of the discrepancies between the two (2) records to be able to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to present fairly the property, plant and equipment account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
A Physical Inventory and Disposal Committee has been created by management.

RPCPPE is due for submission to COA..
observations.reason: A Physical Inventory and Disposal Committee has been created by the local chief executive.  The Committee has started its physical count of the PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Supply Officer Designate to prepare the Inventory an Inspection Report of Unserviceable/Unused Properties and Equipment and facilitate the immediate disposal thereof in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties was submitted on February 20, 2019.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties was not supported with complete documentation.

Disposal of these unserviceable properties were not yet done.
(This audit observation was reiterated in CY 2019 AAR.)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit written sufficient justification in pursuing the conduct of a fund raising activity despite the absence of a resolution from the Local Sanggunian authorizing the collection of fees, otherwise personnel involved thereto will be made to answer accordingly.   

We also recommend that Management refrain from conducting similar activities and charging fees without the approval and authority from the Local Sanggunian pursuant to Section 132 of the Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: No fund raising activity was conducted in CY 2019.
observations.reason: Management has not submitted any written justification in the conduct of the fund raising activity as recommended..

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and the related DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the prompt and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the implementation of priority development projects in order to optimally attain the socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of projects under the 20% Development Fund was hampered due to lack of personnel in the Engineering Department to prepare the Plans and Program of Works.
observations.reason: Management has employed one (1) additional personnel to help in the preparation of the Plans and Program of Works for infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to comply with the guidelines issued under COA Circular No. 2013-004 relative to the information and publicity of the Programs/Projects/Activities implemented by the municipality particularly the submission of monthly updates and quarterly reports on government projects, and the display of signboards in conspicuous places in every location of the PPAs, irrespective of amount.
observations.managementAction: Billboards/signboards were posted only on big budgeted projects.
observations.reason: Not all on-going projects has billboards/signboards as required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the municipality fast tract the development of a Sanitary Landfill/Dumpsite as a final garbage disposal site of the local government unit for the protection of the health and environment hazards of its constituents.

We further recommend that Management activate its Solid Waste Management Board to ensure the viability and effective implementation of the Local Government Ecological Waste Management Plan as required under Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management (MESWM) 10-Year Plan of the Municipality of Mabini was approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) through NSWMC Resolution No. 1133, Series of 2018, dated October 29, 2018.
observations.reason: Management will negotiate with the DECS for the use of a lot owned by the municipality located within the vicinity of the school compound for the Ecological Waste Facility.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the OIC Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge and record the wages of the contractual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services.

We also recommend that management limit the hiring of casuals/job order employees for intermittent, emergency jobs or specific piece of work with short duration as guided by CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and maximize the services of the permanent employees for regular agency functions.
observations.managementAction:
Management has directed the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to record wages of casuals/job order employees to its appropriate account. 
 
Stream-lining of employees is also highly considered in order to observe austerity measures.
observations.reason: Wages of job order employees assigned in various departments were charged to Personnel Services except for a few, and those that were assigned in the field were charged to the project funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to furnish copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders with complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval. Any unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to comply shall be subjected to the administrative disciplinary action under the penalty clause in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Submission on copy of contracts and its supporting documents has been made to COA but not within the five (5) days after their perfection/issuance.
observations.reason: Management have submitted perfected contracts to COA but not within the five (5) working days for economy reasons considering the distance of the municipality to COA Office which is more or less 100 kilometers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit justification in writing on why financial assistance under the AICS Program were granted to clients despite the absence of supporting documents as enumerated in Section 4.2  of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 15, Series of 2014.  

Also, direct the Municipal Social Welfare Officer to submit the records of the cases and all other basic supporting documents relative to the claims for further evaluation, otherwise the transactions will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management directed all persons liable/responsible to refund the whole amount of financial assistance granted.
observations.reason:
Refund has been taking place on installment basis. 

Total amount refunded as of December 31, 2019 is ₱353,950.00.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Management require all officials and personnel who have participated in the Benchmarking Activity to refund the amount so received, otherwise the officials who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable.
observations.managementAction: Motion for Reconsideration dated May 30, 2016 was filed at the Office of the Regional Director on the Notice of Disallowance No. 15-001-100(15) amounting to ₱1,333,000.00.
observations.reason:
Reconsideration was not taken cognizant by the COA Regional Office because it is not allowed under Section 9, Rule IV of the 2009 Revised Rules, and Procedures of the COA (RRPC), however, the same was returned to the appellants per letter dated August 1, 2016 with the advice to file an Appeal Memorandum pursuant to Section 2 of Rule V of the 20.

Partial refund of persons liable has been taking place on installment basis.  As of December 31, 2019, the total amount refunded is ₱389,088.90.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management should:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry voucher to take up the cancellation of the stale checks. Likewise, direct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the stale checks and to submit the original copies of the cancelled checks.

b) Require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer towards the implementation of the proper recording and reporting of supplies and materials to promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.

c)	Provide additional information to be presented on the notes to financial statements which must be concise but briefly discussed using a narrative and descriptive formats, taking into consideration that the effects of financial transactions of significant amount. To ensure that the same is clearly understandable to its interested users, disclosure of such relevant information must be duly supported with corroborating schedules that must be consistent with the reported balances shown in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Government Equity.

d)	Require the Municipal Account to submit Financial Statements per Fund.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2019 | The Accounting Office already prepared journal entry voucher to record the cancellation of the stale checks during the year.
















Additional information and disclosures were already provided by the management in the Notes to Financial Statements which has relevant effects in the financial statements for CY 2019.
















Submission of Financial Statements per fund was already complied by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

a.) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of PPE to determine the actual existence of all assets owned by the Municipality; and to furnish the Accounting Office with copies of reports on PPEs which were already waste, and those PPE’s whose control of the risks and rewards are no longer in the municipality.

b.) Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to (i) maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger   Card   (PPELC)   for   each   category   of   plant,   property   and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards (PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same (ii) reconcile their records for PPE accounts and determine the causes of the variance thereof and cause proper disposition; (ii) identify all PPE items without ARE and the employees accountable for the items to pave the way for the issuance of the ARE to all employees/officials concerned.

c.) Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee created conducted partially the physical count of its PPE during the year. 




The Accounting Office already installed a system to generate the PPELC but they have not yet started  recording their PPE acounts in the system. On the other hand, The GSO had not started maintaining the Property Card for the PPE.





Management already created the Appraisal and Disposal Committee during the year.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II of this audit report.




Management claimed that this is due to time constraints but committed to complete an inventory count on a particular category of their PPE this CY 2020.




The Accounting Office asserts that recording of PPEs on the PPELC requires ample time. However, they committed to record all their PPEs  on PPELC in CY 2020 and reconcile the same with the GSO records when they start maintaining their Property Cards in CY 2020. 




The Appraisal and Disposal Committee have not submitted any report on their unserviceable properties during the year.

observations.recommendation:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to fully recognize the RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of tax payers’ amount due and collectible for the year to be prepared by the Municipal Treasurer in order to record in the books not the mere income estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes and to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statements. 

2. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile regularly the accounting and treasury records to verify the accuracy and validity of their records based on certified list of taxpayers and immediately provide corrective action for discrepancies noted.

3. Advice the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to consider purchasing a computer software to expedite the treasury processes and explore possible option for an enhanced operational quality of its recording system that readily provides report required upon demand by the oversight agencies.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office already recognized the RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of tax payers’ amount due and collectible for the year.




During the year, the Accounting Office and the Treasury Office had reconciled their records regularly.





The management had purchased computer software-the E-Tracks System- for the recording and control of transactions pertaining to collections of RPT/SET receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to determine and reclassify the actual amounts that are long overdue or beyond 1 year as non-current assets and to take the following actions.

1. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances/receivables and strictly comply with provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting and liquidation of cash advances, otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.

2. Refrain from granting additional cash advances unless previous cash advances are liquidated. Implement withholding of salaries for those who failed to liquidate within the time frame set by regulations.

3. Avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more and where settlement could no longer be ascertained
observations.managementAction:
Management did not act on this recommendation in CY 2019.






Management already implemented the “No additional granting of cash advance without  liquidation of previous cash advance granted” policy.




Management did not avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more.
observations.reason:
This was overlooked by management. However, the Accounting Office is considering withholding of salary of employees who have outstanding cash advances as of 12/31/2019





They have difficulty in locating most of the accountable officers/debtors and its last known addresses because these loans/cash advances were granted several years ago. Vital documents in availing such remedy are also lacking and needs time and financial resources to obtain from other concerned agencies. However,      they were again reminded to avail of the remedy in the writing off of receivables even starting to those receivables belonging to   persons who are dead several years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that the LGU, its BAC and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) meticulously and judiciously plan all of its procurement activities, particularly the procurement of infrastructure projects, taking into consideration the selection of ideal period for its implementation, and prepare the required Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for  consolidation  to  the  Annual  Procurement  Plan  (APP)  in accordance  with  the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | Management already acted positively  on this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant concurred with the observation and assured the audit team to withhold the amount from the final billing to the Contractor for the project on Concreting of Core Local Access Road project on March 7, 2019. In addition, he pledged to deduct the remaining amount to be withheld, if any from the final billings of the concerned project/s.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | Management already withheld the 10% retention money from the final billing to the concerned Contractor for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Planning and Development and Budget offices to ensure that the Projects, Programs and Activities to be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) shall be expressed in specific terms and refrain from using generic or lump-sum appropriation to ensure timely implementation. Likewise, the disbursement should be duly supported with an authority from the Sannguniang Bayan Officials concerned to make these transaction not illegal, otherwise this would be a valid ground for the issuance notice of disallowance.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | Management already specified each project, its equivalent location and corresponding project cost  in their 2019 Annual Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management that accounting and reporting guidelines should be strictly observed.
Justify in writing as to why charges made to the Special Trust Funds for unexpended balance should not be disallowed in audit. Unless the premises found to be meritorious, these shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | The MDRRMO partially  complied with the prescribed format of LDRRMFIP since 2019.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO admitted misunderstanding on how to document their LDRRMFIP with the required information using the prescribed format under the prevailing guideline

observations.recommendation: We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for proper recording of unexpended balance of LDRRM funds in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | The Accounting Office already drawn the journal entry voucher during the year. But for CY 2019 balance of DRRM Fund, the same error is committed by the agency.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office asserted that they cannot immediately transfer the cash from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund the DRRM fund balance at the end of year because there are still unrecorded expenses and unliquidated cash advances that hinders them to establish the accurate amount to be transferred at the end of the year

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMO should comply with the minimum standards for disaster preparedness volume 2 on items marked as either non-operational or non-existing.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | The MDRRMO had complied only to a certain level with the National Disaster Preparedness and Mitigation Plan 2011-2028 as prescribed even if there are programs, Projects, and Activities by the office that may be aligned with both Provincial and national Disaster Preparedness Plan. However, status of implementation of related will be subjected to further validation by the audit team once Enhanced Community Quarantine is lifted.
observations.reason: Due to not properly accomplished LDRRMFIP with the required information in form and in content, relevant information may not be readily available as ready reference in planning PPAs intended for disaster preparedness.

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommended that a sufficient budget be set aside for the construction and development of sanitary landfill facility as final disposal site for solid wastes. 



2. Pending the relocation to another area for establishment and operation of sanitary landfill, exhaust all legal remedies necessary to adopt specific measures to implement proper waste reduction/segregation. 



3. Abrupt abandonment of the existing dumpsite should be undertaken the soonest possible time with due consideration on the guidelines prescribed by the DENR. Likewise, ensure operation and design of a desired design of sanitary landfill subject to applicable guidelines mentioned in the same Act and its IRR.
observations.managementAction:
AR 
CY 2018 | The LCE and MENRO is currently planning to loan sufficient amount in order to finish the construction of at least one chamber in a standard sanitary landfill.




The management exhausted its means to cope with the requirements for environmental compliance but the prevailing conditions still needs  significant improvement.
observations.reason:
The compliance of the Municipality of having a functional sanitary landfill is still pending due to insufficient funds in converting the current ECO-park and utilizing it for the operation of controlled dumpsite in the municipality.


Implementation of the measures for proper segregation of wastes is barred by stubbornness of the waste generators.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to cause the immediate disposal of these accountable forms by destruction in the presence of a Disposal Committee duly witnessed by a COA auditor or his/her authorized representative, subject to the prevailing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | The management already shredded the obsolete accountable forms duly witnessed by the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Engineer to explain and justify in writing the noted deficiencies and be directed to submit the lacking documentary requirements for further evaluation in audit. Likewise, cause the rectification of the noted deficiencies, where applicable in close coordination with the COA audit team, taking into consideration the guidelines prescribed under prevailing procurement law and auditing rules and regulations.

Re-evaluate the ratio of manpower assigned in the Municipal Engineering Office that is deemed applicable for the implementation of number of projects in a given timeframe. Likewise, require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to conduct close monitoring of the projects in accordance with approved development plans.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | The management had already acted on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Planning and Development and Budget offices to ensure effective and efficient implementation of Projects, Programs and Activities to be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to maximize the benefits due to its beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | The management was able to establish a Project Monitoring Committee and had managed to prepare the Program of Works required for Projects in the CY 2019 given the number of projects reduced to certain level in order to prioritize the loan payment.
observations.reason: Some projects were either partially/remained unimplemented due to limited number of staff and numerous programs of work to be prepared in the Muncipal Engineer’s Office, including those projects funded by National Government Funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a) Integrate in the financial reports the qualitative characteristics such as reliability and comparability, in order to be useful to wide range of decision makers;
 

b) Provide only relevant but sufficient information, may it be narrative and descriptive and/or corroborating schedules in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and

c) Ensure that the submitted reports are free from substantial inconsistencies that undermine reliability of information.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2017 CY | The deficiencies noted on the 2017-2018 Financial Statements were already rectified but there are still deficiencies prevailing in the 2019 Financial Statements.





The noted deficiencies  were already rectified in the 2019 Financial Statements as discussed in the preceding audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:

a. Strictly comply with the provision of Section 22 (c) of RA 7160.

b. Inform and as much as possible, require the Municipal Development Council to propose projects for implementation that are specific and sufficiently detailed. 

c. In cases where there are projects, or goods and services mentioned in generic terms, properly secure the required authorization/
approval of the Sangguniang Bayan Council before entering into any contract.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2017 CY | For the current year, there are still projects expressed in generic terms with lump-sum appropriation where authorization/
approval of the Sangguniang Bayan Council were not secured.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.managementAction:
AAR 2017 CY | The Office In-charge 
is conducting periodic information and education campaign on waste management  best practices especially on the proper operation of respective MRFs of the barangays. They claimed they had encouraged the LSWMB to monitor the proper implementation of proper waste management in their respective barangays. At present, however, the MENRO in close coordination with the Barangays had been implementing a strict observance of no segregation no garbage collection policy.














The management is still operating an open dumpsite.
observations.reason:
It can be assessed from their claims that there is a need set new objectives which can address the current issues besetting their locality, thus review and eventual revision of the solid waste management plan of the Municipality is highly required for this purpose.














Lack of sufficient funds for the construction and maintenance of the required sanitary landfill as well as the stubbornness of the waste generators hinders the implementation of controlled dumpsite and its related activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

a. Exert more effort in observing the timely implementation of the PPAs.

b. Monitor the status of the implementation of the projects especially those PPAs enumerated in the AIP.

c. Revise the AIP as often as necessary especially when for those PPAs which could not be implemented during the year. This should be brought up in a meeting with the Municipal Development Council so that appropriate actions could be done.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2017 CY | Management was not able to implement these projects as at yearend. 




For CY 2018, there are still capital outlay projects that remained unimplemented also as at yearend.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

a. Implement Section 296(a) of the RRC on dealing with the delinquent market vendors for the timely collection of market rentals and to prevent the eventual increase of uncollected surcharges and interests.

b. To execute the provisions of Section 301 in awarding the declared vacant market stall through the adjudication process. The same process is expected to be also applied when there is transfer of rights over the market stall from previous occupant to the new one.





c. Revisit the steps/procedures on the monitoring of the market operations especially on the collection of monthly rentals and how to ensure that the surcharges and interests already incurred by the vendors will be collected. Determine the deficiencies noted and take necessary steps on how to improve the monitoring and collection system.





d. Stop the practice of allowing the original lessees of market lots to sub-lease their constructed buildings to other business operators.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2017 CY | The management admitted they had imposed the penalty for late payments in the last quarter of CY 2018 and some delinquent vendors requested deferment of payments of monthly rentals to be paid on renewal of their business permits on CY 2019. 

There was no transfer of rights transpired  for the market stalls for CY 2018.

They tried to enforce some procedures in attempting to increase on collection of market rentals but still fell short of their expectation.

The management is implementing its policy that the delinquent  account is paid before giving of clearance as a requirement in the renewal of the business permits.





For effective monitoring of collection, the management requested to re-organize and assign personnel from the office of the treasury, to focus on the collection of lot and stall rentals, since the Economic.Enterprise Office is under the Treasurer’s Office’s jurisdiction.
observations.reason:
Management consented to vendors’ personal consideration
and due to lack of personnel.

In the Calendar Year 2017, the management had not yet implemented the Section 296 of the RRC on dealing with delinquent market vendors due to no proper turnover on how the collection process should be made since the newly designated personnel started to report in the Public Market. Likewise it had not imposed the said Revenue. However, they find ways to fix and execute or implement its policy on the imposition surcharges and interest as much as they could in the last CY 2018 up to present.

The same rules is being applied for the new applicant of vacant market stall lessee. The Economic.Enterprise. Manager informed that the deficiency had been corrected and implemented to a certain degree the guidelines for the right processing to own the stall vacant. So far, in the CY 2018 to present, the management undertake the same measures such that the new lessee would pay for the occupancy fee, signing of contract, getting of business permits, signing of waiver to the previous lessee to waive his right and give the favor to the new lessee to avoid conflict in a coming year.

The owner of the buildings and the sub-lease had their internal arrangement to transaction as to the building without informing /formal notice to the Econ. Enterprise Manager. The Econ.Enterprose. Manager only knew when the buildings are run and operated by another person.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to remit on time the shares of the Provincial Government on the additional 1% tax on real property accruing to the Special Education Fund so that the Provincial Government can timely utilize the collections to finance various projects, programs, and activities beneficial to the constituents of the Province of Compostela Valley. 

Observe religiously the deadline set for the remittance of provincial shares which falls on every 15th day of the following month as mandated.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 | The management had made their remittance  before deadline started only on July 2019
observations.reason: This was not properly monitored during the year, but they assured that they will not incur any delay in their remittance in the  CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly follow the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004, particularly on the use of tarpaulin signboards in accordance with the prescribed specifications, and the posting of the required information on such signboards.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 | Tarpaulins were placed on the project site but complete information regarding programs/projects/ activities (PPAs) was not provided in all instances by the management.
observations.reason: The management have not closely monitored the implementation with regards to the requirements on the information and publicity of programs/ projects/ activities pursuant to the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004. However, assurance was made by management for full disclosure on information in publicity in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
Avoid the practice of delaying remittances of the various trust liabilities since these are budgeted and included in the annual appropriations of the municipality. 
 
Instruct the personnel-in-charge of the trust liability accounts to conduct monthly reconciliation of same to determine the correct amounts that should be remitted within the prescribed period provided in the applicable rules and regulations so that the concerned government agencies could utilize the funds due them for their programmed activities and incurrence of penalties, interest and surcharges by the municipality relative thereto could be avoided. 

If there are penalties and surcharges incurred for late remittances, the same should not be charged against the funds of the municipality instead it should be treated as personal liability of the personnel responsible for the delay.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2013 | For CY 2019, the management still incurred delays in their remittance to BIR and GSIS. 




Even after remittance, balances  representing unremitted  deductions is still appearing in the subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
Exhaust all measures to collect receivables from the grantees of credit and financing program even those receivables from NGAS and other receivables.
    
Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue regularly statement of accounts and prompt verification of accounts in the books of the creditors.        Make entries to Prior Year’s Adjustments on the amount pertaining to those accounts which recovery is already impossible due to death or disability fully supported with necessary supporting documents.  

Reclassify and adjust accounts in consideration of unrecorded repayments to reflect the correct balances duly confirmed with the concerned debtors.  

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of receivables using the billing statements from the Accounting Department in order to recover government funds and utilize collection to finance the present thrusts and priorities of the Municipality.
Exhaust all measures to gather the documents pertaining to the receivables and collect from debtors.

There should a meeting between the LGU concerned and the loan recipients , to sit down and confer together in order to discuss matters of collection and payments to finally settle the accounts and the possibility of continuing this financing scheme in a more effective way for the benefit of the LGU and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2011 | The management had already sent demand letters to various accountable officers.
observations.reason: The concerned accountable officers still refuse to settle their obligations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
management:
 Seek for the assistance of
Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to
create a law or ordinance
declaring the two public
utilities as Local Economic
Enterprise as stated in
Section 105, Volume I of
Manual for NGAs for LGUs.
The said law or ordinance
will be the basis of Local
Finance Committee to charge
the administrative costs,
material costs and other
related expenses in
maintenance and
preservation, improvement
and operation of the public
utilities as Local Economic
Enterprise (LEE), not in 20%
DF and;
 Require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain a
separate account for the two
LEE to determine whether
the income generated by the
economic enterprises are
sufficient to meet their
respective operating costs
and to provide adequate
information as to the assets,
liabilities and equity of each
special account as prescribed
in Section 106 of the same
Manual.

Recommendation:

We recommend that Management conduct physical inventory count of the Work/Zoo Animals recorded in the books and to submit to the Auditor the report on the physical count thereof not later than January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.  Likewise, we further recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) as provided under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by management.
observations.reason: No Physical Inventory Report of Work/Zoo Animals was submitted to COA in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that Management refrain from incurring expenditures for the Organic Demonstration Farm until an ordinance regulating the overall operations of the economic enterprise shall have been crafted and passed by the Local Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: Management has not acted on the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The municipality has not crafted an ordinance to regulate the overall operations of the Organic Demonstration Farm, a local economic enterprise.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.
observations.managementAction: Management and the Vice-Mayor’s League of the Philippines has not submitted any justification as to the legal basis on the release and receipt of financial aid.
observations.reason:
It was agreed upon during the conference of LGS COA officials and the Union of Local Authorities of the Philippines (ULAP) that official receipts of the leagues were allowed to be used in acknowledgement of payments made by its members until stock on hand were all issued.

To refer/raise this issue to higher authorities of the League.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that management require the LMP, VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the Section 264 of Republic Act No. 8424 or the National Internal Revenue Code of the Philippines. 

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer not to accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.
observations.managementAction:
Official Receipts of the League were being issued in acknowledgement of payments made from its members.

To refer/raise this issue to higher authorities of the League.
observations.reason: It was agreed upon during the conference of LGS COA officials and the Union of Local Authorities of the Philippines (ULAP) that official receipts of the leagues were allowed to be used in acknowledgement of payments made by its members until stock on hand were all issued.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that management submit explanation and/or justification why the procurement of equipment rentals were split as discussed.

We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee adheres to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations to ensure that all procurement activities of the municipality are in accord therewith. 

It is also recommended that the management instruct the municipal treasurer to see to it that all required data and information needed in the supporting documents shall be completely filled up before processing the transaction for payment.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit explanation and/or explanation on the splitting of requisition in the procurement of equipment rentals.
observations.reason: A Notice of Disallowance No. 16-001-100(16) has been issued on said transactions and as of  12/31/16, no refund has been made or Motion of Appeal has been filed to the COA Regional Office on this regard.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

Municipal officials and employees invited to attend seminars and workshop with a private training institution should ensure that the event is properly authorized by the Department of Interior & Local Government (DILG).

To secure from Bernales Leadership & Research Institute an accreditation of the convention or seminar design or training module by the Local Government Academy.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by management.
observations.reason: Required documents still not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:

a. Strictly comply with the provision of Section 22 (c) of RA 7160.

b. Inform and as much as possible, require the Municipal Development Council to propose projects for implementation that are specific and sufficiently detailed. 

c. In cases where there are projects, or goods and services mentioned in generic terms, properly secure the required authorization/
approval of the Sangguniang Bayan Council before entering into any contract.
observations.managementAction:
For the current year, there are still projects expressed in generic terms with lump sum appropriation where authorization/
approval of the Sangguniang Bayan Council were not secured.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

a. Exert more effort in observing the timely implementation of the PPAs.

b. Monitor the status of the implementation of the projects especially those PPAs enumerated in the AIP.

c. Revise the AIP as often as necessary especially when for those PPAs which could not be implemented during the year. This should be brought up in a meeting with the Municipal Development Council so that appropriate actions could be done.
observations.managementAction:
Management was not able to implement these projects as at yearend. 

For CY 2018, there are still capital outlay projects that remained unimplemented also as at yearend.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to remit on time the shares of the Provincial Government on the additional 1% tax on real property accruing to the Special Education Fund so that the Provincial Government can timely utilize the collections to finance various projects, programs, and activities beneficial to the constituents of the Province of Compostela Valley. 

Observe religiously the deadline set for the remittance of provincial shares which falls on every 15th day of the following month as mandated.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, management incurred again more delays in the remittance of provincial share representing the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property accruing to the Special Education Fund (SEF).
observations.reason: Due to heavy workload, this was not properly monitored by management. However, they assured the timely remittance on CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government to prepare and enter into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the recipient beneficiaries of the agricultural, fisheries and seaweed projects under the Bottom-Up Budgeting Program. 

Make sure that the duties and responsibilities of binding parties are clearly defined and established in order to at least ensure the smooth implementation, effectiveness, and sustainability of the projects. 

Stop the practice of not entering into a MOA with the intended beneficiaries when implementing priority programs and projects.

Follow up the delivery of the remaining 16 cattle to ensure the success of the cattle production project. Impose appropriate liquidated damages for the delayed deliveries.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to prepare Memorandum of Agreements.
observations.reason:
MOA preparation is impractical to prepare at this time due to the following reasons in the implementatin of  the projects:
1.Corn Production Enhancement-This was not sustained due to long drought that hit at the start of the project.
2.Seaweed Stilt Drier-Not productive due to ice-ice disease.
3.Bangus Production in Marine Fishcage- Not productive because the area is silted that affects its operation.
4. Cattle Production/Re-Stocking- this was not realized due to failure of bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government, the immediate usage of donated lot for the operation and management of Eco-Park with Alternative Technology Approach for its final waste disposal and for the purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes to sustain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan.

Prioritization of the funding for these properties should be immediately done so that the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste would not be hampered.

We recommended also the stoppage of the operation of the present disposal site and the strict implementation of the waste segregation policy of the Municipality from the source, in order to successfully attain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, management was not able to utilize another area of their lot for the operation of new controlled dumpsite. The purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes was not also realized due to lack of funds.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct annual physical
inventory of all properties and submit copy of the RPCPPE to the
auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year;

b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
maintain complete and updated records and to facilitate the
reconciliation of the balances of the Property, Plant and Equipment;
and

c. Ensure compliance with the procedures provided by existing laws,
rules and regulations on property and supply management.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to
the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated
February 22, 2017 of DBM and DILG by optimally utilizing the 20%
development funds solely for the implementation of projects, programs and
activities identified in Section 3.0 of the Joint Memorandum Circular of
DBM and DILG.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the
Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare
and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers
showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on
RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in
setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes
at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that management enforce the segregation of wastes,
require the repair of the Material Recovery Facility and provide
appropriation for sanitary landfill for the disposal of collected wastes in
conformity with the provision of Republic Act 9003 and to involve the
participation of the barangay officials for information dissemination.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance
with Sections 121 and 124 Volume I of the MNGAS on the proper accounting
of inventories by directing the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required
records and to immediately prepare/submit the required Report of Supplies
and Materials Issued with the original copies of the Requisition and Issue
Slips for CY 2018 to the Accounting Division/Unit as basis in the
recording/adjustments in the books of accounts, and to conduct physical
count of inventory items and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Budget
Officer and the GFPS-TWG Chair to stop from charging expenses that are
not related in addressing gender issues, promote women’s empowerment and
gender equality as provided in the provisions of the Joint Circular of PCW-
NEDA-DBM; and to charge the unrelated expenses to the regular
Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the LGU.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare and
submit to the Head of the Procuring Entity the Procurement and Monitoring
Report and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to the 2016 Revised IRR
of RA 9184.

We further recommended that the BAC furnish the audit team copy
of the said report to facilitate monitoring on the status of the procurement
activities and to be used as basis for contract review on the procured goods
and infrastructure projects.

We recommended that management provide a motor pool or well-
secured garage as a measure to protect and safeguard the motor vehicles
owned and maintained by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
personnel concerned of the Municipality to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption and submit the same to the audit team for verification to
ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively
controlled.

We recommended that Management require the BAC Chairman and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all perfected contracts for the
implementation of infrastructure projects of the Municipality are complete
before payment is made to ascertain their validity and propriety pursuant to
Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001, and to
submit the required supporting documents enumerated in Annex B to support
the contracts on procurement of infrastructure.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Awards Committee to submit the copies of the contracts and all the required
supporting documents within five (5) days from perfection thereof pursuant
to Sections 3.1.1 to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
(LDRRMO) to prepare and submit the monthly sources and utilization of the
LDRRMF in compliance with the Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-
002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance
with the procedures provided by existing laws, rules and regulations on
property and supply management and to:

 Create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct physical
inventory of all road networks and prepare the Report on the
Physical Count of the Road Network System of the local
government unit;
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Engineer to maintain and prepare the required
records and reports and to facilitate the recording of the road
networks in the books; and
 Take appropriate action against the concerned accountable
official/s for non-compliance and repeated unjustified failure to
comply with the requirements pursuant to Section 127 of PD 1445.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the
Municipal Treasurer exert extra effort to intensify collection of delinquent
accounts of local taxes and stall rentals and adopt the necessary measures
provided in Sections 254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known
as the “Local Government Code of 1991. These will provide additional funds
to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to
its constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure efficient,
effective and economical use of government resources by requiring the
immediate utilization of the Plastic Melter Densifier and Multipurpose
Shredder in the Material Recovery Facility and provide adequate safeguard
for the equipment against possible loss due to theft.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
ensure that the Inventory Committee conduct physical inventory of all the
Municipality’s assets and prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment. Make strategic actions/plan on how to conduct a complete
physical inventory of all Municipal properties, engage and adopt all the necessary
means to accomplish the task/plan accordingly. Instruct the Municipal Accountant
and the Property, Plant and Equipment Inventory Committee to reconcile its
records to address the reliability and existence of PPE accounts in the financial
statements and to properly identify unserviceable and missing properties.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor create Office of the GSO with
sufficient staff foe procurement, property management and general services who
shall perform functions enumerated under Section 490 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following officials:
a. Municipal Treasurer to reconcile and update its record of Real Property Unit
Cards per taxpayer to come up with a more reliable Certified List of Taxpayers
and submit the report to the Municipal Accountant for setting up of receivables at
the beginning of the year;
b. Municipal Treasurer to send Notice of Real Property Tax Delinquencies and
exert extra efforts in the collection of delinquent accounts;
c. Municipal Treasurer to submit the Certified List of all Real Property Tax
Delinquencies to the Sangguniang Bayan on or before the 31st of December of
the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were
incurred, with the request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for
collection therein. Also, coordinate with the Office of the Provincial Treasurer
for issuance of Warrant of Levy;
d. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the RPT and SET Receivables and prepare
subsidiary ledgers per individual taxpayers to support the balances presented in
the financial statements; and
e. Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to correct the balances of RPT
and SET Receivables presented in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Barangay Officials in
coordination with the Municipal Agriculture Office to prioritize funding for the
establishment in their respective Barangays Material Recovery Facilities in
compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168, dated October 27, 2009.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor take the necessary actions to
implement the plans and programs embodied in the approved Ten Year Solid
Waste Management Plan. Update the Audit Team on the status of its proposed
Sanitary Landfill.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the official concern to fully
utilize the fund for the implementation of programs/projects under Local Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) for the benefit of constituents
particularly in the affected and vulnerable areas and to refer to Section 5.1.10 of
COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and Section 21 of Republic
Act 10121 for the recording and reporting of LDRRMF and use of 30 percent and 70
percent calamity fund.
We recommended that the MDRRMO prepare and submit the Monthly Report of
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Accountant on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month in accordance with the format
provided by COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We also recommended that the MDRRMO prepare and submit the LDRRMF
Investment Plan in accordance with the format provided by the same circular to
reflect the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with
details as to projects and activities to be funded, as well as the list of projects and
activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert to the General
Fund the excess amount transferred to the Special Trust Fund and make the
necessary adjusting entries. Similarly, revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the
General Fund all unutilized balance of more than five years in the Trust Fund of
the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG):
a. to scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower and equipment
on all the projects to be awarded and not on an individual project only in cases
where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or
overlapping implementation period. This is to oblige the winning contractor to
supplement its manpower and/or equipment to be utilized in the projects
awarded to it or to disqualify it from obtaining several awards or contracts in
conformity with the provisions of Section 34.1 and 34.3 of the Revised IRR of
RA 9184;
b. to monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government agencies
and to coordinate with them involved in implementing infrastructure projects
not disclosed in the Statement of On-going and Completed projects to avoid
overlapping and delay in project accomplishment and/or substandard quality
of work; and
c. to conduct the required processes and procedures in all the stages of the
bidding in the procurement of infrastructure and comply strictly with all the
documentary requirements as mandated by RA 9184. Non-compliance of the
required documents by the prospective bidder shall be a cause for automatic
disqualification in the eligibility and award of contract.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
strictly and closely monitor all projects simultaneously implemented by the
contractors and prepare an individual status report per project to be submitted to
the Honorable Mayor on a monthly basis for monitoring purposes and in order to
check, verify and evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the said
contractors.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to submit the
minutes of the pre-bid conference of the ten infrastructure projects with a total
contract costs of P 39,774,828.97.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and
Municipal Engineer to coordinate and work closely to ensure full utilization of the
20% Development Fund for the efficient and effective attainment of desired
socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes;
b. Instruct the MPDC and Municipal Budget Officer see to it that the proposed PPAs
chargeable against the 20% Development Fund are properly
identified/itemized and the related information such as the specific timetable and
the quantity of the expected outputs are defined in the AIP and Annual Budget;
c. Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate
closely in monitoring completed projects to be able to reflect in the books of
accounts. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
understatement.
d. Refrain from charging expenses under the 20% Development Fund which are
not capital in nature.
e. Make the necessary efforts and adopt immediate actions to utilize idle project
- Community Hospital (emergency and ancillary) and maximize the benefits
that can be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to
evaluate carefully the programs and projects identified during the year in
accordance with the provisions stated in Section 272 of the RA 7160 and
DepEd/DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 and
cause its effective implementation in order to achieve its purpose of providing
quality education in the public schools.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality refrain from charging non-priority
expenses from the Special Education Fund and strictly comply with the provisions
of the above-stated regulations.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the PPE
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to strictly follow the
pertinent provisions of RA 9184 on the procurement. Be cautious in using the
different methods of procurement and ensure that the requirements for each were
adhered to. Submit an explanation/justification letter regarding the mode of
procurement being used by the Municipality for the purchase of textbooks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through its GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS):
a. Facilitate the updating of its existing GAD database to have an effective basis for
gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. Refer to
Annex B of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-02 for data
requirements.
b. Undertake thorough gender analysis, based on relevant data for proper
identification of existing gender issues, to ensure that the approved PPAs to be
incorporated in the GPB are responsive to address the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 require the LCPC to improve the level of functionality of the LCPC by fully
discharging its functions, duties and responsibilities;
 direct the members of the LCPC to prepare a Local Development Plan for
Children (LDPC), Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC) and Local Code
for Children (LCC); and
 ensure that the fund allocated for LCPC was fully maximized and the
programs, projects and activities were implemented for the protection of
children.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to strictly follow the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR. Be cautious in using the
different methods of procurement and ensure that the requirements for each were
adhered to. Submit an explanation/justification letter regarding the mode of
procurement being used by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the practice of hiring the services of
legal consultant pursuant to COA Circular No. 98-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
 The Municipal Accountant to immediately remit to the source agency any
unexpended balances of fund transfers after project completion and the
share of the national government in the Building Permit Fees;
 The BAC Chairman to submit explanation letter on the mode of
procurement being applied in the procurement of goods to be used in the
Supplementary Feeding Program for further evaluation of the Audit Team;
 MSWDO and Municipal Accountant to exhaust all possible efforts to collect
the balance of the program “Isang Bayan, Isang Produkto, Isang Milyong
Programa ni GMA” with an outstanding balance of ₱632,883.00 otherwise,
verify with the source agency if the obligation is still existing in their books.
Make the necessary reversion after securing a certification from the source
agency that you are no longer required to liquidate the fund.
We also recommended that the Management ensure strict compliance with the
provision of EO No. 51, whereby the NFA shall be the supplier of rice that the
government entity would need in connection to its functions, unless a waiver is
issued by the NFA.
We further recommended that the Management ensure that the provisions of
Memorandum of Agreement made and entered into by the Municipality were
strictly followed. Submit explanation letter why the 20% of the ABC for the
Construction of Tourism Office Building as LGU counterpart was not being
provided.

We recommended that the Municipality (a) reconcile employees’ records at GSIS,
PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the unremitted
balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies; (b) make the
necessary actions to collect the unpaid premium contributions and in-default
loans of the municipal employees (c) identify the cause/s of the over remittance
for some accounts and make the proper adjustments and (d) remit the due GSIS
loan amortizations pursuant to Section 14.2, Rule III of the Implementing Rules
and Regulations of Government Service Insurance Act of 1997 or R.A. No. 8291 to
avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed
penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to set-up
