and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating
performance and viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises and 20%
Development Fund, and to serve as a decision making tool of the agency. Also, record
all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding
subsidiary ledgers.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to persons who still has an outstanding balances urging settlement of their outstanding balances and request write-off of the balances for the other receivables that cannot be substantiated after exhausting all possible means and efforts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.

Recommendation:
Require the inventory committee/team to conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor in accordance with Section of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume 1.  Likewise, reconciliation between the records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should be made and effect necessary adjustments when appropriate.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit a written explanation on the necessity of the above-listed disbursements and discontinue the incurrence of excessive and unnecessary catering services for meals and snacks. If found unwarranted, a corresponding disallowance will be issued.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the municipality fast tract the development of a Sanitary Landfill/Dumpsite as a final garbage disposal site of the local government unit for the protection of the health and environment hazards of its constituents.
We further recommend that Management activate its Solid Waste Management Board to ensure the viability and effective implementation of the Local Government Ecological Waste Management Plan as required under Republic Act No. 9003.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the OIC Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge and record the wages of the contractual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services.
We also recommend that management limit the hiring of casuals/job order employees for intermittent, emergency jobs or specific piece of work with short duration as guided by CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and maximize the services of the permanent employees for regular agency functions.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the LDRRM Officer to promptly submit the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

Recommendation:
We require Management to submit a written explanation as to why claims of contractors on progress billings for infrastructure projects were paid despite the non-preparation of a duly verified and certified Statement of Work Accomplished.
We further recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims from contractors on infrastructure projects without the preparation and attachment of a duly certified Statement of Work Accomplished in order to ensure that the total amount claimed and paid, really correspond with the actual accomplishments of the project.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to furnish copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders with complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval. Any unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to comply shall be subjected to the administrative disciplinary action under the penalty clause in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit a written explanation/justifications on the causes of delay in the projects’ completion. We further recommend that henceforth, Management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management review the operation of the swine breeding center and formulate procedures including the use and/or disposal of the municipality’s share of the offspring, as guided by Title VI of RA 7160.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management-
Require Mr. Gaudioso T. Libres, the designated Caretaker of the Swine Breeding Center of the municipality, to submit valid written justification on disposing three (3) government-owned pigs in violation to Section 356 of RA 7160 and Title V of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management.
Require the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Auditor the memorandum of agreement entered into by the Department of Agriculture, the municipality and Mr. Silverio T. Miano, for verification.
Require the former Officer-in-Charge of the Office of the Municipal Agriculture, Mr. Aguirico T. Bernales to submit valid written justification on the failure of his Office to monitor and supervise the proper implementation of the Dispersal Program
Conduct re-evaluation on the reasonableness of the weight on each pig as declared, taking into consideration their ages/number of months as reflected in the Animal Release Orders and require immediate remittance of the proceeds to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for proper accounting and recording of government funds.
We further recommend that Management require the refund from the persons determined to be accountable and liable for the amount of losses incurred in the improper disposal of government-owned live stocks pursuant to Section 377 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 105 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, without prejudice to the filing of administrative and/or criminal case, if warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the municipal officials concerned to adhere with the prescribed period in the submission of financial and other reports. Failure to submit these reports on time shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries pursuant to Sections 122 of PD 1445.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to coordinate and conduct meeting with the different LGU offices and national government agencies involved in the implementation of the planned GAD programs and activities; make an evaluation as to the clear-cut causes of the non-implementation of these projects and; formulate strategies in order to ensure the complete implementation of GAD programs and activities in the future.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management revisit its procurement process with the end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in every procurement activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184.
We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee practice wide dissemination of bid opportunities, send invitation to independent observers, extend equal opportunity and issue bid documents to all qualified parties to dispel presumptions of rigged biddings and promote transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management adhere to the provisions prescribed by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017, amending the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security in the procurement of goods, in order to protect the interest of the government.
We further recommend that future procurements be deducted with the one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) retention money or require a special bank guarantee which is equivalent to one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the total contract price, otherwise, expenses due to the defects of the goods within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials approving the payment.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management should:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry voucher
to take up the cancellation of the stale checks. Likewise, direct the
Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the
stale checks and to submit the original copies of the cancelled
checks.
b) Require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer towards the implementation of the proper
recording and reporting of supplies and materials to promote better
control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.
c) Provide additional information to be presented on the notes to
financial statements which must be concise but briefly discussed
using a narrative and descriptive formats, taking into consideration
that the effects of financial transactions of significant amount. To
ensure that the same is clearly understandable to its interested
users, disclosure of such relevant information must be duly
supported with corroborating schedules that must be consistent
with the reported balances shown in the Statement of Changes in
Net Assets/Government Equity.
d) Require the Municipal Account to submit Financial Statements per
Fund.
Recommendation
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
(a) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of PPE to
determine the actual existence of all assets owned by the Municipality;
and to furnish the Accounting Office with copies of reports on PPEs which
were already waste, and those PPE’s whose control of the risks and
rewards are no longer in the municipality.
(b) Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to (i) maintain Property,
Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for each category of
plant, property and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards
(PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and
disposition of the same (ii) reconcile their records for PPE accounts and
determine the causes of the variance thereof and cause proper disposition;
(ii) identify all PPE items without ARE and the employees accountable for
the items to pave the way for the issuance of the ARE to all
employees/officials concerned.
(c) Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for
the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable
properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all
obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section
79 of PD 1445.
Recommendations:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to fully recognize the RPT/SET
Receivable at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of tax
payers’ amount due and collectible for the year to be prepared by the
Municipal Treasurer in order to record in the books not the mere income
estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes
and to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statements.
2. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile regularly the accounting and treasury records to verify the
accuracy and validity of their records based on certified list of taxpayers
and immediately provide corrective action for discrepancies noted.
3. Advice the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to consider purchasing a
computer software to expedite the treasury processes and explore possible
option for an enhanced operational quality of its recording system that
readily provides report required upon demand by the oversight agencies.
Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to determine and reclassify the
actual amounts that are long overdue or beyond 1 year as non-current assets and
to take the following actions.
1. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash
advances/receivables and strictly comply with provisions of COA
Circular No. 97-002 in the granting and liquidation of cash advances,
otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.
2. Refrain from granting additional cash advances unless previous cash
advances are liquidated. Implement withholding of salaries for those
who failed to liquidate within the time frame set by regulations.
3. Avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005
for dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving
for ten (10) years or more and where settlement could no longer be
ascertained.

We recommended that
management require the
OIC-Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the discrepancy
between the balances of
construction in progress-
infrastructure asset account
vis-à-vis to the amount of
contract costs of the
completed infrastructure
asset in order to recognize
the same as an asset with
specific account in the
statement of financial
position. Further, instruct the
OIC-Municipal Accountant
to provide depreciation of
the said asset account from
the time the said
infrastructure asset have
been completed until the
reporting period so that the
balance of PPE accounts is
fairly presented in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
(LCE) instruct the Municipal
Treasury Office to strictly
require all stall owners to
enter into a lease contract
and imposed the payment of
rights fee before occupying
any stall in the market. We
likewise recommended that
the LCE request the SB to
enact an ordinance
instituting Market Code in
order that the Treasury
personnel can enforce the
delinquent stall owner to
fully paid their rights fee.

We recommended that
management instruct the
Municipal Accountant to
prepare adjusting entries to
record the amount of rental
dues as well as the fines and
penalties as of December 31,
2017 as income from market
operation by debiting to
account of Operating Lease
Receivable (1-03-02-010),
and crediting to Receipts
from Market Operations (4-
02-02-150) and Fines and
Penalties-Business Income
(4-02-02-980) accounts
pursuant to the Revised
Chart of Accounts under
COA Circular No. 2015-009
dated December 1, 2015.
Likewise, the Municipal
Accountant shall analyze the
market records to recognize
additional revenues accruing
to the Municipality from
stall owners with unpaid
rental fees and penalties.

We recommended that
management instruct the
Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to:
 Stop the practice of
indicating the brand
name/model of equipment to
be procured by the
Municipality pursuant to
Section 18 of Rule VI of
2016 Revised IRR of R.A.
9184;
 Award contracts to lowest
calculated bidder that has
complied with all Class “A”
and “B” documents as
mentioned in Section 23.1 of
the 2016 Revised IRR of
R.A. of 9184, and;
 Submit all lacking
documents of the above-
mentioned contracts to
support the propriety and
legality of procurement
procedures undertaken and
the payment made thereof.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
instruct the Municipal
Engineer and Municipal
Planning Development
Officer to issue Certificate of
Final Acceptance only after
one (1) year defects liability
had lapsed and after
conducting final inspection
to determine the presence of
any defects for which the
concerned contractor may be
held liable according to
warranty provisions in the
contract in accordance with
the provisions of Section
62.2.1-2 of the 2016 Revised
IRR of R.9184.

We recommended that
management take the
following courses of action:
 Prepare and submit the
individual PPMPs duly
evaluated and reviewed by
the Municipal Budget
Office to the BAC
Secretariat for
consolidation;
 Prepare the Annual
Procurement Plan (APP) to
guide the agency in the
procurement of goods and
services to minimize the use
of shopping as separate
alternative method of
procurement. Likewise,
approved APP shall be
inconformity with the
prescribed format to reflect
procurement activities that
it plans to undertake within
the calendar year;
 Assign individual / office /
department who shall be in-
charged with the
procurement of all
common-use supplies and
materials of the
Municipality and;
 Require the regular
purchase of various office
supplies through check
disbursements payable
directly to the
supplier/creditor
inconformity with Section
93 of PD 1445.
Reimbursements should be
allowed only when the need
for the items purchased is
indeed exceptionally urgent
and the amount should not
exceed ₱1,000.00 per
procurement.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
(LCE) require the BAC
Secretariat to post all
Notices of Award, Notice to
Proceed and the approved
contracts within the
prescribed date/period for all
procurements conducted
either through public bidding
and/or alternative methods
of procurement,
inconformity with the
revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the
Municipal Treasury Office
and the Municipal
Engineering Office
responsible for the
acceptance of deliveries shall
notify the COA of the time
and date of the scheduled
deliveries for ocular
inspection and verification.
In addition, copies of
Inspection and Acceptance
Reports should be submitted
within 24 hours upon
acceptance as mandated by
the regulation.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
instruct the members of
MCPC to prepare all
required administrative
reports which will be the
basis of Sangguniang Bayan
to provide as sufficient
budget allocation to
strengthen the implementation
and monitoring of proposed
programs, projects, activities
funded by the one (1)
percent component of IRA.

We recommended that
management establish GAD
Database that will serve as
their basis for gender-
responsive planning,
programming and policy
formulation and a GAD
Code that will support the
Municipality’s efforts in
promoting, protecting and
fulfilling women's human
rights, women's economic
empowerment and gender-
responsive governance
towards the attainment of
gender equality and women's
empowerment.

We recommended that
management take the
following courses of action:
 Institute a human resource
policy or guidelines to limit
the numbers of personnel
hired based only on the
needs of the LGU;
 Likewise, execute a contract
in the engagement of
services of job orders with a
well-defined functions,
responsibilities, expected
output and contract duration
to facilitate proper evaluation
of their work performed,
and;
 Ensure that payment of
wages of job orders are
supported by a complete
documentation as required
under COA Circular No.
2012-001 to ensure
propriety, legality and
validity of their aforesaid
claims. Non-submission of
the aforesaid documents
shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the
management stop allowing
personnel hired through job
orders and contract of
service in attending
trainings, seminars while
paying any expenses
incurred relative to their
attendance of the same.
Continuous non-compliance
thereof shall automatically
be DISALLOWED in audit.

We recommended that
management strictly follow
the guidelines provided
under COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 30,
2013 to avoid possible
administrative and or
criminal sanction.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
(LCE) require all department
heads concern to strictly
comply with the full
disclosure policy required by
the DILG Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-140, to
ensure transparency on the
Municipality’s financial
operations and transactions
as well as to allow the
Municipality’s constituents
to understand how their local
government’s budget were
spent for public services.

We recommended that
management require the
Municipal Engineer and
Municipal Planning
Development Officer to tightly
monitor all programs and
projects which were
identified and prioritized
under 20% DF, in order to
fully achieve the desired
socio-economic development
and environmental outcomes
and ensure that public
benefits are received timely
from these projects by the
constituents of the
Municipality. Further,
instruct the Municipal
Engineer to provide program
of works including estimates
of works, items, quantities
and costs of man power of
all projects to be undertaken
to avoid irregular cost of
salaries and wages charged
to each project. It is further
recommended that
management implement the
infrastructure projects under
the 20% DF through contract
in order that the same would
be completed on time and
the funds would be spent
efficiently and effectively.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider establishment of the General Services Office with sufficient staff for procurement, property management and general services who shall perform the functions enumerated under Section 490 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO was designated as GSO.
observations.reason: Although the Inventory Committee has already been created, they are still in the process of implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following officials: a. Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT and SET Receivable at the gross amount of collectible taxes at 1% each and prepare subsidiary ledgers or any schedules for the RPT and SET Receivables accounts that will support the outstanding balances of the accounts presented in the financial statements; b. Municipal Treasurer to prioritize and take action on updating the Real Property Unit Cards of each taxpayer and preparing the list of delinquent real property taxes at year-end and providing the same to the Municipal Accountant for finalization of financial statements; Being a first class municipality, we further recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider computerization of the Assessor’s Office and the Assessor’s Office giving the RPTA System to the Province.
observations.managementAction: Set up the RPT and SET Receivable at the gross amount of collectible taxes at 1% each. Updating the property cards. Computerization will be given funding on the coming years.
observations.reason: Subsidiary Ledgers weren’t prepared yet due to insufficient personnel. The Municipality is still waiting for the Batangas Province to give the RPTA System to the Assessor’s Office. Computerization will be given funding on the coming years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Provincial Accountant verify the material discrepancies noted in the balances of Due and Due to accounts and submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) of the adjustments made.
observations.managementAction: Necessary documents were timely furnished to the other party for proper recording so as to avoid such discrepancies.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going with the Provincial Accountant for those balances that weren’t reconciled yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and CAFOA to check and verify the entries in the CAFOA and ensure that all expenses incurred are requested for obligation under appropriations allotted for such purpose; b. the Budget Officer post entries in the RAAO based on approved obligations in the CAFOA; and c. the Municipal Accountant record expenses in proper accounts prescribed and compliant with PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: Post entries in the RAAO based on approved obligations in the CAFOA were performed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to prioritize and take action on previous year’s recommendations relative to these long outstanding receivables and that disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements regarding the probability or status of recovery of such receivables be provided.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO coordinated with various barangays and acted on previous year’s recommendations relative to these long outstanding receivables.
observations.reason: The documents submitted will be re-evaluated by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended creation of PRAISE Committee and Grievance Machinery and establishment of a PRAISE Model Performance Management Team to ensure alignment of office performance targets and provide inputs for grants of awards and incentives, and ensure rules, policies and guidelines in performance planning, monitoring and evaluation are in place.
observations.managementAction: The PRAISE Committee and Grievance Machinery have been created. The Performance Management Team has been created.
observations.reason: The PRAISE Programs and Policies were not specific and did not include the guidelines in performance planning, monitoring and evaluation. The reasons were not given because they believe that their programs and policies are sufficient.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer that all changes in the approved annual budget for the Executive Sector be proposed to the Sanggunian Bayan and seek their approval thru enacted ordinance. The Sanggunian Bayan should ensure all changes are properly enacted thru ordinance. Otherwise such realignment/reversion/augmentation shall not be deemed effected and no obligations can be charged thereto.
observations.managementAction: Documents have been submitted.
observations.reason: The submitted documents will be subjected for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer-Designate to: a. meticulously plan and judiciously plan procurement for each quarter and consolidate them in the Annual Procurement Plan; b. comply with procurement regulations and procure ordinary and regular office supplies from PS-DBM; shopping from private suppliers to be resorted to only for items not available in PS-DBM and supported by certification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is currently in the transition period and the designated HRMO/GSO is new in the position. The practice of centralizing procurement is being consolidated.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of transitioning procurement planning. The GSO-designate is newly assigned and the transition period is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all on-going and other projects/programs/activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the year, and thereafter submit quarterly in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the list of all on-going and other governments projects/programs/activities (PPA).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents of the Municipality, particularly those to be submitted to the Audit Team, with consideration on the preservation period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: Construction of the storeroom is currently being constructed.
observations.reason: The storeroom is currently being constructed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue granting cash advances to Department of Education officials and employees and designate a permanent employee of the Municipality as Special Disbursing Officer to perform disbursing functions out of Special Education Fund. Henceforth, adhere with COA Circular No. 97-002 on grant and utilization of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Henceforth, adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; the Local School Board authorized the Municipal Treasurer to disburse funds from the Special Education Fund pursuant to RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board evaluate and review the SEF budget with emphasis on the proper identification and selection of priority programs and projects, prepare SEF budget presented by program/activity/project (P/A/P) and per school using DepEd SEF Budget Preparation Form No.1 pursuant to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.1 s.2017 to ensure SEF budget is net of budgetary provisions for the same items in DepEd budget.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality reviewed the SEF budget emphasizing proper identification of programs/projects. Submission and evaluation are ongoing. The necessary adjustments were being prepared.
observations.reason: The Three-Year Budgeting Plan was not prepared. The submitted program, activity and project details will ensure SEF budget is net of related items; documents to ensure SEF budgeting were not yet fully presented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) which requires at least one LDRRM Officer and three LDRRM staff pursuant to RA 10121 and the Joint Memorandum; require preparation of annual LDRRM Plan and budget specifying programs/projects including those charged to unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and implement at intended period.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Plan was submitted. The Municipal DRRM Office is being staffed; funds for specific programs are being allocated.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Plan submitted did not incorporate the unexpended balances of the previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to update its master list and provide justification and adopt quarterly validation and monitoring of beneficiaries to determine their situation and utilization of stipend.
observations.managementAction: The Office conducted the quarterly validation and updated the master list. The DSWD Regional Office is validating the list of senior citizens in order of priority.
observations.reason: The Master List submitted does not guarantee that all eligible and qualified senior citizens were included in the Master List.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require MSWDO to furnish the Audit Team the complete master list submitted to DSWD and provide proper justification on names of beneficiaries and submit progress/accomplishment reports on status of the program.
observations.managementAction: The refund by the participants was made for those who did not attend. The required documentation was compiled and submitted.
observations.reason: Submitted documents will be evaluated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the financial statements in compliance with PPSAS including the required reconciliation to be presented in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to the Surplus was prepared but does not present accurate reconciliation. Monthly reconciliations at year-end were prepared.
observations.reason: Differences were not easily traced; monthly reconciliation was being performed by the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer but full reconciliation to PPSAS requirements needed improvements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly reconciliation of surplus/deficit with the net operating cash flow for easy reconciliation at year-end and adhere with rules on disclosing information in notes to financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the net cash flows from operating activities to the surplus was prepared but did not present accurate reconciliation.
observations.reason: Monthly reconciliation was performed; differences still required detailed tracing but reconciliation work was being carried out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor delegate management of supplies and property to a single person with assigned staffs for inspection, control and monitoring; prepare Purchase Requests for supplies needed for the quarter; maintain stock cards and prepare SSMI; accept delivered items with AIR and submit RPCI; and ensure PR/RIS/SAI processes are followed.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and Inventory Committee was created; property management was assigned to the Treasurer in January 2018. Some procurement practices were consolidated.
observations.reason: The inventory taking of supplies was not among the priorities of management earlier; transition of responsibilities took time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the list of unreleased checks paid and at the end of the year submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for reversion back to cash in bank and payables accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The list of unreleased checks was prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant for proper presentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the BAC validate the list of on-going projects and ensure all possible on-going projects are included; monitor contractors’ ongoing projects with other agencies; ensure sufficiency of manpower and equipment before awarding multiple projects.
observations.managementAction: The BAC reminded contractors to indicate all on-going projects and validated the projects not yet started.
observations.reason: Validation of projects awarded and coordination with contractors is ongoing; some projects were validated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require submission of deficient documents and require collection of corresponding business taxes/permits. Ensure strict compliance with bidding requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality required the collection of corresponding business taxes/permits and submitted the required documents. The practice of requiring business tax collection increased collections by ₱12,522,219.21 in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure funds allocated for priority projects be optimally utilized for intended purposes to fully attain objectives of the fund and maximize benefits.
observations.managementAction: Some priority projects were realigned; efforts being made to utilize funds for development.
observations.reason: The 20% Development Fund was not fully utilized by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended centralization of Purchase Order issuance under the GSO/Municipal Treasurer; ensure Acceptance and Inspection Reports are dated; ensure complete details in PO and monitor deliveries; and ensure proper supporting documents exist.
observations.managementAction: The Purchase Order preparation and issuance procedure was centralized; GSO designated and Purchase Orders are now issued with complete details.
observations.reason: No refund was made yet for certain transactions; submitted documents will be evaluated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor ensure cash advances be supported with complete documents, discontinue granting cash advances without authority, ensure refunds for those who did not participate, and require complete liquidation reports.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation reports were properly accomplished and required documentation was attached to the liquidation reports. Refunds were made by participants who did not attend.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were missing for certain vouchers; submissions are being evaluated and some refunds have been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local School Board stop grant of allowance from SEF to nationally hired teachers; ensure all expenditures are covered with sufficient budget allotments and monitor realignments against actual expenditures.
observations.managementAction: No refund was made initially. The Sanggunian approved certain realignments and the practice was stopped. The procedure of realignment and augmentation was being observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended refund by the officials who approved the disbursement and refund by participants who did not attend; strengthen controls on team building expenses and ensure proper documentation.
observations.managementAction: Refund was made by the participants totaling amount claimed as not attended.
observations.reason: The submitted documents will be evaluated; refunds were made for some participants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor require officials and employees to refund amounts which do not serve public purpose and ensure strict review before processing of payments and proper supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that crucial scrutiny of documents was made before processing for payments. Adjustments and withholdings were applied where applicable.
observations.reason: Some documents were lacking or had discrepancies; evaluations are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant ensure all claims are supported with complete documents to avoid deficiencies and recover overpayments.
observations.managementAction: All required documents were submitted; some overpayments were recovered.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were still missing for certain claims; recovery actions were underway for overpayments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Sanggunian enact ordinances, prioritize establishment of sanitary landfill and MRFs, enter into agreements with Bauan Solid Waste Management, enforce segregation policies and conduct IEC and barangay coordination.
observations.managementAction: Ordinance was already for public hearing. MENRO coordinated with DENR and other agencies regarding provision of funds/facilities. Some barangays have established MRFs and additional barangays allocated budget in CY 2019. An agreement with Bauan Solid Waste Management, Inc. was entered for hauling and dumping.
observations.reason: Some barangays still don’t have MRFs; lack of funds and remaining steps for ordinance enactment and full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer immediately cause the closing of bank accounts in private rural bank and open new current account with Land Bank of the Philippines to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Authority to withdraw all monies under accounts maintained by the Municipality was granted to the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Vice-Mayor and Municipal Treasurer per Resolution No. 273-2017 dated July 6, 2017. The new account with Land Bank of the Philippines - Bauan Branch was opened.
observations.reason: The accounts maintained with the Ibaan Rural Bank, Inc. - Mabini Branch was not yet closed as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that management explain why the projects were provided in generic terms and do not contain sufficient details and cost of each project. Henceforth, require from the Local Development Council a list of duly identified projects in the formulation of future annual budgets.
observations.managementAction: Development projects under the 20% Development Fund were duly identified in the CY 2018 Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC Municipal Treasurer to release timely the 30% share of the RPT income due to barangays and see to it that all remaining balances as of end of the year shall have been wholly released in order to fast track the implementation of various programmed projects in the barangay level.
observations.managementAction: Shares of the real property taxes for barangays for CY 2017-2018 were duly released to concerned barangays.
Audit recommendations were all complied with.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s Index Card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a copy of it to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books pursuant to Section 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1. 

Direct the Accountant to exert diligent effort to locate the source documents giving     rise to the entry of this account in the books and to coordinate and reconcile his records with the concerned agencies to be able to establish the accuracy and validity of this reported receivable account.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were all complied with.
Audit recommendations were all complied with.
Disbursement vouchers for gasoline were all supported with charge slips and duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets in the ensuing years.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that Management require the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advance balances granted as provided for in Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.  Moreover, send individual letter of demand to every officer and employee, enjoining immediate liquidation of their long outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Liquidation reports for the Confidential and Intelligence Fund have been submitted to the COA Central Office.

The Municipal Accountant has sent demand letters to persons liable on the other outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Credit Notices from COA Central Office for the Confidential Fund has not been received by management.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant identify the recipients and the specific amounts granted to each organization/association and institute actions for its prompt settlement and liquidation.  And also, direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has identified the identity of the debtor-organizations and will send demand letters to debtor-organizations/recipients in the incoming year.
observations.reason: List and names of debtor-organizations/recipients has been identified.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that Management direct all accounting office personnel to assist the current municipal accountant in the identification of the receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has identified the identity of the debtor-organizations and will send demand letters to debtor-organizations/recipients in the incoming year.
observations.reason: List and names of debtor-organizations/recipients has been identified.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that Management require the Office of the Treasurer and the Accounting Section to maintain and update regularly property cards and property ledger cards, and immediately reconcile any variance detected between the accounting and property records.  Moreover, direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine the causes of the discrepancies between the two (2) records to be able to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to present fairly the property, plant and equipment account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE was submitted and received by Office of the Auditor on February 20, 2019.
observations.reason:
CY 2018 Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) was submitted on February 20, 2019, however, accounts’ balances per RPCPPE does not reconciled with the balances per books. 

Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards were also not maintained by the Accounting Section.


.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Supply Officer Designate to prepare the Inventory an Inspection Report of Unserviceable/Unused Properties and Equipment and facilitate the immediate disposal thereof in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties was submitted on February 20, 2019.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties was not supported with complete documentation.

Disposal of these unserviceable properties were not yet done.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that the Management allocate funds in the annual budget for the processing of the Torrens Title for all lots owned by the municipality. We further recommend that Management designate a personnel to work on the titling of these land.
observations.managementAction: Land Titles for seven (7) lots were already submitted to the DENR for titling purposes.
observations.reason: Processing of land titles were still on-going at DENR.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that Management submit written sufficient justification in pursuing the conduct of a fund raising activity despite the absence of a resolution from the Local Sanggunian authorizing the collection of fees, otherwise personnel involved thereto will be made to answer accordingly.   

We also recommend that Management refrain from conducting similar activities and charging fees without the approval and authority from the Local Sanggunian pursuant to Section 132 of the Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: No fund raising activity was conducted in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Management has not submitted any written justification in the conduct of the fund raising activity.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that Management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and the related DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the prompt and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the implementation of priority development projects in order to optimally attain the socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Unimplemented programs/projects are to be implemented in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Only five (5) out of the ten (10) programs and activities budgeted in CY 2018 were implemented.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to comply with the guidelines issued under COA Circular No. 2013-004 relative to the information and publicity of the Programs/Projects/Activities implemented by the municipality particularly the submission of monthly updates and quarterly reports on government projects, and the display of signboards in conspicuous places in every location of the PPAs, irrespective of amount.
observations.managementAction: Billboards/signboards were posted only on big budgeted projects.
observations.reason: Not all on-going projects has billboards/signboards as required.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that the municipality fast tract the development of a Sanitary Landfill/Dumpsite as a final garbage disposal site of the local government unit for the protection of the health and environment hazards of its constituents.

We further recommend that Management activate its Solid Waste Management Board to ensure the viability and effective implementation of the Local Government Ecological Waste Management Plan as required under Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management (MESWM) 10-Year Plan of the Municipality of Mabini was approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) through NSWMC Resolution No. 1133, Series of 2018, dated October 29, 2018.
observations.reason: Management will negotiate with the DECS for the use of a lot owned by the municipality located within the vicinity of the school compound for the Ecological Waste Facility.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend the Management appropriate one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment of the municipality for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the Local Council for the Protection of Children.  Also, prepare and submit the necessary reports enumerated in Items No. 1 of Part II of the above-quoted DILG Memorandum Circular for the proper and effective implementation of programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, the LGU appropriated the amount of ₱120,000.00 but utilized only the amount of  ₱104,961.68 for the Local Council for the Protection of Children.
observations.reason: Budgeted amount for CY 2018 has not reached the required allocation of 1% for the Local Council for the Protection of Children as per IRA received.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that management submit justification in writing on why financial assistance under the AICS Program were granted to clients despite the absence of supporting documents as enumerated in Section 4.2  of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 15, Series of 2014.  

Also, direct the Municipal Social Welfare Officer to submit the records of the cases and all other basic supporting documents relative to the claims for further evaluation, otherwise the transactions will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management directed all persons liable/responsible to refund the whole amount of financial assistance granted.
observations.reason:
Notice of Disallowances No. 17-001-100(16)
No. 17-002-100(16)
No. 17-003-100(16)
No. 17-004-100(16)
No. 17-005-100(16)
No. 17-006-100(16)
No. 17-007-100(16)
No. 17-008-100(16)
No. 17-009-100(16)

Notices of Finality of Decisions on these disallowances has been issued and duly received by the persons  liable/responsible.

Refund has been taking place on installment basis.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that management require the Bids and Awards Committee and other officials concerned to fast track the implementation of the Waterworks Project funded out from the DILG Support Fund in order to deliver the intended benefits due the public, and also, to avoid additional costs to finish the project if implemented in the subsequent years due to constant rise of prices in construction materials.
observations.managementAction: The  implementation  of the ₱1.3M project was delayed due to some revisions of the Program of Works for different sanitation facilities in 26 schools in order to suit their needs.
observations.reason: The P5M waterworks project was completed, while the ₱1.3M for water and sanitation facilities in 26 public schools was 95.54% as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to charge and record the wages of the casuals/job order employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and Wages-Casual/Contractual as prescribed in the Philippines Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).  

Moreover, minimize the hiring of casuals/job order employees as to necessity and maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 55% Personal Services Limitation and always observe austerity measures in the disbursement of public funds as provided for in Administrative Order No. 103, Series of 2004.
observations.managementAction:
Management has directed the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to record wages of casuals/job order employees to its appropriate account. 
 
Stream-lining of employees is also highly considered in order to observe austerity measures.
observations.reason: Wages of job order employees assigned in various departments were charged to Personnel Services except for a few.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization and implementation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are duly implemented. We also recommend that management faithfully adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely review of the required LDRRM reports.
observations.reason:
The CY 2018 Plan and Budget and the Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund was submitted only on March 8, 2019.

Monthly Report on the Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund were also not submitted before the 15th day of each month as required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a) Integrate in the financial reports the qualitative characteristics such as reliability and comparability, in order to be useful to wide range of decision makers;
 
b) Provide only relevant but sufficient information, may it be narrative and descriptive and/or corroborating schedules in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and

c) Ensure that the submitted reports are free from substantial inconsistencies that undermine reliability of information.
observations.managementAction: The deficiencies noted on the 2017 Financial Statements were already rectified but there are still deficiencies prevailing in the 2018 Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the members of the Local School Board (LSB) see to it that the Special Education Fund should be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures in adherence to the provisions of DepEd-DBM_DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s 2017 dated January 19, 2017. Prioritize only those expenditures which promote the quality of education and those programs which support the primary needs of the students within the municipality. 


Inform also the DepEd District Office of the expenditures which could not be anymore charged against the SEF budget of the Municipal Government to preclude the issuance of audit suspension/
disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board had already managed that the Special Education Fund was spent solely and exclusively for expenditures in adherence to the provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the members of the LCPC to prepare the Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC). Ensure that all PPAs under the said plans are amply provided with funds, and that all are implemented in adherence to the provisions of DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. 

Also, engage in partnership with private organizations, establishments, and entities that provide financial assistance for the implementation of LCPC programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management already prepared the Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC) with budgeted amount. They also claimed that they engage in partnership with other private organizations establishments and entities in the implementation of programs and activities for the welfare of children in the municipality.

The Office In-charge 
is conducting periodic information and education campaign on waste management  best practices especially on the proper operation of respective MRFs of the barangays. They claimed they had encouraged the LSWMB to monitor the proper implementation of proper waste management in their respective barangays.

The management is still operating an open dumpsite.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

a. Implement Section 296(a) of the RRC on dealing with the delinquent market vendors for the timely collection of market rentals and to prevent the eventual increase of uncollected surcharges and interests.

b. To execute the provisions of Section 301 in awarding the declared vacant market stall through the adjudication process. The same process is expected to be also applied when there is transfer of rights over the market stall from previous occupant to the new one.

c. Revisit the steps/procedures on the monitoring of the market operations especially on the collection of monthly rentals and how to ensure that the surcharges and interests already incurred by the vendors will be collected. Determine the deficiencies noted and take necessary steps on how to improve the monitoring and collection system.

d. Stop the practice of allowing the original lessees of market lots to sub-lease their constructed buildings to other business operators.
observations.managementAction:
The management admitted they had imposed the penalty for late payments in the last quarter of CY 2018 and some delinquent vendors requested deferment of payments of monthly rentals to be paid on renewal of their business permits on CY 2019. 

There was no transfer of rights transpired  for the market stalls for CY 2018.

They tried to enforce some procedures in attempting to increase on collection of market rentals but still fell short of their expectation.
observations.reason:
Management consented to vendors’ personal consideration
and due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Officials strictly monitor the implementation of the projects as targeted and planned. 

Determine the reason/s for the delay or non-implementation of the projects, as the case may be, in order to determine the next steps to be taken by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to implement all the  projects as at yearend..

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly follow the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004, particularly on the use of tarpaulin signboards in accordance with the prescribed specifications, and the posting of the required information on such signboards.
observations.managementAction: Tarpaulins were set up but compliance on providing complete information of programs/projects/ activities (PPAs) was not adhered to by Management.
observations.reason: The manage ment have not closely monitored the implementation with regards to the require ments on the information and publicity of programs/ projects/ activities pursuant to the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004. However, assurance was made by management  for full disclosure on information in publicity in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to submit the consolidated GPBs of the Municipality to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) to ensure the alignment of the Municipality’s GAD PPAs to the priorities of the province, and then from PPDO to the DILG Regional Office for its review and approval within the schedule set under JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA. 

Ensure also that a copy of the GAD AR, together with its complete supporting documents, are submitted to the COA Audit Team for proper evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The management already submitted the Gender and Development Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO and the corresponding  GAD Accomplishment Report to COA.

observations.recommendation:
Avoid the practice of delaying remittances of the various trust liabilities since these are budgeted and included in the annual appropriations of the municipality. 
  
Instruct the personnel-in-charge of the trust liability accounts to conduct monthly reconciliation of same to determine the correct amounts that should be remitted within the prescribed period provided in the applicable rules and regulations so that the concerned government agencies could utilize the funds due them for their programmed activities and incurrence of penalties, interest and surcharges by the municipality relative thereto could be avoided. 

      If there are penalties and surcharges incurred for late remittances, the same should not be charged against the funds of the municipality instead it should be treated as personal liability of the personnel responsible for the delay.
observations.managementAction:
The management already made remittance before deadlines set by each GOCCs. 

Even after remittance, balances  representing unremitted  deductions still appearing in the subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: There were instances that management did not observe the timely submission of required personnel data. Also, there were times that management did submit the necessary data but the concerned GOCC have not updated/rectified its own data base.

observations.recommendation:
Exhaust all measures to collect receivables from the grantees of credit and financing program even those receivables from NGAS and other receivables.
    
Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue regularly statement of accounts and prompt verification of accounts in the books of the creditors.        Make entries to Prior Year’s Adjustments on the amount pertaining to those accounts which recovery is already impossible due to death or disability fully supported with necessary supporting documents.  

Reclassify and adjust accounts in consideration of unrecorded repayments to reflect the correct balances duly confirmed with the concerned debtors.  

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of receivables using the billing statements from the Accounting Department in order to recover government funds and utilize collection to finance the present thrusts and priorities of the Municipality.

Exhaust all measures to gather the documents pertaining to the receivables and collect from debtors.

There should a meeting between the LGU concerned and the loan recipients , to sit down and confer together in order to discuss matters of collection and payments to finally settle the accounts and the possibility of continuing this financing scheme in a more effective way for the benefit of the LGU and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management had already sent demand letters to various accountable officers.
observations.reason: The concerned accountable officers still refuse to settle their obligations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to remit on time the shares of the Provincial Government on the additional 1% tax on real property accruing to the Special Education Fund so that the Provincial Government can timely utilize the collections to finance various projects, programs, and activities beneficial to the constituents of the Province of Compostela Valley. Observe religiously the deadline set for the remittance of provincial shares which falls on every 15th day of the following month as mandated.
observations.managementAction: The management still incurred delays in the remittance of shares of the Provincial Government on the additional 1% tax on real property accruing to the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: This was overlooked by manage ment but pledged to impede the incurrence of delays in its remittance in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government to prepare and enter into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the recipient beneficiaries of the agricultural, fisheries and seaweed projects under the Bottom-Up Budgeting Program. Make sure that the duties and responsibilities of binding parties are clearly defined and established in order to at least ensure the smooth implementation, effectiveness, and sustainability of the projects. Stop the practice of not entering into a MOA with the intended beneficiaries when implementing priority programs and projects.

Follow up the delivery of the remaining 16 cattle to ensure the success of the cattle production project. Impose appropriate liquidated damages for the delayed deliveries.
observations.managementAction: The management still failed to prepare and enter into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the recipient beneficiaries of the agricultural, fisheries and seaweed projects.
observations.reason: The Municipal Agricultural Officer claimed that they have no funds for the expenses for notarial services in the preparation of the MOA. However, he was advised to request for such charges in the Office of the Mayor.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that management
take the following courses of action:
 Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of
Municipal properties, and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE.
 Require the Inventory committee in coordination with the Municipal
Accounting and Municipal Treasury Office continue to exert efforts to locate
possible sources of available records to determine the existence and validity of
PPE’s in pursuit of the conduct of annual physical count.
 Designate a Property Officer who will man the recording and management of
properties as well as the preparation of property cards by each class of PPE.
Likewise, the Municipal Accounting Office shall maintain PPELC and RPLC to
effectively and efficiently monitor the movements of PPE account balance;
 Instruct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer assist the Municipal
Accounting Office in recording all real properties owned by the Municipality;
 Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Municipality’s budget a
fund for land titling of various lots acquired through purchased and donations
that were not yet transferred in the name of the Municipality. Likewise, strictly
instruct the Municipal Assessors Office to monitor the progress of the same,
and;
 Require the Municipal Accountant to de-recognize the value of unserviceable
properties from the balance of the PPE account to fairly present the account in
the financial statements. Likewise, Municipal Government shall immediately
dispose those properties in order prevent further deterioration and loss of value
of the same.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to prepare an updated assessment roll of
taxable properties as a basis of Municipal Treasury Office to compute amount of
taxes due and demandable for the year. Likewise, instruct the Municipal
Accountant to set-up the correct receivable account in the books of accounts of Real
Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables. Lastly, the Municipal
Accounting and Treasury Office shall conduct periodic reconciliation of balances of
the aforesaid accounts.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive:
 Require the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary adjusting entry to
recognize the inventory items which were already consumed and or distributed
to end users at the end of the year.
 Designate a Property Custodian that will prepare and submit a weekly SSMI to
the OIC-Municipal Accountant within three days after the end of the week for
reconciliation.
 Require the inventory committee to conduct physical count of inventory items
once per semester and prepare the report using the prescribed form, the Report
on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and to submit to the Audit Team
not later than July 31 and December 31, of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively.

We recommended that management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the discrepancy between the balances of construction in progress-
infrastructure asset account vis-à-vis to the amount of contract costs of the
completed infrastructure asset in order to recognize the same as an asset with
specific account in the statement of financial position. Further, instruct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation of the said asset account from the
time the said infrastructure asset have been completed until the reporting period so
that the balance of PPE accounts is fairly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasury Office as the designated GSO with complete description
and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Require the Inventory Committee spearheaded by the MTO to conduct the
Physical Count of Road Network System and prepare the corresponding report,
and thereafter,
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks
equivalent to the required transition provision of 50% at the end of 2017
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the
Municipal Treasury Office to strictly require all stall owners to enter into a lease
contract and imposed the payment of rights fee before occupying any stall in the
market. We likewise recommended that the LCE request the SB to enact an
ordinance instituting Market Code in order that the Treasury personnel can
enforce the delinquent stall owner to fully paid their rights fee.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare adjusting entries to record the amount of rental dues as well as the fines
and penalties as of December 31, 2017 as income from market operation by
debiting to account of Operating Lease Receivable (1-03-02-010), and crediting to
Receipts from Market Operations (4-02-02-150) and Fines and Penalties-Business
Income (4-02-02-980) accounts pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts under
COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall analyze the market records to
recognize additional revenues accruing to the Municipality from stall owners with
unpaid rental fees and penalties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Engineer and Municipal Planning Development Officer to issue Certificate of Final
Acceptance only after one (1) year defects liability had lapsed and after conducting
final inspection to determine the presence of any defects for which the concerned
contractor may be held liable according to warranty provisions in the contract in
accordance with the provisions of Section 62.2.1-2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of
R.9184.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
Prepare and submit the individual PPMPs duly evaluated and reviewed by the
Municipal Budget Office to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation;
Prepare the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) to guide the agency in the
procurement of goods and services to minimize the use of shopping as separate
alternative method of procurement. Likewise, approved APP shall be
inconformity with the prescribed format to reflect procurement activities that it
plans to undertake within the calendar year;
Assign individual/office/department who shall be in-charged with the
procurement of all common-use supplies and materials of the Municipality and;
Require the regular purchase of various office supplies through check
disbursements payable directly to the supplier/creditor inconformity with
Section 93 of PD 1445. Reimbursements should be allowed only when the need
for the items purchased is indeed exceptionally urgent and the amount should
not exceed ₱1,000.00 per procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the BAC
Secretariat to post all Notices of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved
contracts within the prescribed date/period for all procurements conducted either
through public bidding and/or alternative methods of procurement, inconformity
with the revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended the management instruct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to submit the copies of contracts and purchase orders together
with their supporting documents within the prescribed period as required under
Section 3.1.1 to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasury Office and the Municipal
Engineering Office responsible for the acceptance of deliveries shall notify the COA
of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries for ocular inspection and
verification. In addition, copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports should be
submitted within 24 hours upon acceptance as mandated by the regulation.

We recommended that management:
Seek for the assistance of Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to create a law or ordinance
declaring the two public utilities as Local Economic Enterprise as stated in
Section 105, Volume I of Manual for NGAs for LGUs. The said law or
ordinance will be the basis of Local Finance Committee to charge the
administrative costs, material costs and other related expenses in maintenance
and preservation, improvement and operation of the public utilities as Local
Economic Enterprise (LEE), not in 20% DF and;
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate account for the two
LEE to determine whether the income generated by the economic enterprises
are sufficient to meet their respective operating costs and to provide adequate
information as to the assets, liabilities and equity of each special account as
prescribed in Section 106 of the same Manual.

We recommended that management fill up the mandatory positions in
the LDRRMO by hiring qualified personnel. Priority shall be given to job
orders assigned in the office or other offices within the Municipality, in
accordance with CSC-COA-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
In the event of budget limitation, management may opt to augment staff of the
LDRRMO through any combinations provided under Section 5.5 of NDRRMC-
DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all Municipal
Officials concerned to stop the practice of the charging expenses to LDRRMF
which do not directly support the implementation of disaster risk reduction
management activities. Administrative expenses shall be charged against the
Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) under the General Fund
(GF) in accordance with Section 4.3 NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, through the LDRRM
Council and LDC to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF monthly, to be submitted on or before 15th day of the following
month, certified correct by the Municipal Accountant as prescribed in the
abovementioned JMC.

We recommended that management:
Instruct the concerned offices to establish a solid waste management plan that
includes programs, projects and activities with environmentally-sound methods
of solid waste disposal to ensure protection of public health and environment.
The said plan shall be submitted immediately to Environmental Management
Bureau (EMB) of the regional office for review and approval;
Require the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to allocate sufficient funds
to convert the existing open dump site into controlled dump site pursuant to the
abovementioned regulations. Likewise, ensure to get an Environmental
Compliance Certificate to the EMB in the operation of dump site to avoid
penalties and sanctions that will impose by the bureau, and;
Request the Sangguniang Bayan to legislate appropriate ordinances to aid the
executive department in the implementation of the proposed programs, projects
and activities under the Municipality’s SWMP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the members of
MCPC to prepare all required administrative reports which will be the basis of
Sangguniang Bayan to provide as sufficient budget allocation to strengthen the
implementation and monitoring of proposed programs, projects, activities funded
by the one (1) percent component of IRA.

We recommended that management establish GAD Database that will serve
as their basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation
and a GAD Code that will support the Municipality’s efforts in promoting,
protecting and fulfilling women's human rights, women's economic empowerment
and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and
women's empowerment.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
Institute a human resource policy or guidelines to limit the numbers of
personnel hired based only on the needs of the LGU;
Likewise, execute a contract in the engagement of services of job orders with a
well-defined functions, responsibilities, expected output and contract duration
to facilitate proper evaluation of their work performed, and;
Ensure that payment of wages of job orders are supported by a complete
documentation as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure
propriety, legality and validity of their aforesaid claims. Non-submission of the
aforesaid documents shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the management stop allowing personnel hired
through job orders and contract of service in attending trainings, seminars while
paying any expenses incurred relative to their attendance of the same. Continuous
non-compliance thereof shall automatically be DISALLOWED in audit.

We recommended that management strictly follow the guidelines provided
under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to avoid possible
administrative and or criminal sanction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require all
department heads concern to strictly comply with the full disclosure policy
required by the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-140, to ensure
transparency on the Municipality’s financial operations and transactions as well as
to allow the Municipality’s constituents to understand how their local government’s
budget were spent for public services.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the
Municipal Treasury Office to exert more effort in the collection of taxes due and
collectible. In this regard, the MTO may institute other mechanisms to force
delinquent taxpayers to pay promptly their real property taxes such as sending
notices of delinquency or by creating a committee that will conduct an information
dissemination regarding the consequences of non-payment of taxes.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the Inventory
Committee conduct a complete, accurate and dependable physical count of all
municipal Property, Plant and Equipment and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE
and reconcile with the accounting records to ensure the existence and reliability of the
accounts presented in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider establishment of
the General Services Office with sufficient staff for procurement, property
management and general services who shall perform the functions enumerated
under Section 490 of RA 7160.

Time and again, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
following officials:
a. Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT and SET Receivable at the gross
amount of collectible taxes at 1% each and prepare subsidiary ledgers or
any schedules for the RPT and SET Receivables accounts that will
