support the outstanding balances of the accounts presented in the
financial statements; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the establishment of reliable balances
of RPT and SET Receivables of the Municipality by updating the Real
Property Unit Cards of each taxpayer and preparing the list of
delinquent real property taxes at year-end and providing the same to the
Municipal Accountant for finalization of financial statements thereof and
to the Provincial Government for issuance of notice of levy. After which,
reconcile the list against the recorded RPT and SET Receivables to
present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the
financial statements;
Being a first class municipality, we further recommended that the Municipal
Mayor consider computerization of the Treasurer’s Office and Assessor’s Office to
generate consistent and reliable reports on real property and special education taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the
material discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due from and Due to LGU
of the Municipality as of December 31, 2017. Submit to the Audit Team the Journal
Entry Vouchers (JEVs) of the adjustments made.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that, as agreed,
the necessary documents are timely furnished to the other party for proper
recording so as to avoid such material discrepancies. However, in future
reconciliation especially at year-end, the Municipal Accountant must make follow-up
in writing for easier, documented verification of discrepancies and to elicit
immediate response and action.

We recommended that the concerned Municipal officials ensure that there
were appropriations for overtime expenses before approving its payment.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer to check and verify
the entries in the CAFOA and ensure that all expenses incurred are
requested for obligation under appropriations allotted for such purpose;
b. the Budget Office post entries in the RAAO based on approved
obligations in the CAFOA; and
c. the Municipal Accountant record expenses in proper accounts prescribed
in and compliant with PPSAS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Agriculturist, Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to prioritize
and take action on previous year’s recommendations relative to these long
outstanding receivables. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant
provide disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements regarding the probability or
status of recovery of such receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials and
employees to refund the amount of ₱35,000.00 each or a total of ₱4,700,500.00.
For full adoption of PRAISE providing concrete basis for future grants of
allowances and incentives, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create the PRAISE Committee and Grievance Machinery Committee, and
require them to perform their specific responsibilities enumerated in the
Municipality’s PRAISE Model;
b. consider establishment of a performance management team that shall
ensure alignment of office performance targets and measures established
with those of the Municipality and the rational distribution of work
among offices/units, recommend approval of the office performance
ratings, act as appeals body for performance management issues the
agency, identify potential top performers and provide inputs to the PRAISE
Committee for grant of awards and incentives; and
c. ensure that the rules, specific policies and guidelines in performance
planning, monitoring and evaluation and mechanism for recognizing the
awardees are in place before granting awards to the officials and
employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
submit justification/comment on why the expenses were allowed and obligated
against amounts realigned without enactment through ordinance.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer that all changes in the approved annual budget for the Executive Sector be
proposed to the Sanggunian Bayan and seek their approval thru enacted ordinance.
We also recommended the Sanggunian Bayan to ensure that all changes in the
budget it has originally approved are properly enacted thru ordinance. Otherwise,
such realignment/reversion/augmentation even within the same expense class shall
not be deemed effected and no obligations can further be charged thereto.
In the absence of any document to prove any appropriations ordinance or law relative
to the realignment against which the ₱7,534,625.00 expenditures were charged, said
disbursements shall not be allowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services
Officer-Designate to:
a. meticulously and judiciously plan procurement for each quarter as
consolidated and incorporate them in the Annual Procurement Plan
(APP) in order to adopt competitive public bidding to provide reasonable
assurance that the best quality at the lowest price is availed; and
b. comply with the regulations that procurement of ordinary and regular
office supplies and equipment involving amounts below the threshold
should first be procured from DBM. Shopping from private suppliers
should be resorted to only for items not available in the PS-DBM. Thus,
in case shopping is resorted to, the claim should be supported with a
certification from the PS-DBM that the items to be procured are not
available in their stock.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer
and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all
on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPA) and those that are to be
implemented during the year, and during every quarter thereafter, in compliance
with the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of the
Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the
safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those
for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of
ten (10) years as mandated by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue the practice of granting
cash advances to Department of Education officials and employees and designate a
permanent employee of the Municipality as Special Disbursing Officer to perform
disbursing functions out of Special Education Fund. Henceforth, adhere strictly
with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant and utilization of cash
advances.
We also recommended that the Local School Board authorize the Municipal
Treasurer to disburse funds from the Special Education Fund pursuant Republic
Act 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board
evaluate and review the SEF budget with emphasis on the proper identification and
selection of priority programs and projects suitable to the needs of the public
schools in the Municipality and come up with a Procurement/Investment Plan to
support the SEF budget.
We also recommended that the Local School Board approve the SEF budget
that is presented by program, activity and project (P/A/P), by allotment class and
per school using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 pursuant to DepEd-DBM-
DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017. Ensure that the SEF
budget is net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget
for the Department of Education for a more strategic and efficient utilization of
resources to better support the supplementary budgetary needs for the operation
and maintenance of public schools within the municipality.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office which requires of at least one
LDRRM Officer and three DRRM staff pursuant to RA 10121 and NDRRMC-
DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer make clear the source
of appropriation for the improvement of waterworks in Malimatoc II. Otherwise,
the excess utilization without appropriation shall be disallowed in audit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit to the
local sanggunian through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Council and Local Development Council the annual LDRRMO Plan and budget
specifying in detail the list of programs, activities and projects which include under
a separate caption those projects and programs charged to the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years, and implement at the intended period of its
implementation to ensure that the purpose for which the fund was established are
met.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to
provide the specific guidelines in selecting and identifying the qualified beneficiaries
together with the amount to be granted for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to update
its master list indicating the economic status of each senior citizen and include all
the eligible and qualified senior citizens in order of priority provided under the
DSWD Administrative Order No. 15 series of 2010 dated December 13, 2010.
Likewise, conduct quarterly validation and monitoring to the beneficiaries to
determine their situation and the utilization of the stipend for the full
implementation of Social Pension Program of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to furnish
the Audit Team of the complete master list submitted to DSWD and provide proper
justification on the 22 beneficiaries that did not receive assistance but listed in the
master list.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the financial statements in compliance with the requirements
of PPSAS including the required reconciliation to be disclosed in the Notes to
Financial Statements.

We recommend that Management require the immediate liquidation of the
outstanding cash advance balances granted as provided for in Section 5.1 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445. Moreover, send
individual letter of demand to every officer and employee, enjoining immediate
liquidation of their long outstanding cash advances.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant identify the recipients and
the specific amounts granted to each organization/association and institute actions
for its prompt settlement and liquidation. And also, direct the Municipal
Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all account.

We recommend that Management direct all accounting office personnel to
assist the current municipal accountant in the identification of the receivables of the
municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after exhausting all
possible means to substantiate the account.

We recommend that Management require the Office of the Treasurer and
the Accounting Section to maintain and update regularly property cards and
property ledger cards, and immediately reconcile any variance detected between the
accounting and property records. Moreover, direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to determine the causes of the discrepancies between the two
(2) records to be able to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to present
fairly the property, plant and equipment account balances in the financial statements.

We recommend that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and
the Supply Officer Designate to prepare the Inventory an Inspection Report of
Unserviceable/Unused Properties and Equipment and facilitate the immediate
disposal thereof in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.
92-386.

We recommend that the Management allocate funds in the annual budget for
the processing of the Torrens Title for all lots owned by the municipality. We
further recommend that Management designate a personnel to work on the titling of
these land.

We recommend that Management conduct physical inventory count of the
Work/Zoo Animals recorded in the books and to submit to the Auditor the report
on the physical count thereof not later than January 31 of each year in accordance
with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. Likewise, we further recommend
that the Municipal Accountant maintain the Work, Other Animals and Breeding
Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) as provided under Section 114 of the NGAS
Manual.

We recommend that Management submit written sufficient justification in
pursuing the conduct of a fund raising activity despite the absence of a resolution
from the Local Sanggunian authorizing the collection of fees, otherwise personnel
involved thereto will be made to answer accordingly.
We also recommend that Management refrain from conducting similar
activities and charging fees without the approval and authority from the Local
Sanggunian pursuant to Section 132 of the Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommend that Management refrain from incurring expenditures for
the Organic Demonstration Farm until an ordinance regulating the overall
operations of the economic enterprise shall have been crafted and passed by the
Local Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommend that management explain why the projects were provided in
generic terms and do not contain sufficient details and cost of each project. Henceforth,
require from the Local Development Council a list of duly identified projects in the
formulation of future annual budgets.

We recommend that Management strictly comply with the provisions of
Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and the related DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the prompt and full utilization of the 20%
Development Fund for the implementation of priority development projects in order
to optimally attain the socio-economic and environmental development of the
municipality.

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to comply with the
guidelines issued under COA Circular No. 2013-004 relative to the information and
publicity of the Programs/Projects/Activities implemented by the municipality
particularly the submission of monthly updates and quarterly reports on
government projects, and the display of signboards in conspicuous places in every
location of the PPAs, irrespective of amount.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the
OIC Municipal Treasurer to release timely the 30% share of the RPT income due to
barangays and see to it that all remaining balances as of end of the year shall have
been wholly released in order to fast track the implementation of various programmed
projects in the barangay level.

We recommend that the municipality fast tract the development of a
Sanitary Landfill/Dumpsite as a final garbage disposal site of the local government
unit for the protection of the health and environment hazards of its constituents.
We further recommend that Management activate its Solid Waste
Management Board to ensure the viability and effective implementation of the Local
Government Ecological Waste Management Plan as required under Republic Act
No. 9003.

We recommend the Management appropriate one percent (1%) of the
Internal Revenue Allotment of the municipality for the strengthening and
implementation of the programs of the Local Council for the Protection of Children.
Also, prepare and submit the necessary reports enumerated in Items No. 1 of Part II
of the above-quoted DILG Memorandum Circular for the proper and effective
implementation of programs, projects and activities.
We also recommend that Management maximized the use of funds duly
appropriated for Local Council for the Protection of Children in pursuance to
Republic Act 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

In view thereof, we recommended that Management:
Integrate in the financial reports the qualitative characteristics such as reliability and comparability, in order to be useful to wide range of decision makers;
Provide only relevant but sufficient information, may it be narrative and descriptive and/or corroborating schedules in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
Ensure that the submitted reports are free from substantial inconsistencies that undermine reliability of information.

In view of the above audit observations, we recommended to the management to:
Strictly comply with the provision of Section 22(c) of RA 7160.
Inform and as much as possible, require the Municipal Development Council to propose projects for implementation that are specific and sufficiently detailed.
In cases where there are projects, or goods and services mentioned in generic terms, properly secure the required authorization/approval of the Sangguniang Bayan Council before entering into any contract.

We recommended that the members of the Local School Board (LSB) see to it that the Special Education Fund should be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures in adherence to the provisions of DepEd-DBM_DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s 2017 dated January 19, 2017. Prioritize only those expenditures which promote the quality of education and those programs which support the primary needs of the students within the municipality. Inform also the DepEd District Office of the expenditures which could not be anymore charged against the SEF budget of the Municipal Government to preclude the issuance of audit suspension/disallowance.

We recommended that the Management direct the members of the LCPC to prepare the Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC). Ensure that all PPAs under the said plans are amply provided with funds, and that all are implemented in adherence to the provisions of DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. Also, engage in partnership with private organizations, establishments, and entities that provide financial assistance for the implementation of LCPC programs, projects and activities.

We recommended to the Management to:
Exert more effort in observing the timely implementation of the PPAs.
Monitor the status of the implementation of the projects especially those PPAs enumerated in the AIP.
Revise the AIP as often as necessary especially when for those PPAs which could not be implemented during the year. This should be brought up in a meeting with the Municipal Development Council so that appropriate actions could be done.

We recommended to the Management to:
Implement Section 296(a) of the RRC on dealing with the delinquent market vendors for the timely collection of market rentals and to prevent the eventual increase of uncollected surcharges and interests.
To execute the provisions of Section 301 in awarding the declared vacant market stall through the adjudication process. The same process is expected to be also applied when there is transfer of rights over the market stall from previous occupant to the new one.
Revisit the steps/procedures on the monitoring of the market operations especially on the collection of monthly rentals and how to ensure that the surcharges and interests already incurred by the vendors will be collected. Determine the deficiencies noted and take necessary steps on how to improve the monitoring and collection system.
Stop the practice of allowing the original lessees of market lots to sub-lease their constructed buildings to other business operators.
AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Management failed to create an inventory committee and locate possible sources of available records of acquired PPE’s since there was no executive order was issued for the creation of inventory committee. The OIC-Property Custodian and the OIC- Municipal Accountant complete and update their property records and equipment ledger cards, respectively, in order to facilitate the verification of the PPE account. The OIC-Property Custodian be also required to issue PARs for all equipment to be issued to the end-users of the Municipality for control purposes pursuant to existing government regulations.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Resignation of designated Property Officer/ Custodian and absence of complete and update their property records and equipment ledger cards added to the reason of non-implementation of the same.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury Office provided the Municipal Accounting Office an estimated collectibles for the year based on the targets given by the BLGF.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Preparation of payment is liable to procurement of equipment and other goods is made on the basis of the value of delivered items. However, payment of Audit Disallowance on equipment and other goods is made on the basis of the value of delivered items. Payment is made on the basis of the value of delivered items. However, payment of Audit Disallowance is only partially paid.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Prepare and submit annual LDRRMFIP was fully complied.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Management ensures 30% QRF will be used based on the guidelines provided by the oversight agencies.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Programs and Projects charged to the SEF budget were strictly in accordance with the prescribed rules and regulations in the utilization of SEF.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Preparation of 10-year solid waste management plan is on-going. Management promised to submit to Environmental Management Bureau (EMB) Regional Office No. 1, hence reiterated in Part II under Observation No. 16, page 60 of this report.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: All insurable properties were covered/insured to GF the GIF of the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accounting Office strictly followed the recording of transactions in accordance with the PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Management promised to specify the projects funded under 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Preparation of Program of Works are made only to some projects funded under 20% DF, Hence, reiterated in Part II under Observation No. 24, page 75 of this report.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Management promised to establish GAD database and GAD Code. Prepared Annual GAD Plan and Budget was submitted to the DILG Regional Office No. 1, Hence endorsement of the same office.

observations.recommendation: AAR 2016
observations.managementAction: Optimum utilization of 20% DF. Management promised utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with the guidelines set forth by the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office effect the necessary reclassification entries and observe proper recording of transactions in consonance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to provide fair financial statement presentation. In addition, for those projects funded with LGU counterpart that were already completed, revert the residual amounts not utilized to the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The agency already effected the reclassification entries and observe proper recording of transactions in consonance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to provide fair financial statement presentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Officials strictly monitor the implementation of the projects as targeted and planned. Determine the reason/s for the delay or non-implementation of the projects, as the case may be, in order to determine the next steps to be taken by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented already the 2 projects while the 4 projects were not entirely accomplished as at year end.
observations.reason:
The manage-ment claimed that due to electrical problems and obstruction in the
installation of pipings because it happened on the site of the on-going construction of roads, the 2 infrastructure projects were not finished as at year end. For the  other 2 unfinished projects, they alleged that they have difficulty in determining the beneficiaries while original  prices for supplies and materials in program of works (POW) is not already its current price, thus it needs the POW to be revised.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to limit the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to the programs and projects enumerated under DILG and DBM JMC 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and to ensure strict monitoring of the disbursements to preclude the issuance of audit suspensions/disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The management already observed the utilization of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the programs and projects enumerated under DILG and DBM JMC 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMC comprehensively and periodically review the LDRRM plan of the agency, taking into consideration also the utilization of LDRRMF (from current year appropriation and previous years' unexpended balances) to finance programs/projects/activities which will contribute to the resiliency of the municipality against calamities and disasters.
observations.managementAction: The agency already maximized the utilization of the unexpended balances of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF).

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and BAC-Technical Working Group (TWG) to ensure that the required documents for procurement are properly filled by the procuring entity and the prospective and winning bidders. Strictly require the winning bidders to indicate the dates when the documents such as Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed are actually received. In addition, the management and winning bidders are cautioned to observe the guidelines under RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The management already abides on the guidelines in the procurement of projects under RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly follow the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004, particularly on the use of tarpaulin signboards in accordance with the prescribed specifications, and the posting of the required information on such signboards.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government did not constantly implemented the requirements on the information and publicity of programs/projects/ activities.
observations.reason: The manage ment have not closely monitored the implementation with regards to the require ments on the information and publicity of programs/ projects/ activities pursuant to the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officers and the Accountant strictly adhere to the mandatory period of submission of financial reports and accounts pursuant to PD No. 1445 and other pertinent COA regulations to ensure the timely review and verification of accounts of the agency. Failure to comply will be a sufficient ground for the suspension of payment of salaries as provided for under Section 122 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management already adhered to the mandatory period of submission of financial reports and accounts pursuant to PD No. 1445 and other pertinent COA regulations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to provide the necessary information to strictly comply with current accounting standards especially on the presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The accounts were already rectified by management and the necessary information were already provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to limit the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to the programs and projects enumerated under DILG and DBM JMC 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Ensure strict monitoring of the disbursements against the 20% Development Fund. In addition, properly plan and allocate more funds for projects which have more impact to the development to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The management already observed the utilization of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the programs and projects enumerated under DILG and DBM JMC 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Government to strictly comply with the regulations provided in R.A 9184 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 on the grant of honoraria to officers/employees involved in government procurement. Refund the excess amount of BAC honorarium claimed and ensure that the subsequent claims for BAC honorarium are adequately supported with the necessary documents.
observations.managementAction: Concerned members of Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat, and BAC Technical Working Group (TWG) already refunded the excess payments of BAC Honoraria of P454,460.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Officials concerned to secure the proper government official receipt when paying registration fees in attending conferences, seminars or assemblies sponsored by their respective leagues of elective officials.

Stop the practice of accepting privately printed Official Receipts instead of the Government Official Receipt (AF 51) when attending conferences/ seminars sponsored by a Government Organization such as the Philippine. Councilors' League, Liga ng mga Barangays, Mayors' League, etc.
observations.managementAction: Notices of Settlement of Suspension/ Disallowance/ Charges (NSSDCs) were already issued to the concerned persons responsible for the lifting of the previously issued Notices of Suspension (NS).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to provide the necessary information to strictly comply with current accounting standards especially on the presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The accounts were already rectified by management and the necessary information were already provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to submit the consolidated GPBs of the Municipality to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) to ensure the alignment of the Municipality’s GAD PPAs to the priorities of the province, and then from PPDO to the DILG Regional Office for its review and approval within the schedule set under JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA. Ensure also that a copy of the GAD AR, together with its complete supporting documents, are submitted to the COA Audit Team for proper evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The management already submitted the Gender and Development Plan and Budget (GPB) for CY 2017 but the corresponding 2017 GAD Accomplishment Report was not yet submitted.
observations.reason: The manage ment assured that they will submit the 2017 GAD Accomplishment Report as soon as possible in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government direct the Municipal Property Inventory Team to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the Municipality at least once a year and prepare inventory report thereof using the prescribed form – Report on the Physical Count of Plant, Property and Equipment (RCPPE). The report must be properly accomplished and copy of such must be furnished to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation purposes.
observations.managementAction: The management already submitted the Inventory Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2017.
The management had already prepared the DRRM Implementing Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management intensify their collections from market block/space/lot rentals, especially on the delinquent vendors, and strictly follow the imposition of penalty or surcharges as clearly stipulated in the terms and condition of the contract. Replace the delinquent vendors if they are not willing to settle their obligations with other qualified parties who are interested to establish business in the area.

We recommend also that the management monitor closely the collections from the Market and Terminal operations so that the corresponding expenditures could be controlled and minimized.                                     Enjoin the Market Supervisor to prepare and submit monthly status report on collections especially in the market operations. Reshuffle collectors on regular basis to avoid hesitancy in the collection of market space rentals.
observations.managementAction: Although management increased the market rental collections in CY 2017, there are still lapses that hinders its operations that affected their collection efficiency.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Government, the immediate usage of donated lot for the operation and management of Eco-Park with Alternative Technology Approach for its final waste disposal and for the purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes to sustain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan.

Prioritization of the funding for these properties should be immediately done so that the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste would not be hampered.

We recommended also the stoppage of the operation of the present disposal site and the strict implementation of the waste segregation policy of the Municipality from the source, in order to successfully attain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government already utilize the donated lot for the operation and management of Eco-Park. The purchase of new equipment for collection of waste was not materialized as at year end.
observations.reason: The manage ment is still complying the necessary papers for the application of loan for the purchase of additional equipment for use in their Solid Waste Management Program.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Property Inventory Team to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the Municipality at least once a year, prepare inventory report thereof using the prescribed form – Report on the Physical Count of Plant, Property and Equipment (RCPPE), properly accomplished, and furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the report for reconciliation purposes. All unserviceable equipment or properties should be included in the physical count. Then a report of all unserviceable properties using the form – Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) should be prepared and be submitted to the Office of the COA Auditor, together with the request of the Local Chief Executive for inspection and appraisal. This will serve as basis for possible disposal and/or dropping from the books of accounts.

Further,  additional personnel should be assigned  both at the Offices of designated MGSO and of the Municipal Accountant that would focus solely in   maintaining   and updating property cards  and property ledger cards, respectively.  Periodic reconciliation of the involved records in said Offices should be made so that necessary adjustments thereof, if there are any, would then be effected in the books of the LGU in order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management already submitted the Inventory Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
Avoid the practice of delaying remittances of the various trust liabilities since these are budgeted and included in the annual appropriations of the municipality. 
 
Instruct the personnel-in-charge of the trust liability accounts to conduct monthly reconciliation of same to determine the correct amounts that should be remitted within the prescribed period provided in the applicable rules and regulations so that the concerned government agencies could utilize the funds due them for their programmed activities and incurrence of penalties, interest and surcharges by the municipality relative thereto could be avoided. 

      If there are penalties and surcharges incurred for late remittances, the same should not be charged against the funds of the municipality instead it should be treated as personal liability of the personnel responsible for the delay.
observations.managementAction: The agency is still reconciling the actual amount and to whom these are to be credited if remitted. There are also those who have deductions made on premiums but were not also remitted to the concerned GOCCs.
observations.reason: The agency attributed some of these deficiencies during the time when the Department of Budget and Manage ment made outright deductions on premiums due to the GSIS. Some employees have not submitted the required data, thus no remittance were made because these will not be accepted by the system operated procedure in remittance. Also, the manage ment is having a hard time tracing the various creditors for the BIR remittance due to the absence also of pertinent records.

observations.recommendation:
Exhaust all measures to collect receivables from the grantees of credit and financing program even those receivables from NGAS and other receivables.
    Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue regularly statement of accounts and prompt verification of accounts in the books of the creditors.        Make entries to Prior Year’s Adjustments on the amount pertaining to those accounts which recovery is already impossible due to death or disability fully supported with necessary supporting documents.  Reclassify and adjust accounts in consideration of unrecorded repayments to reflect the correct balances duly confirmed with the concerned debtors.  

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of receivables using the billing statements from the Accounting Department in order to recover government funds and utilize collection to finance the present thrusts and priorities of the Municipality.

Exhaust all measures to gather the documents pertaining to the receivables and collect from debtors.
There should a meeting between the LGU concerned and the loan recipients , to sit down and confer together in order to discuss matters of collection and payments to finally settle the accounts and the possibility of continuing this financing scheme in a more effective way for the benefit of the LGU and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management had already sent demand letters to various accountable officers and was able to collect the amount of P7,297.00 for CY 2017.
observations.reason: Refusal by concerned debtors to pay their obligations.

observations.recommendation: Give utmost priority to the titling of the lots which are utilized by the Municipality in order to ensure ownership over said lands and to avoid possible damage to government properties by unwanted transfer through eviction.
observations.managementAction: The documentary requirements of the titling of the lots are already submitted to the concerned agencies.
observations.reason: The required documents were already submitted to the concerned agencies for the titling of lots.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that:
The Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of Municipal properties, and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE.
The Inventory Committee in coordination with the Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasury Office locate possible sources of available records to determine the existence and validity of PPE’s in pursuit of the conduct of annual physical count.
The OIC-Property Custodian and the OIC-Municipal Accountant complete and update their property records and equipment ledger cards, respectively, in order to facilitate the verification and reconciliation of the PPE account. The OIC-Property Custodian be also required to issue Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) for all equipment to be issued to the end-users of the Municipality for control purposes pursuant to existing government regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the inventory items which were already consumed and or distributed to end users at the end of the year.
Require the OIC-Property Custodian to prepare and submit the weekly SSMI for all supplies and materials issued to end-users to the OIC-Municipal Accountant within three days after the end of the week for recording purposes in the books of accounts.
Conduct physical count of inventory items once per semester and prepare the report using the prescribed form, the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and to submit to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and December 31, of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
Require the OIC-Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain stock cards and supplies ledger cards to facilitate reconciliation and verification of the inventory account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to prepare an updated assessment roll of taxable properties as a basis of Municipal Treasurer’s Office to compute the correct amount of taxes due and demandable for the year. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to set-up the correct receivable account in the books of accounts, pursuant to Volume I Section 20 of Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs, in order that the said receivable accounts would be accurately presented in the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the concerned supplier and all other persons determined liable to settle the Audit disallowance. It is also recommended that management strictly follow the pertinent provisions of RA 7160 and RA 9184 in the procurement of equipment and other properties of the Municipality to prevent possible loss of government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRMO to prepare annually the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 which shall include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
We likewise recommended that the LDRRM Council approve the LDRRMPs and LDRRMFIP of the municipality thru a resolution where members of the council will sign for transparency and support the authority of the implementation of every project and activities set in Plan.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the regulations for utilization of 30% Quick Response Fund in accordance with 5.1.2-3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 6.3 of NDRRMC-DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended that management and the members of the Local School Board, to strictly follow the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 as well as the guidelines set forth under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01-A dated March 1, 2000 and 01-B dated June 25, 2001 in the preparation of the SEF Budget so that only the authorized priority expenditures identified shall be implemented and paid out of the fund to ensure proper utilization thereof.

We have recommended that management:
Establish a solid waste management plan that includes programs, projects and activities as well as environmentally-sound methods of solid waste disposal to ensure protection of public health and environment and that encourages the conservation and recovery of valuable resources.
Enact a Municipal Ordinance enforcing the collection of garbage fees in the entire Municipality. Similarly, a Municipal Ordinance declaring certain prohibited acts regarding solid waste disposal and impose penalties to those violators relative to the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Act shall be likewise enacted.
Implement educational campaigns against Solid Waste Management Disposal by increasing the public awareness on existing laws and regulations in attaining values of cleanliness and ensure the safety of every individual on the proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste.

We recommended that management ensure that all insurable properties and assets of the Municipality are adequately covered/insured with the GIF of the GSIS to protect the interest of the government in conformity with the Memorandum Circular of the Office of the President.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to be more prudent in recording the transactions so that correct balances of an account will be reflected in the financial statements. This will help to avoid confusion among end-users with regards to the nature of transactions recorded in each accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Planning Development Officer in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality to specify the program and projects funded out of 20% Development Fund to ensure their implementation and delivery of services to its constituents pursuant to existing government laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare program of works including the costs estimates and activities of projects to be undertaken to avoid incurrence of obligations which are not related to their implementation; and
Further, advise the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of 9184, specifically the conditions and requirements in the implementation of infrastructure projects by administration to avoid any sanctions and administrative liability.

We recommended that management:
Establish GAD Database that will serve as their basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and GAD Code that will support the Municipality’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women's human rights, women's economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women's empowerment.
Present a clear gender issue arising from the unequal status of women and men or emphasize the extent of gender disparity. Likewise, it is also recommended that the Municipality ensure that PPAs are gender responsive, relevant to the identified gender issues and their causes as well as the correctness and alignment of entries in the revised GPB form; and
Prepare Annual Accomplishment Report (AR) in the required format for proper evaluation of programs, projects and activities that had implemented.

We recommended that management promote/encourage an optimum utilization of the 20% development fund. It is also recommended that tightly monitoring of the programs and projects which were identified and prioritized under Section 3 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 should be taken by the Municipality. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineering and Municipal Planning and Development Office to efficiently and effectively implement such programs and projects to fully achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and ensure that public benefits are received timely from these projects by the constituents of the Municipality.

Require the inventory committee/team to conduct the physical inventory of
all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor in accordance with
Section of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume 1. Likewise, reconciliation between the records maintained by the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should be made and effect
necessary adjustments when appropriate.

We recommend that Management require all officials and employees
concerned to submit immediately the liquidation reports of their outstanding cash
advances and/or refund the unexpended cash balance, if any, otherwise, direct the
Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due
them and strictly enforce that no additional cash advance shall be granted until full
liquidation of the previous cash advances is made.

We also recommend that Management require the former municipal officials
to make follow-up or send tracers for the issuance of Credit Advice from the COA
Central Office and monitor the liquidation of the outstanding balance of the
Confidential/Intelligence Fund.

We recommend that management submit justification in writing on why financial
assistance under the AICS Program were granted to clients despite the absence of
supporting documents as enumerated in Section 4.2 of DSWD Memorandum
Circular No. 15, Series of 2014.

Also, direct the Municipal Social Welfare Officer to submit the records of the cases
and all other basic supporting documents relative to the claims for further
evaluation, otherwise the transactions will be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that management require the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to submit justification in writing on why another person was able to sign
official documents and represents a supplier/bidder for and in his behalf without the
later executing a duly notarized Special Power of Attorney, contrary to Section 25.3
of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We also recommend that management install, implement and monitor a sound
Internal Control System pursuant to Section 124 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommend that management instruct the LDRRM Officer to furnish a
copy of the LDRRMF Investment Plan to the Municipal Accountant-Designate for
the recording of the unexpended CYs 2014-2015 Quick Response Fund and
DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Fund as Trust Liability, and to the Budget Officer for
the recording of the DRRMF-Capital Outlay as continuing appropriation in the
SAAOB.

We also recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer-
Designate to issue check to actually set aside and transfer to the Trust Fund account
the unexpended LDRRMF for CY 2014-2015.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to remit immediately to the
PFDA the accumulated retainer’s fee totaling P45,232.73 representing 10% of the
gross income from the operations of the Barangay Baybayon Fish Port pursuant to
Paragraph 17, Article III of the MOA entered into by the LGU and the PFDA.

Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to regularly furnish
monthly and annual financial and statistical reports to PFDA as required.

We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the
Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top
of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the
activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning
local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.

We recommend that management require the Bids and Awards Committee
and other officials concerned to fast track the implementation of the Waterworks
Project funded out from the DILG Support Fund in order to deliver the intended
benefits due the public, and also, to avoid additional costs to finish the project if
implemented in the subsequent years due to constant rise of prices in construction
materials.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and
the Municipal Accountant-Designate to charge and record the wages of the
casuals/job order employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and Wages-
Casual/Contractual as prescribed in the Philippines Public Sector Accounting
Standards (PPSAS).

Moreover, minimize the hiring of casuals/job order employees as to necessity
and maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 55%
Personal Services Limitation and always observe austerity measures in the
disbursement of public funds as provided for in Administrative Order No. 103,
Series of 2004.

We recommend that management immediately stop the practice of charging
to SEF transactions outside the scope for which this fund was established and
observe the proper utilization thereof pursuant to the provisions of DECS, DBM
and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1, series of 1998, 01-A series of 2000 and 01-B series
of 2001, Section100(c) of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 4 of
DepEd Order No. 12, series of 2014.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor
the utilization and implementation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are duly
implemented. We also recommend that management faithfully adhere to the
provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely
review of the required LDRRM reports.

We recommend that the Management require the inventory committee to
conduct physical inventory of the Work/Zoo Animals/Breeding Stocks, with the
active participation of the Municipal Agricultural Officer, as a member of the
Inventory Team. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain
the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) as
provided under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.

We recommend that management require the LMP, VMLP and PCL to
submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of
receipts contrary to the Section 264 of Republic Act No. 8424 or the National
Internal Revenue Code of the Philippines.

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer not to accept the old receipts of the
leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National
Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and
registration fees.

We recommend that Management form a Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board to prepare the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the
municipality. We further recommend that funds be appropriated for the safe and
sanitary management of solid wastes generated in the LGU pursuant to Republic
Act No. 9003.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant-
Designate to submit monthly trial balances on the 20th day of the following month to
facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU, as well as submit
trial balance at the end of the quarter on time.

We recommend that the Municipal Accounting Office effect the necessary reclassification entries and observe proper recording of transactions in consonance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to provide fair financial statement presentation. In addition, for those projects funded with LGU counterpart that were already completed, revert the residual amounts not utilized to the General Fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Officials strictly monitor the implementation of the projects as targeted and planned. Determine the reason/s for the delay or non-implementation of the projects, as the case may be, in order to determine the next steps to be taken by the LGU.

We recommend to limit the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to the programs and projects enumerated under DILG and DBM JMC 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and to ensure strict monitoring of the disbursements to preclude the issuance of audit suspensions/disallowances.

We recommend that the LDRRMC comprehensively and periodically review the LDRRM plan of the agency, taking into consideration also the utilization of LDRRMF (from current year appropriation and previous years' unexpended balances) to finance programs/projects/activities which will contribute to the resiliency of the municipality against calamities and disasters.

We recommend to the Municipal Treasurer to remit on time the shares of the Provincial Government on the additional 1% tax on real property accruing to the Special Education Fund so that the Provincial Government can timely utilize the collections to finance various projects, programs, and activities beneficial to the constituents of the Province of Compostela Valley. Observe religiously the deadline set for the remittance of provincial shares which falls on every 15th day of the following month as mandated.

We recommend to the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and BAC-Technical Working Group (TWG) to ensure that the required documents for procurement are properly filled by the procuring entity and the prospective and winning bidders. Strictly require the winning bidders to indicate the dates when the documents such as Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed are actually received. In addition, the management and winning bidders are cautioned to observe the following guidelines under RA 9184:

We recommend that the Municipality strictly follow the provisions in COA Circular No. 2013-004, particularly on the use of tarpaulin signboards in accordance with the prescribed specifications, and the posting of the required information on such signboards.

We recommend that the Accountable Officers and the Accountant strictly adhere to the mandatory period of submission of financial reports and accounts pursuant to PD No. 1445 and other pertinent COA regulations to ensure the timely review and verification of accounts of the agency. Failure to comply will be a sufficient ground for the suspension of payment of salaries as provided for under Section 122 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the management require the Municipal Accountant to provide the necessary information to strictly comply with current accounting standards especially on the presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a dedicated
Inventory Committee that could focus on conducting a thorough, complete,
independent and dependable physical count of all Municipal real properties and
semi-expendable items and. Direct them to prepare an inventory report that has
complete details as to location and where these PPE actually belong. Make sure
to have property Cards and stickers for every PPE counted for easy referencing
during inventory count. Reconcile results of inventory with the accounting
records to arrive at the proper balance of the PPE accounts. Any unserviceable
properties found should be counted separately. Unlocated items must be found,
otherwise, persons accountable thereof shall be required to pay the acquisition
costs,

Considering findings on PPE account balances as a perennial issue of the
Municipality, we recommended that the foregoing procedures be donme by
account until existence and ownership of all PPE accounts in the financial
statements are established by year-end.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated
General Services Office to regularly conduct periodic inventory, monitor the
status of their count and the submission of the annual inventory report not later
than January 31 of the following year for approval, a copy furnished to the COA
Auditor,

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the public
infrastructures along with other unrecorded PPE items that may be found
during reconciliation and effect the necessary reclassification and adjustments as
noted in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare monthly the reconciliation of surplus/deficit with the net operating cash
flow for easy reconciliation at year-end. Strictly adhere with the rules on disclosing
information in the notes to financial statements in compliance with PPSAS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor delegate supplies and property
management to a single person with assigned staffs for the conduct of inspection,
control and monitoring of unserviceable items for disposal. We also recommend
that the assigned General Services Officer/Property Officer be instructed to:

prepare Purchase Request (PR) for supplies and materials needed for the
quarter based on the approved annual procurement program, at the
beginning of the year and of each subsequent quarters;

instruct the Inspector that, after inspection, forward the signed AIR to the
Municipal Accounting Office for recording in appropriate ledger cards and
preparation of disbursement voucher (DV);

submit Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the Property Officer for
inspection;

maintain stock cards to monitor purchase and issuances to respective offices;
issue supplies in stock based on the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS)
prepared by the requisitioning office and prepare PR for those not in stock;
consolidate RIS periodically and prepare the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) and forward to the Municipal Accountant for
proper recording of expense;

accept delivered items before inspection of the Municipal Inspector

prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI)
to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given
date.

pre-number all PARs to be issued for proper referencing and control and
keep a file copy and monitor all issuances of properties to each employee per
office;

cancel previously issued PAR when issuing newly acquired properties to
employees for replacement;

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

record purchases of office supplies, accountable forms, drugs and medicines,
medical and dental laboratory supplies, food supplies, gasoline, oil and
lubricant expense and other supplies expense as debit to appropriate asset
account using the perpetual inventory system and record expenses based on
the SSMI forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer;

maintain the subsidiary ledger cards for each supplies and materials and
update for every delivery when DVs are prepared and issuances based on
SSMI

We further recommended that the Head of offices use the Stock Availability
Inquiry (SAI) for inquiry of supplies availability and the Requisitioning and Issue
Slip (RIS) if stocks /Purchase Request in requesting for supplies.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant set up at
the beginning of the year the total Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax
collectible from the taxpayer and draw adjusting entries to reduce the RPT
receivables by the collections already recognized as income. To facilitate
reconciliation, we also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the
Municipal Accountant with a complete and final list of delinquent taxpayers for
necessary adjustments so that the financial statements would present reliable RPT
receivable account balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to prepare the list of unreleased checks at the end of the year and submit
the same to the Municipal Accountant for reversion back to cash in bank and
payable accounts, the amount of unreleased checks per the submitted list by the
Municipal Treasurer in order to fairly present the cash in bank and other affected
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

e the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all
receivable and payable accounts so that balances of receivables and
payables as of any given time will be easily determined and thus prevent
the incidence of double payment of payables, and

e the Municipal Treasurer to notify immediately all payees of checks for
issuance so as to prevent double billing of payables and/or penalties for
late payments.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and its TWG to:

e scrutinize the contractor’s manpower and equipment on all projects to be
awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where the latter is
the lowest bidder in several projects which have the same or overlapping
implementation period. This is in order to require contractor to augment
its manpower and/or equipment to be utilized in the project awarded to it
or to disqualify it from obtaining several award or contract in consonance
with the provisions of Section 23.7.

e validate the contractor’s list of on-going projects/awarded not yet started;
coordinate/confirm with other agencies/entities and ensure that all
possible on-going projects/awarded not yet started are included therein.

e verify, validate and ascertain all statements made and documents
submitted by the bidder in order to determine whether the bidder
concerned complies with and is responsive to all the requirements and
conditions specified in the bidding documents.

We also recommend that the Municipal Engineer consider re-evaluation of
estimate of project duration based on experience from actual completion of previous
projects and reduce the stipulated contract time, if proper. This would allow the
Municipality to implement more projects during the year given the shorier time
provided to the contractors to complete the projects.

We recommended that the BAC submit the lacking documents noted in audit
for projects awarded to RM Joyce Construction and Supplies and 6 Jewels
Construction, and the Instruction to Bidders, Bid Evaluation Report and Post-
Qualification Report for the contract described as construction/improvement of
Municipal Building amounting to £1,999,000.00 for further evaluation.
Otherwise, such transactions/contracts shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require collection of
corresponding deficient business taxes/permits.

We recommended that the BAC-TWG verify the sales declared by the
bidders in their Mayor’s Permit with the actual sales per financial statements
submitted and/or the total contracts awarded to them by the Municipality per
accounting records and disqualify them when material variances are noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require and review all
documents that should be supporting the BAC Resolutions recommending award to a
contractor before approving such recommendation and issuing a notice of award
to the contractor.

Lastly, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee:

- follow the prescribed sequence and timeline of procedures in the bidding
process;

- prepare Bid Evaluation Report and Post-Qualification Reports;

- require at least two observers during every bidding; and

- meticulously conduct post-qualification and extensive verification of the
representations of the winning bidder as calculated in all its technical and
financial documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor see to it that funds allocated for
priority projects be optimally utilized for the intended purpose in order to fully
attain the purpose and intent for establishing the fund at the soonest possible time,
thereby, maximizing the real value of the money on hand.

We also recommended that in the selection of projects for implementation,
the readiness of the Municipal n terms of availability of funds and all other
necessities for their implementation should also be considered in addition to the
benefits that could be derived therefrom by the constituents.

We recommended that the preparation and issuance of the Purchase Orders be
centralized under the General Services Officer/Manicipal Treasurer. We also
recommend that Purchase Orders issued have the complete pertinent details
required under the COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant carefully examine and
verify liquidation documents submitted.

We also recommended that the concerned employees submit the Program of
Activities and approved Travel Orders for seminars attended within 5 days from
receipt hereof. Otherwise, transactions will be suspended in audit.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from
granting cash advances for travel without the authority to travel and the detailed
basis of the amount of advance such as Itinerary of Travel and Program of Activity
or activity design. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure
that the liquidation report sheet attached is properly accomplished with all the
necessary data as a proof that he/she had checked the liquidation documents
submitted by the concerned officials and employees.

We recommended that the Local School Board:

® require the refund of P407,564.00 and stop the grant of allowance to
nationally hired teachers and non-teaching personnel;

* require the members of the Local School Board to refrain from budgeting
and charging non-priority expenditures against the Special Education
Fund as provided for under Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and Joint Circular
No. 1 5. 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG and instead prioritize projects
and activities for the students and school facilities to be enjoyed primarily
by the students; and

e ensure that all expenditures are charged to sufficient allotments, either
through realigned or augmented budget. Pass resolution/ordinance
authorizing the realignment of the savings from one expense item to
another within the same expense class to avoid disallowance in audit.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure sufficiency
of appropriation before signing the Obligation Request and properly monitor the
appropriation as against the actual expenditures and ensure proper realignment
and augmentation of funds, if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal officials who approved the
disbursement vouchers for all these expenses refund the excess amount of
P194,725.00 for those who did not attend. We also recommend the refund by the
102 participants of the P880.00 per diem each of them claimed or a total of
£89,760.00.

We recommended that the Municipality refrain from conducting/attending
seminars outside of their geographical location to prevent excessive/unnecessary
expenditures and utilize the funds to finance desirable development programs or
projects for the benefit of the constituents thereby, providing quality life for all.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials
refund the amount of $906,474.53 (excluding P44,160.00 of the P82,396.38 under
check number 60068283). We also recommended that the Municipal officials and
employees refrain from claiming for expenses that do not serve any public purpose
and which are also irregular, unnecessary, and extravagant.

We further recommended the Municipal Accountant, acting also as the Internal
Auditor, to be vigilant and compliant with the existing laws, rules and
regulations in the disbursement of funds and be very strict in processing claims, not
allowing those that are for unlawful and personal expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all claims
against governmeni funds be supported with complete documents to avoid such
deficiencies.

We recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan consider enactment, approval and
implementation of ordinances relative to proper solid waste management and
disposal with appropriate incentive programs to encourage participation of
concerned sectors.

We also recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board, headed by

the Municipal Mayor:

a) prioritize the establishment of the Municipality’s own sanitary landfill;

b) bring into operation the Eco-Waste Processing Center and dispose the
salt fertilizers with proper reporting to its donor;

c) strictly enforce Kautusan Bilang 7-2001 and impose the penalties
provided therein for incompliance;

d) require the Barangay officials to prioritize establishment of Materials
Recovery Facility (MRF) in their barangays;

¢) require the MENRO of close and documented monitoring of the
implementation and cooperation of barangays in launched programs
relative to effective solid waste management as well as feedback
mechanisms for issues hindering proper implementation in order to
achieve bearing results in terms of reducing wastes as well as its dumping
costs.

f) enter into a formal agreement with Bauan Solid Waste Management, Inc.
for the dumping of non-toxic/non-hazardous residual wastes in their
sanitary landfill including terms but not limited to duration of the
contract, coverage, assignment of rights, and termination.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Treasurer to immediately cause the closing of bank accounts in Bangko
Kabayan, Mabini Branch and avail the services of the Land Bank of the Philippines
in order to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Gender and Development
Council, headed by the Municipal Mayor:

a. conduct proper survey and study as guided by PCW-DILG- DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 to determine gender
issues surfacing in the locality and perform gender mainstreaming of the
activities of the Municipality and its programs for the barangays;

b. prepare a GAD Plan based on established gender issues, specify the cause
and objective and the funds allocated to finance each and every activity
and submit the same to DILG and to the Provincial Planning and
Development Office for review and to ensure the alignment of the
municipal GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province.See to it that
planned activities are geared towardsthe attainment of gender equality
and women’s empowerment and ensure full utilization of the fund to fully
address gender issues;

c. ensure proper implementation of properly identified GAD related
projects/activities incorporated in the DILG-approved GAD Plan and
Budget and conduct close monitoring of programs to make certain that
specific objectives are being attained.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe provisions of Article
210 to 213 and 215 of RA 7160 and Section 3.4 of National Budget Circular No. 563
dated April 22, 2016. Contributions and elective officials’ expenses related to
leagues which are not included in the preceding articles are not government
expenses and should not be charged against the funds of the municipality.

We recommended that the:

* Municipal Mayor establish the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Office composing of the designated Municipal
DRRM Officer and at least three staffs for the effective and efficient
disaster risk reduction planning and management.

* LDRRM Council to convene and perform improved assessment of the
conditions of barangays and other parts of the territorial area of the
Municipality to identify high disaster risk areas and accordingly plan
and implement necessary mitigating projects or activities. Strengthen
the rescue and recovery facilities, manpower and equipment to ensure
readiness in case of urgency; and the

* Designated MDRRMO carefully plan disaster preparedness and
mitigation programs and activities with emphasis on relativity of
projects to disaster prevention and ensure implementation thereof.

Create an Inventory Committee with technical knowledge to conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team for verification purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management failed to create an inventory committee since there was a change in management. Likewise, no complete accounting and property records were maintained hence they were not able to do the physical count

observations.recommendation: The management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording the acquisitions and issuances of supplies Regular reconciliation should be done between two records in order to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Perpetual Inventory method has already implemented yet the reported balance of inventory account at year-end is still not fairly presented because no inventory  records were maintained in the previous years.

observations.recommendation: The management optimally utilize the funds appropriated to various priority projects under the 20% Development Fund of the City to help achieve its socio-economic development pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: Total budgeted amount per project was not optimally utilized because the nature of implementation of the projects under 20% DF are by administration

observations.recommendation: The MDRRMC consider in the formulation of disaster risk reduction management programs/ projects/activities the previous year’s balance of LDRRMF to attain maximum utilization of the fund and the realization of the agency's ultimate goals and objectives of protecting the lives and properties of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: There was a change in management and key personnel that will man the said reports.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant make a detailed review of the dormant accounts and the unsupported balances reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, where appropriate.
observations.managementAction: No adjustments were made since no documents to support the said transactions.

observations.recommendation: The Honorable Municipal Mayor require the Accountant to submit the official receipts, paid disbursement vouchers and payrolls and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within ten days from receipt of said documents as required under Section 7.2
observations.managementAction: Partial implementation is due to the increase in the volume of transactions which hinder the submission of the required reports within the required time.

observations.recommendation: The management adhere with items 8.3, 10.2 and 10.3 of DBM/DOH Joint Circular No. 01 series of 2012 dated November 29, 2012 in the granting of Subsistence and Laundry Allowance to Public Wokers. Likewise, the management should submit justification why the amount paid in excess of the allowable amount should not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Subsistence and Laundry allowance is given based on actual days rendered.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant reclassify the erroneous entry made to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the correcting entries.
observations.managementAction: Made necessary adjustment to adjust the accounts.

observations.recommendation: The Honorable Municipal Mayor instructs the OIC-Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: Erroneous charging of expenses to the said fund was not strictly monitored.

observations.recommendation: The management strictly adhere the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
