The Local Sanggunian should formulate their GAD Code in coordination with the LGU GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to support LGUs efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women's empowerment and gender equality; and management must use the amount included in its GAD budget for the establishment and maintenance of a GAD Database to serve the LGU in its gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: Establishment of GAD Code and Database is still in process. Likewise, the previous officer who made the GAD Plan and Budget for this year did not include the programs.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Human Resource and Management Officer observe the five days minimum balance of vacation leave credits in granting requests for monetization. The concerned employee should be required to submit clinical abstract/medical procedures undertaken to support his claim. The documentary requirements in the monetization of more than the allowable minimum number of days as set forth in Section 22 Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules on leave by the CSC should also be complied with before granting the same.
observations.managementAction: Management complied on the guidelines of payment of leave credits.

observations.recommendation: The Chairman of the Bids And Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that contracts and all its supporting documents, including those required in the auditorial and technical review, be submitted to the Office of Auditor within five days from its execution pursuant
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted all the documentary requirements of perfected contract to the Audit Team for review.

observations.recommendation: 'The Honorable Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Treasurer's Office to exert more effort in the Collection of taxes due and collectible by sending Notice of Delinquency to RPT Taxpayers and to form a committee to promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes. We also recommend the imposition of the provisions of sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code in the collections of delinquent taxes.
observations.managementAction: Collection on delinquent taxes.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommend that Management submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment based on the results of the actual physical count as required under Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I not later than January the 31st of each year.
b. Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain/update PPE and Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards to facilitate the reconciliation of their respective records.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Property Custodian is in the process of preparing and encoding the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Submission of the report will be made to COA as soon as the report will be completed.
observations.reason: CY 2015 Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) preparation was only submitted on August 11, 2016. However, accounts’ balance per RPCPPE does not reconciled with the balances per books. Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards are not maintained by the Accounting Section.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs).
b. We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 will request approval from the Department of Finance to maintain the accounts. Management also submitted a Certification that a CAMELS rating of "3" as of March 31, 2015 was given by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas to FCB.
observations.reason: Maintained bank accounts not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015 were submitted by management as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local government unit discontinue the practice of allocating discretionary funds in its Annual Budget in order to avoid suspension/disallowance in the 2016 Annual Budget. Any diverse unconstitutional methods and mechanisms should not be adopted in any system of governance, by any name or form, in compliance with the Supreme Court decision promulgated on November 19, 2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The municipality discontinued the practice of allocating discretionary funds in the form of subsidies and assistance in the 2016 Annual Budget.
observations.reason: management discontinued the practice of allocating discretionary funds in the form of subsidies and assistance in the 2016 Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommend that management submit justification and legal basis in engaging with employees’ cooperative in the procurement of goods.
b. We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee revisits its procurement process with the end view of discontinuing the practice of acquiring goods from middlemen or those who are not eligible to transact business with the LGU in pursuant to the Section 89 of RA 7160 and the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management discontinued the practice of acquiring goods from MAG-EMPUC which are not in line with procurement rules.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the BAC Chairman and Secretariat to strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the submission of copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) days after their execution/issuance.
observations.managementAction: AAR Submission on copy of contracts and its supporting documents has been made to COA 2014; contracts and documents of Programs of Work and Plans and Specifications were requested. Management cannot furnish all purchase orders/contracts within five (5) working days for economy reasons considering the distance of the municipality to COA Office which is more or less 100 kilometers.
observations.reason: Several submitted contracts were not supported with a Detailed Estimates of the Programs of Work and blueprint copies of the Plans and Specifications.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommend that management submit explanation and/or justification why the procurement of equipment rentals were split as discussed.
b. We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee adheres to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations to ensure that all the procurement activities of the municipality are in accord therewith.
c. It is also recommended that the management instruct the municipal treasurer to see to it that all required data and information needed in the supporting documents shall be completely filled up before processing the transaction for payment.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management admitted the practice but explained reasons.
observations.reason: A Notice of Disallowance was issued on said transactions and as of 12/31/16, no refund has been made or Motion of Appeal has been filed to COA Regional Office on this regard.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommend that the concerned official submit justification on using the unliquidated cash advance in her subsequent travels.
b. We further recommend that management enjoins all officials and employees to adhere to Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 An Appeal was already submitted to COA Regional Office on the Notice of Disallowance No. 2016-001(15)BF on said travel.
observations.reason: The Resolution on the Appeal has complied with the Regional Office recommendation. Submission of the necessary documents on the audit of cash advances was not yet received.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned employees to strictly adhere with the required time frame on the submission of accounts and other financial reports to the Commission on Audit pursuant to PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-005, in order for the Auditor to review and audit the disbursement vouchers, transactions recorded in the books and collections to assure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Municipal Reports and disbursement vouchers, collections and other report to the Accounting Office were requested.
observations.reason: Some disbursement vouchers, collections and other reports from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office were delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality fully comply with the provisions of the Joint Circular 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015 to ensure proper entitlement, release, use, reporting, and audit of Confidential and Intelligence Funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Treasurer/Accountant submitted reports. Submission of the necessary documents on the audit of Confidential Funds for the 1st to 3rd Quarter of 2015 were submitted to COA Central Office.
observations.reason: Credit Notices on the cash advances for the Intelligence and Confidential Funds issued; however, the required Credit Notices issued from the COA Central Office are still awaited.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommend that Management require the GAD Focal Point to prepare a GAD Plan and Budget with specific GAD-related programs, projects and activities that would institutionalize the pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment. In succeeding years, GAD Plan and Budget duly approved/endorsed by the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) be submitted to the Auditor.
b. We further recommend that Management ensure that the annual GAD Budget is fully utilized and the identified programs and activities be implemented during the year so that intended beneficiaries can enjoy the benefits it may derive. And also, require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the annual GAD Accomplishment Report, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor for verification and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management will revisit GAD Plans by the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW). The Agency will revisit GAD guidelines.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Budget duly approved/endorsed by the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) was not furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, as the property custodian of the municipality, conduct an inventory taking on all unserviceable properties including those are no longer needed and prepare Inventory and Inspection Report pertaining thereto, to serve as the basis for the Municipal Accountant to reclassify these unserviceable properties from the PPE account to the Other Assets account in order to arrive at an accurate and reliable PPE account balance.
b. We further recommend that these assets which are unserviceable and no longer needed in municipality’s operations be disposed accordingly to prevent possible further deterioration, obsolescence and decline in value. Moreover, pending the actual disposals thereof, these unserviceable properties and equipment should be transferred to the Other Assets so that PPE account balance would be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Report of Unserviceable Properties was submitted on April 15, 2015.
observations.reason: Disposal of unserviceable assets were not yet made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local government unit discontinue the practice of allocating discretionary funds of the Local Chief Executive, the Vice-Mayor and Members of the Sangguniang Bayan in its Annual Budget. Any diverse unconstitutional methods and mechanisms should not be adopted in any system of governance, by any name or form, in compliance with the Supreme Court decision promulgated on November 19, 2014.
observations.managementAction: AAR The municipality discontinued the practice of allocating discretionary funds in the 2016 Annual Budget.
observations.reason: management discontinued the practice of allocating discretionary funds in the 2016 Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommend that management adhere to the provisions prescribed in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 pertaining to special warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also in the purchases of goods, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those of the third parties involved in the execution of the government projects.
b. We further recommend that future procurements be deducted with the 10% retention money or require a special bank guarantee which is equivalent to 10% of the total contract price, otherwise, expenses due to the defects of the project or goods within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials approving the payment.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 A 10% retention money were deducted from the billings/claim for infrastructure projects and purchases of goods and equipment.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommend that management assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit as soon as possible an Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2013.
b. It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR 2014 and its ensuing annual reports.
observations.managementAction: AAR Prepared and submitted the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
a. Require all department heads to submit Annual Procurement Plan (APP) to the BAC Secretariat who will prepare the LGU’s APP based on the consolidated listing of supplies allocation for the year. All procurement during the year shall be in accordance with the approved APP.
b. Ensure that all procurement of supplies, materials and equipment are done through competitive public bidding, pursuant to Section 10, Article IV of RA 9184 in order to give the widest publicity possible to all prospective and qualified suppliers with price offers or quotations most advantageous to the government.
c. Strictly comply with the conditions enumerated under Section 52.1, Rule XVI of the same IRR in the event that the original mode of procurement through competitive public bidding as recommended in the APP cannot be pursued and shopping is resorted to as an alternative mode of procurement by the Bids and Awards Committee.
d. Further, strictly adhere with the provisions contained under Section 7.2, Rule II of the same IRR requiring the formulation of the Annual Procurement Plan which shall consolidate the respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of the different programs, projects and activities of the different end-user units of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR Annual Procurement Plan (APP) were duly prepared in the subsequent years. LGU strictly complied with the guidelines on procurement of supplies and materials thru shopping only for goods and supplies which are more than P50,000.00 in the subsequent years. All procurements of goods and supplies were done thru public bidding.

observations.recommendation:
a. Use the funds received in accordance with the project proposals on a timely manner to maximize the beneficial use of government projects.
b. Effect the proper liquidation to the concerned agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds downloaded after the completion of the project unless these are not required to be returned to the trustor.
c. Review all balances specifically the excess funds for fully implemented projects. Once ascertained, return the excess to source agencies or request authority to retain the amount for other purposes.
observations.managementAction: AAR Request for realignment on some financial assistance yet return to source agency. Some remaining balances of unused funds after implementation of the desired projects were not returned to source agency.
observations.reason: Remaining balances of unused funds after implementation of the desired projects were not returned to source agency. Some were sent to agency. Partially implemented.

observations.recommendation:
a. Enact measures to maximize the use of the Special Education Fund, considering its limited funding derived only from the real property taxes, and attain the purpose of creating the fund.
b. Require the Local School Board to adhere to the provisions of Art. 363 of the Rules and Regulations Implementing the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 83, Chapter 5 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I, in identifying transactions or projects to be funded by the Special Education Fund.
c. Advise the Local School Board to make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan for the possible appropriation in the General Fund of the transactions deemed necessary in the operation of the school but are not included among the purposes mentioned in Article 363 of the Rules and Regulations Implementing the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 83, Chapter 5 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 5% of the LGU’s Budget were allotted for the GAD programs of the municipality in the ensuing years. Management will revisit GAD guidelines. (Note: This management action text appears in the source table under a different item; no direct management action for SEF was indicated.)
observations.reason: SEF funds were still used in payment of salaries of non-teaching related personnel.

observations.recommendation:
a. Grant subsequent cash advance only after the issuance of Credit Advice from the Chairman or the submission of liquidation vouchers/reports for the previous Cash Advance to the COA Chairman.
b. Require the immediate submission of the liquidation reports of the end. intelligence funds to COA Central Office, copy furnished the Auditor in charge of the LGU and the Municipal Accountant; c. Follow-up or send tracers to the Commission on Audit, Central Office for the issuance of Credit Advice of the liquidations submitted.
observations.managementAction: AAR Liquidation reports were submitted to COA Central Office. Management followed-up for issuance of Credit Notices.
observations.reason: Credit Notices on the cash advances for the Intelligence Fund were not issued, hence the already unliquidated cash advance for Intelligence increased by P458,640.00, from P1,007,000.00 to P1,465,640.00 as by year end.

observations.recommendation:
a. Allocate and spend the required 5% of the Annual Budget for GAD programs.
b. Observe careful GAD planning and budgeting and prepare specific plans and programs for Gender and Development as mandated under Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 5% of the LGU’s Budget were allotted for the GAD programs of the municipality in the ensuing years. Management will revisit GAD guidelines.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Budget were revisited; however, full compliance with requirements pending.

observations.recommendation:
a. Advice all Leagues through its officers to purchase and use the Official Receipt of the Republic of the Philippines.
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in payments in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Vol. I.
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these leagues by requiring the recipient leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.
d. Require the Leagues concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-Laws as basis of receiving bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: AAR Official Receipts of the League were being issued in acknowledgement of payments. It was agreed upon during the conference of LGS COA officials and the Union of Local Authorities of the Philippines (ULAP) that official receipts of the leagues were allowed to be used in acknowledgement of payments made by its members until stock on hand were all issued.
observations.reason: Some required documents were not submitted. Management accepted the receipts because these were duly issued by the concerned leagues. Implementation outstanding on full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipal Treasurer/Accountant release the shares from the Real Property Tax collections of each barangays, without need of any further action, directly to the barangay treasurer on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter and shall not be subject to any lien or holdback for whatever purpose in order to meet the timely utilization of the fund for their operations and projects beneficial to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Accountant/ Treasurer release the shares from the Real Property Tax only upon request from the barangays concerned.

observations.recommendation:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review and check the completeness of supporting documents attached to the vouchers; requires that all claims for payment of goods and infrastructure projects must be supported with complete documentation.
b. Further require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all boxes for signatures of concerned officials/employees/person in the vouchers and supporting documents are completely signed and dated; Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transactions are properly supported and data is accurate and valid.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Accountant thoroughly reviews the supporting documents; some disbursement vouchers were not properly and completely filled-up.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were not completely filled-up.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the officials concerned to properly and completely fill-up the trip tickets attached to the vouchers and adhere strictly to the guidelines promulgated in the use of government vehicles; b. Require the gas station to issue charge slips every reloading of fuel to municipal vehicles and attach the same to the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTTs). Further, require the gas station to send monthly billing statement together with the copy of the RIS and DTTs to support the claim for gasoline; c. Submit to the Commission On Audit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels within ten (10) days of the succeeding month for validation; d. Provide the Commission On Audit a complete list of serviceable municipal vehicles to ascertain the reliability of fuel consumption and for validation purposes; e. Control and evaluate the use of fuel and ensure that the usage are only for official purposes before approving gas/oil requests.
observations.managementAction: AAR Payment for gasoline were supported with charge slips and duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets in the ensuing years.
observations.reason: However, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption were not submitted to COA within ten (10) days of the succeeding month. Complete list of serviceable municipal vehicles were not submitted to COA as required.

observations.recommendation:
a. Exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds and property and be minimized guided with Sections 164 and 165 of the GAAM, Volume I for the prevention of incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures.
b. Minimize/limit the incurrence of catering/meals expenses to what is necessary and appropriate.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management implemented controls and minimized catering services incurred in LGU’s related activities only.
observations.reason: Catering services were incurred in LGU’s related activities only.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management advise the Municipal Accountant to appropriately record the actual assessed value of the land in the books of accounts. We further recommend that the GSO or the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Municipal Assessor for the proper titling in the name of the municipality of the subject lots with concerned agency of the government in conformity with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: AAR Reconciliation was not completed due to the unavailability of old records.
observations.reason: Records were not reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to limit the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to the programs and projects enumerated under DILG and DBM JMC 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Ensure strict monitoring of the disbursements against the 20% Development Fund. In addition, properly plan and allocate more funds for projects which have more impact to the development to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: There were still expenses charged against the 20% Development Fund that were not among those allowed  under the guidelines of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 during CY 2016.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Finding No. 3 in this year's Annual Audit Report (AAR).

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Municipal Government to stop funding the payment of honoraria out of the regular appropriations of the Municipality. The amount necessary should be taken from the savings and proceeds from sale of bid documents, fees from contractor/supplier registry, fees charged for copies of minutes of bid openings, BAC resolutions and other BAC documents, protest fees, other proceeds from bid security forfeiture and from the agency's savings after the mandatory expenditure items have been satisfied with.
observations.managementAction: The agency already acted in accordance with the provisions of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007 in the payment of honoraria to the members of BAC, BAC Secretariat and BAC TWG.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Municipal Government to strictly comply with the regulations provided in R.A 9184 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 on the grant of honoraria to officers/employees involved in government procurement. Refund the excess amount of BAC honorarium claimed and ensure that the subsequent claims for BAC honorarium are adequately supported with the necessary documents.
observations.managementAction: Out of disallowed amount of P454,460.00, refunds were made amounting to P408,120.18 by the  accountable officers leaving the unsettled amount of P46,339.82 as of the end of year.
observations.reason: Due to financial constraints, the two (2) concerned personnel who has still a balance of disallowed amount as at year end requested that the full payment be made upon the release of 2017 Mid  Year Bonus to be deducted in their payrolls. The Municipal Accountant shall be given authority deduct such amount from the payroll through their respective letter requests.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Municipal Officials concerned to secure the proper government official receipt when paying registration fees in attending conferences, seminars or assemblies sponsored by their respective leagues of elective officials.

Stop the practice of accepting privately printed Official Receipts instead of the Government Official Receipt (AF 51) when attending conferences/ seminars sponsored by a Government Organization such as the Philippine. Councilors' League, Liga ng mga Barangays, Mayors' League, etc.
observations.managementAction: The management did not act on this recommendation as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason:
The Office of the Audit Team Leader  issued the Notices of Suspen
sions to the Municipal Officials who failed to secure the govern
ment official receipt.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Municipal Government to prepare and enter into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the recipient beneficiaries of the agricultural, fisheries and seaweed projects under the Bottom-Up Budgeting Program. Make sure that the duties and responsibilities of binding parties are clearly defined and established in order to at least ensure the smooth implementation, effectiveness, and sustainability of the projects. Stop the practice of not entering into a MOA with the intended beneficiaries when implementing priority programs and projects.

Follow up the delivery of the remaining 16 cattle to ensure the success of the cattle production project. Impose appropriate liquidated damages for the delayed deliveries.
observations.managementAction: The management was able to prepare the MOA with the respective recipients as at year end.
observations.reason:
The MOA was not subscribed due to amend
ments to be made to some  of the provisions contained therein. However, they pledged to submit the subscribed MOA before the end of February 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the Municipal Accountant to provide the necessary information to strictly comply with current accounting standards especially on the presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Some accounts were already rectified by management but there are still accounts that did not disclose the necessary information and did not comply with the current accounting standards.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Finding No. 9 in this year's Annual Audit Report (AAR).

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Municipal Government to stop the practice of charging disbursements which are not aligned with the PPAs under the LDRRMF. For CY 2016, see to it that the PPAs in the Annual Budget which are not related to the disaster risk reduction and management (DRRM) activities but are programmed to be charged against the LDRRMF should be re-appropriated to fund PPAs which are related to DRRM activities. Seek the aid of the Sangguniang Bayan to facilitate the implementation of such action.
observations.managementAction: The management did not incur expenditures that are not related to LDRRMF for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to strictly follow the guidelines on the attribution of the budget pursuant to Sections 4(C.3 No. 2) and 4(C.4 No. 5) of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.

Likewise, we also recommend that in case the agency is not trained in the administration of HGDG, they may seek assistance from gender analysis specialists/experts or from oversight agencies such as DILG, PCW, DBM, and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: The concerned GAD focal person was able to apply the guidelines on the attribution of the budget pursuant to Sections 4(C.3 No. 2) and 4(C.4 No. 5) of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 through the administration of HGDG for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to submit the consolidated GPBs of the Municipality to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) to ensure the alignment of the Municipality’s GAD PPAs to the priorities of the province, and then from PPDO to the DILG Regional Office for its review and approval within the schedule set under JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA. Ensure also that a copy of the GAD AR, together with its complete supporting documents, are submitted to the COA Audit Team for proper evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The concerned GAD focal person had already submitted  a copy of 2016 GPB to the PPDO. A copy was sent also and reviewed by the  DILG Provincial Office.
observations.reason:
The manage
ment did not submit the required 2016 GAD AR to the Office of the Audit Team Leader due to the absence of some data. However, the GAD FPS committed to submit the report by March 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Government direct the Municipal Property Inventory Team to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the Municipality at least once a year and prepare inventory report thereof using the prescribed form – Report on the Physical Count of Plant, Property and Equipment (RCPPE). The report must be properly accomplished and copy of such must be furnished to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation purposes.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the Inventory Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2015. For CY 2016, the required Report was not yet submitted to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.
observations.reason:
The delay is attributed on the conversion of accounts to PPSAS that affected the identifica
tion of some assets. However, the manage
ment, assured that the Inventory Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2016 will be submitted by March 2017.

observations.managementAction: The management had already formulated  the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Implementing Plan and was adopted through the approval of Sangguniang Bayan Resolution.
observations.reason:
The Implemen
ting Plan is still for approval by the Sangguni
ang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GFPS of the Municipality should formulate its GPB for CY 2015 in accordance with Section 4C of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The agency had already prepared the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2016 in accordance with Section 4C of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Government strictly follow the guidelines on the attribution of the budget in pursuant to Sections 4(C.3 No. 2) and 4(C.4 No. 5) of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.

Likewise, we also recommend that in case the agency is not trained in the administration of HGDG, they may seek assistance from gender analysis specialists/experts or from oversight agencies such as DILG, PCW, DBM, and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: The concerned GAD focal person was able to apply the guidelines on the attribution of the budget pursuant to Sections 4(C.3 No. 2) and 4(C.4 No. 5) of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 through the administration of HGDG for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management intensify their collections from market block/space/lot rentals, especially on the delinquent vendors, and strictly follow the imposition of penalty or surcharges as clearly stipulated in the terms and condition of the contract. Replace the delinquent vendors if they are not willing to settle their obligations with other qualified parties who are interested to establish business in the area.

We recommend also that the management monitor closely the collections from the Market and Terminal operations so that the corresponding expenditures could be controlled and minimized.                                     Enjoin the Market Supervisor to prepare and submit monthly status report on collections especially in the market operations. Reshuffle collectors on regular basis to avoid hesitancy in the collection of market space rentals.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, the management increased its receipts from market operations to P1,185,702.97 from CY 2014 of P832,961.87, having an increase of P352,741.10 or 30% of its collections as of December 31, 2016
observations.reason: There are still market vendors who did not regularly pay their monthly market rentals as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Municipal Government, the immediate usage of donated lot for the operation and management of Eco-Park with Alternative Technology Approach for its final waste disposal and for the purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes to sustain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan.

Prioritization of the funding for these properties should be immediately done so that the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste would not be hampered.

We recommend also the stoppage of the operation of the present disposal site and the strict implementation of the waste segregation policy of the Municipality from the source, in order to successfully attain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Local Government Unit.
observations.managementAction: The management had already utilized the donated lot for  waste disposal for CY 2016.
observations.reason:
No appropria
tion was made for the purchase of additional equipment for collection of wastes in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Property Inventory Team to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the Municipality at least once a year, prepare inventory report thereof using the prescribed form – Report on the Physical Count of Plant, Property and Equipment (RCPPE), properly accomplished, and furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the report for reconciliation purposes. All unserviceable equipment or properties should be included in the physical count. Then a report of all unserviceable properties using the form – Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) should be prepared and be submitted to the Office of the COA Auditor, together with the request of the Local Chief Executive for inspection and appraisal. This will serve as basis for possible disposal and/or dropping from the books of accounts.

Further,  additional personnel should be assigned  both at the Offices of designated MGSO and of the Municipal Accountant that would focus solely in   maintaining   and updating property cards  and property ledger cards, respectively.  Periodic reconciliation of the involved records in said Offices should be made so that necessary adjustments thereof, if there are any, would then be effected in the books of the LGU in order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the Inventory Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2015. For CY 2016, the required Report was not yet submitted to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.
observations.reason:
The delay is attributed on the conversion of accounts to PPSAS that affected the identifica
tion of some assets. However, the manage
ment, assured that the Inventory Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2016 will be submitted by March 2017.

observations.recommendation:
Avoid the practice of delaying remittances of the various trust liabilities since these are budgeted and included in the annual appropriations of the municipality. 
 
Instruct the personnel-in-charge of the trust liability accounts to conduct monthly reconciliation of same to determine the correct amounts that should be remitted within the prescribed period provided in the applicable rules and regulations so that the concerned government agencies could utilize the funds due them for their programmed activities and incurrence of penalties, interest and surcharges by the municipality relative thereto could be avoided. 

      If there are penalties and surcharges incurred for late remittances, the same should not be charged against the funds of the municipality instead it should be treated as personal liability of the personnel responsible for the delay.
observations.managementAction: These unremitted amounts pertain to previous years especially the account "Due To GSIS" wherein there was a time that outright deductions of GSIS premiums were made by the  Department of Budget and Management on  Internal Revenue Allotments for LGU personnel which they are presently trying to reconcile with its records.
observations.reason:
Due to the present system introduced by the concerned GOCCs, the manage
ment had a hard time in reconciling their remittan
ces. However, the Municipal Accountant made an assurance to remit these balances in CY 2017. 
For unremitted balances in the account "Due to BIR", he further pledged to remit the withheld taxes as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation:
Exhaust all measures to collect receivables from the grantees of credit and financing program even those receivables from NGAS and other receivables.
    Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue regularly statement of accounts and prompt verification of accounts in the books of the creditors.        Make entries to Prior Year’s Adjustments on the amount pertaining to those accounts which recovery is already impossible due to death or disability fully supported with necessary supporting documents.  Reclassify and adjust accounts in consideration of unrecorded repayments to reflect the correct balances duly confirmed with the concerned debtors.  

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of receivables using the billing statements from the Accounting Department in order to recover government funds and utilize collection to finance the present thrusts and priorities of the Municipality.

Exhaust all measures to gather the documents pertaining to the receivables and collect from debtors.
There should a meeting between the LGU concerned and the loan recipients , to sit down and confer together in order to discuss matters of collection and payments to finally settle the accounts and the possibility of continuing this financing scheme in a more effective way for the benefit of the LGU and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management sent various demand letters to respective debtors in CY 2016 and there are those who positively responded by paying their obligations. Likewise, the management is on the process of preparing pertinent documents to seek authority from the Commission on Audit for the writing-off  of those receivables of which collections could not be realized.
observations.reason: Refusal by concerned debtors to pay their obligations.

observations.recommendation: Give utmost priority to the titling of the lots which are utilized by the Municipality in order to ensure ownership over said lands and to avoid possible damage to government properties by unwanted transfer through eviction.
observations.managementAction: This was not acted by the management as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason:
The new administra
tion was not properly informed regarding the titling of lots owned by the agency.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we
recommend that the Municipal
Mayor instruct Bids and Awards
Commitiee to:

a) submit written basis for
awarding subject contracts
despite  deficiencies  on
documentary  requirements
submitted by the bidders;

b) include in the Invitation to
Bid (ITB) posted in PhilGEPs
all necessary information as
cited in this observation to
attract  more  interested
bidders that would result in a
competitive  bidding and
observe the proper process of
posting in PhilGEPs starting
from the ITB up to the
generation of notices  of
award and to proceed;

¢) likewise, post the Notices of
Award and Proceed and the
contract agreement in the
PhilGEPs:;

d) verify the computation of Net
Financial Contracting
Capacity (NFCC) of the
contractor and validate the
reflected amounts of ongoing
projects by requiring the List
of Ongoing Projects
including those awarded but
not yet started,

e) ensure that the manpower and
equipment pledged by the
contractor is clearly
established and documented
by requiring the Statement of
Availability of Key Personnel
and of Equipment and verify
odds of their availability
through the Manpower and
Equipment Utilization
Schedule  especially  for
projects simultaneously
implemented by the same
contractor;

f) scrutinize submitted
bidding/supporting
documents to ensure that
certificates,  permits  and
licenses are valid and
complete bidding documents
are prepared and submitted in
the form prescribed by
Government Procurement
Policy Board (GPPB);

g) ensure  that  sufficient
performance bond of 30%, if
in the form of a surety bond
callable upon demand, be
posted by the contractor
before entering into contract
agreement; and

h) instruct the BAC Secretary to
narrate in the Minutes of
Opening of Bids the detailed
proceedings of the bidding
proper for all contracts
involved in a particular
bidding day.
observations.managementAction:
No written basis was
provided to the Audit
Team. Same deficiencies
were observed in the audit
of CY 2016.

Limited information and
seminars attended for
process despite
recommendations and
claimed that they lack
sufficient
understanding of the
procurement process
updates in RA
9184 during CY
2016. See finding no.
20 in Part I of this
report.

Notice to Proceed, of
Award and  perfected
contract agreement
remained not posted in
PhilGEPs.

Net Financial Contracting
Capacity as submitted by
the bidders did not
consider the amount of
ongoing projects and yet,
the BAC, passed them in
post qualification and
recommended the award
of contracts to them.

Manpower and equipment
pledged by the declared
winning bidders were the
same for simultancous
projects sampled in audit
of CY 2016.

The Municipal BAC does
not comply with the
proper forms and
sufficient details are not
embodied in the bidding
documents especially in
the minutes, invitation to
Bid, etc. BAC reports on
bid evaluation and post-
qualification are also not
prepared.

Sampled contracts showed
sufficient performance
bond of 30%. However,
for projects which the
contract time was
extended, validity of the
performance bond was not
extended putting the
Municipality ~ at  the

disadvantage of having no
security  during  the
extension period.

The BAC changed the
format used for the
Minutes of Opening of
Bids but which still
showed no details as to
what transpired in the
proceedings.

Request for change orders
remained undocumented.

The —BAC  was
neglectful of the
recommendations and

sufficient
understanding of the
process despite
seminars attended for
the updates in RA
9184 during CY
2016.

observations.recommendation:

a. the driver prepare and fully
accomplish the trip tickets
during every travel using the
LGU’s vehicle indicating its
plate  number, time of
departure and arrival, 
approximate distance
travelled, number of liters of
gasoline used, balance of
gasoline after the travel and
the odometer reading;

b. the Management submit
verifiable documentation for
the significant difference in
normal and actual average
gasoline consumption of SAA
2933 and SJV 402;

¢. the Management stop
allowing gasoline expenses of
private cars allegedly used for
official purpose and instead
avail of the travelling
expenses and fare equivalent
to the customary mode of
transportation; and

d. the Management stop the
practice of granting cash
advances for payment of
gasoline  expenses  instead
settle them by drawing checks
payable directly to the
suppliers to ensure that the
proper procurement process
was adopted and
corresponding  taxes  are
withheld.  Negotiate ~ with
nearby gasoline franchise
holders who could extend
credit  limits  to the
Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended that
the:

a) Management include
incentive based programs like
“Clean and Green award”,
“Most Environmentally
Outstanding Enterprise” and
the like on its 10-year Solid
Waste Management Plan as
prescribed under RA 9003;

b) Municipal  Agriculturist
ensure that all Municipal and
Barangay garbage collectors
wear  proper  protective
equipment when handling
solid wastes;

c) Management  device a
scheduling  system  for
collection of different solid
waste  classification  or
restructure the vehicle used in
garbage collection so that
different solid wastes will
have proper compartments;

d) Management enact
resolutions and ordinances
aligned with the
Municipality’s solid waste
management plan  which
includes but not limited to
prohibition on use of plastic,
open burning of waste, and
littering to ensure realization
of its long term and short
term goals;

e) Management ensure
implementation of Kapasiyahan
Blg 175-2001 by informing the
community of its provisions and
imposing appropriate fines to
violators; and

f) Municipal Agriculturist thru
the  Municipality’s ~ Solid
Waste Management Board
monitor the 25% waste
reduction plan through well-
defined indicators and ensure
its effective and efficient
realization  through  well-
established strategies.
